# Project Status — Agent Controller (Mission Control)

> Last updated: 2026-07-12g (**Task 7416 was a DOUBLE failure — inbound twin fixed** (`1ddfc34`): the router packs the current user message as the last `conversationHistory` turn, but the prompt-blob runners (nanoclaw, heavy in-process, heavy containerized, swarm) built prompts from title+description only — chat-routed tasks reached the agent as a 60-char truncated title over a 24KB persona blob ("unactionable prompt"). Shared `renderConversationContext()` now appends the thread (last user turn flagged as the live instruction) at all four sites; absent history renders "" so ritual/API/subtask prompts are byte-identical. Also this session: "excelente" eval-word contract wired (`4fdf224` — anywhere-match praise, swallow-safe intercept, operator's single eval word). Combined with 12f, the 14:12 request now works end-to-end: instruction reaches the agent (1ddfc34) and the agent's answer reaches the operator (de6ae8a). Deployed newPid 932348.)

> Last updated: 2026-07-12f (**Operator-visible bug fixed: nanoclaw chats delivered the reflector's third-person English verdict instead of the agent's answer** (`de6ae8a`; tasks 7413/7416 "answering oddly"). Root cause: the 07-11 heavy fix (b0fa5c4 family) taught the router to prefer `finalAnswer` over `content` and made heavy-worker emit it — but the nanoclaw sibling was never swept: nanoclaw-worker emitted only `content` = reflection.summary, and nanoclaw-runner's parse shape would have dropped `finalAnswer` anyway, so the preference always fell through to the verdict. Zero operator-facing nanoclaw chats between 07-06 and 07-12 hid it. Fix mirrors heavy exactly (worker emits `finalAnswer` via collectFinalAnswer, runner passes it through) + hardening: the TARGET_NOT_IN_SANDBOX structural guard now also checks the agent's answer text (the sentinel's actual home), not just the reflector summary. Regression spec pins the passthrough. Deployed newPid 876490 + image rebuilt (worker runs inside); E2E smoke: nanoclaw task 7418 returned `finalAnswer` with the correct agent answer. Diagnosis also surfaced two secondary findings: (a) Jarvis tone shift (warmer/longer/emoji, e.g. 7411) = the Phase-1 settingSources decontamination removing the operator's leaked no-sycophancy/concision rules — NOT a defect; proposed fix is porting a deliberate style block into Jarvis's own system prompt, awaiting operator decision; (b) watchdog false-positive: restarted a HEALTHY service at 10:00:01 UTC killing an in-flight ritual — same signature 2026-07-05 same minute; queued. Sweep lesson memorized: when a fix teaches a consumer to prefer a new field, sweep every producer of that shape — by output-shape, not filename.)

> Last updated: 2026-07-12e (**V8.5 Phase 2 COMPLETE — model tier refresh, three gated changes shipped.** **2.2** (`5d9a1b4`): the retired `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` literal in `vision.ts`'s prefix-derivation fallback → `claude-sonnet-4-6` (vision-capable, fleet-consistent); dormant path under claude-sdk routing (empty-primary guard throws first, production vision = Groq via env), gate N/A since the corpus never exercises vision. **2.1** (`8f849cf`): `OPUS_MODEL_ID` claude-opus-4-7 → **claude-opus-4-8** — same request surface AND tokenizer as 4-7 (the Sonnet-5 cache-re-baseline class does not apply), affects Prometheus complex paths only; 4.8's flag-uncertainties profile targets the confabulation-guard class; pricing table gains a 4-8 $0 entry alongside 4-7; eval:gate PASS 74.41 (delta −0.39, threshold 72.79). **2.3** (`e3d2237`): classifier `modelTier` → SDK **effort** knob wired end-to-end — the signal had been dead since v6 (tierToProvider collapsed everything to "primary", and the claude-sdk path silently dropped `InferenceRequest.effort` which only the openai path honored). fast-runner now maps flash→low / standard→medium / capable→high through `InferWithToolsOptions.effort` → `queryClaudeSdkAsInferWithTools` → SDK `Options.effort`; deliberate boundaries: unset → key ABSENT → SDK default high (rituals fail toward quality), Haiku retry legs carry no effort (no support on Haiku 4.5), Prometheus untouched; 3 seam specs pin the contract; eval:gate PASS 74.60 (delta −0.19). Both behavior changes deployed (newPid 832302) + container image rebuilt (SDK runs inside nanoclaw/heavy); live smoke: flash-tier task E2E "OK" in 6.1s, 0 in-flight at both restarts. **Known gap**: the gate's selection probe runs effort-unset, so the standard→medium live-quality shift is gate-unverified — watch fast-task quality; revert = the `tierToEffort` mapping; the durable fix is the ≥150-case tool_selection corpus (plan Phase 4.3). Phase 2.4 (Sonnet 5) stays RULED OUT and 2.5 (Fable 5) PARKED per operator rulings §10. Next: Phase 3 (context & cost — 3.1 needs the cache re-measure on the post-0.3.x profile first) or Phase 4/5 items, operator's pick.)

> Last updated: 2026-07-12c (**V8.5 Phase 1b — root cause found, and it inverts the 12b verdict: the "tool_selection regression" was the removal of an OPERATOR-CONFIG LEAK. SDK 0.3.207 ADOPTED + `settingSources: []` isolation shipped + eval incumbent re-baselined.** Bisect (worktree-isolated, live tree untouched): score-gate probes narrowed to 0.3.142–173, then a cheap token-signature probe (cold-call prompt 50.8k "fat" vs 27.4k "lean" via `validate-sdk-tool-visibility.ts`, ~$0.17/step) pinned the flip at **0.3.152→0.3.153** ("parity with Claude Code v2.1.153" — the **lean system prompt** rollout). Composition probes then revealed what the ~23k actually was: **the operator's own Claude Code config stack — `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`, `~/.claude/rules/*.md`, `/root/CLAUDE.md`, repo `CLAUDE.md`, auto-memory `MEMORY.md`, MCP instructions — loaded into EVERY Jarvis inference call** because `settingSources` was UNSET (0.2.x loaded the stack despite its own docs promising isolation-when-omitted; scrubbed-env probe ruled out session-env leakage — production was contaminated too). Confirmation: explicit `settingSources: []` on 0.2.101 → prompt drops to 27k, stack gone, gate scores **53.20** — statistically identical to 0.3.207's 53.59/51.65. **The 57.95 incumbent was the operator's personal operating rules coaching Jarvis's tool selection.** Clean scores are the honest ones; the two SDK versions are score-neutral on the decoupled system. **Shipped**: `settingSources: []` (pinned by spec — Jarvis context comes from its own systemPrompt/KB, never operator dotfiles), SDK 0.2.101→0.3.207 + `@anthropic-ai/sdk@0.111.0` explicit peer, `alwaysLoad: true` on the jarvis MCP server (0.3.x defers tools behind SDK tool search by default; 30/30 visibility verified), refusal handling now formalized upstream, incumbent re-captured via `--update-baseline` on the clean config (justified under the operator's standing "always fix stale data — truth above anything" directive: the old baseline measured a contaminated system; full evidence chain here + memory). Service-SAFE swap this time (stopped first, 0 in-flight, deploy newPid 3900779, smoke PASS ~6s — leaner prompts are faster), container image rebuilt (SDK runs inside nanoclaw/heavy). Side-effects logged for Phase 3: 0.3.x writes 1h-TTL cache (2× write, likely net WIN for cross-task hits); the stable cache prefix shrinks ~23k — cross-task cache-miss economics (queue item, ~$754/mo) must be re-measured on the new profile before picking a fix option. Follow-ups: CRM/agentic-crm + any other SDK consumers on this VPS should get the same `settingSources` audit (grep-sweep pending, next session); upstream docs-vs-behavior discrepancy on 0.2.x worth an issue. Bisect total: ~5 full gates + 8 cheap probes ≈ $10.5.)

> Last updated: 2026-07-12b (**V8.5 Phase 1 — Agent SDK 0.2.101→0.3.207 attempted, gate-FAILED twice, REVERTED. Ships only the forward-compatible residual.** The migration itself was clean — tsc 0 across 106 releases, all 311 inference + 490 sdkTool-consumer + 304 runner tests green, breaking changes absorbed (`env` spread already merging `process.env`; `alwaysLoad: true` pinned turn-1 tool loading; peerDep `@anthropic-ai/sdk@0.111.0` added) — but `eval:gate` FAILED both runs: **tool_selection 53.59 then 51.65 vs the old SDK's triple-reproduced 57.95** (composite 74.79/73.82 vs threshold 74.88; scope 93.34 + classification 100 byte-identical in every run). The regression is REAL and systematic (~2-3 of the ~51 tool cases flip consistently), and per the standing rule the swap did not ship. **Three mechanism hypotheses tested and refuted** with a new probe harness (`scripts/validate-sdk-tool-visibility.ts`, kept): (1) tool deferral — 30/30 registry tools visible cold AND warm; (2) systemPrompt semantics — `string` = custom/replace in both versions' types; (3) description truncation — model reproduced `schedule_task`'s description verbatim to the last line. Confirmed side-findings: 0.3.x warm-call caching is BETTER (100% read vs 65%; warm call $0.0138) and requests are ~45% leaner for identical tool sets (~23k tokens of unidentified 0.2-era prompt content gone — the leading suspect for the selection drop, mechanism unresolved); 0.3.x writes prompt cache at 1h TTL (2× write cost — cost ratio 0.3058/0.1911 = 1.60 confirms; possibly a net WIN for cross-task cache hits, feed into Phase 3 analysis). **INCIDENT (operator-visible, my fault)**: the `npm install` ran under the LIVE service — at 03:23:29 the operator's real chat task 7379 ("Los errores siguen ahí. Diagnostica y corrige", mid Google-Sheets session) died when a CC subprocess exited 1 → fatal EPIPE → service crash → systemd restart, and the service then ran ~20 min on mixed dist(old)/node_modules(new) until the revert restarted it cleanly at 03:45. Operator never retried the failed message. Lesson memorized: never swap runtime-loaded deps under a live service — stop first, or stage in a worktree. **Revert verified**: npm ci from the pre-swap lockfile snapshot (`/root/claude-backups/mc-db-pre-sqlite12-2026-07-12/package-lock.pre-sdk-0.3.json`), SDK 0.2.101 confirmed, clean restart, 0 error lines, smoke PASS. **Ships in this commit** (all green on 0.2.101): refusal-handling at the SDK seam (assistant `stop_reason:"refusal"` → provider-alive [no breaker trip] + deterministic `[refusal]…STATUS: BLOCKED` text when no prose streamed; runs BEFORE the breaker record — ordering pinned by test; dormant on 0.2.101, live the day the SDK moves) + `model_refusal_fallback` observability log + 3 specs + the tool-visibility harness + the buildMcpServer NOTE for the re-attempt. **Next-attempt plan (Phase 1b, new session)**: bisect 0.3.x versions against a tool_selection-only gate subset (~$1/run) to find the regressing release; wire-level request diff old-vs-new (the ~23k delta) if the bisect is inconclusive; re-add `alwaysLoad` + re-run the full gate. eval:gate for THIS commit: N/A beyond the session's BEFORE run (76.97 PASS on this exact tree modulo the dormant refusal branch — no model/prompt/tool-description change).)

No new deps — 15+2 invariant holds. vitest 4 / TS 6-7 / nanoclaw-v2 explicitly deferred per plan. **New: `mc-ctl v83-promote <capability> [--confirm]`** (`src/lib/v8-3/promotion.ts` + `scripts/v83-promote.ts`) — the "small guarded command" deferred at V8.3 Phase 7. Allow-list doctrine: the ONLY expressible transition is L1→L2 (L≥3 has no code path — pinned behind §17 + judgment linkage + per-capability signoff), WHERE clause repeats `level=1` (concurrent-update refusal on `changes!==1`), `gate_config.max_level` fails closed on unparseable JSON, §14 gate must be `pass` (verified live: refuses `insufficient_data` today with exit 2 — inert until shadow decisions flow), dry-run default with `--confirm` signoff per spec §11 (`level=2, promoted_at=now, override_integral=0, window reset`), MADR promotion record rendered to `logs/decisions/` (filename whitelist-sanitized per adr-writer precedent). qa-auditor **PASS, 0 Critical / 0 Warning** (4 Info; 2 folded: global-vs-per-capability gate documented in module header — per-capability signal is the v2 controller —, ADR filename sanitization); auditor verified L2 grants no autonomy (pipeline's sole autonomous branch is `effectiveLevel>=3`, both L1/L2 sync-confirm). Tests: +10 promotion specs, 161 v8-3 green; tsc 0; full suite via pre-commit hook. eval:gate N/A (no model-id/system-prompt/tool-description change). Deployed newPid 3636196, migration gate OK, 257 tools, 0 startup errors, smoke PASS ~15s. 0 in-flight tasks at restart.)

> Last updated: 2026-07-11 (**Fix: two "weird replies" — heavy chat delivered the reflector's meta-summary instead of the answer; NEEDS_CONTEXT clarifying questions swallowed into "No pude completar eso".** `/diagnose` of "Last two responses from Jarvis were somewhat weird." (1) The Azteca concurso-mercantil comment was classifier-escalated to the **heavy** runner (the first heavy chat in 2+ weeks — 1 of ~390 chat tasks, which is why this survived unnoticed); heavy output = `{content: <reflector meta-summary>, score, learnings, finalAnswer: <the agent's real report>}`, and `router.extractResultText` checked `.text/.output/.result/.content` — never `finalAnswer` — so the operator received "Single-goal chat task completed successfully: delivered concise Mexican-Spanish strategic commentary…" while the actual 3-angle commentary sat unread in the task row; the thread buffer + retain recorded the meta-summary too. FIX: `extractResultText` prefers non-empty `finalAnswer` over `content` (aligns with `extractPersistText`, which already ordered `finalAnswer` first; swarm has no `finalAnswer` key and fast wins on `.text` — auditor-verified no other producer affected; ritual/proactive/scheduled delivery now also correctly ships the report, same class). (2) Operator sent "?"; the model answered correctly — "No hay pregunta en tu mensaje. ¿Qué necesitas?" (recovered from the runs row) — but with `STATUS: NEEDS_CONTEXT`, and at 47 chars it fell under fast-runner's >100-char promotion threshold → `success=false` → dispatcher emitted `task.failed` with error="Unknown error" (runner text not in the payload) → generic failure line. FIX: `TaskFailedPayload` gains optional `result`; the dispatcher's task.failed emission carries `result.output`; `handleTaskFailed` delivers the runner's own text when the task status is `needs_context`/`blocked` AND the result carries a real text field (qa-audit W1: the `JSON.stringify` fallback is explicitly rejected so a text-less output object can't send raw JSON), pushes the exchange to the thread capped at `THREAD_RESPONSE_CAP` (qa-audit I3; without the push the operator's answer to the question would arrive with no trace of the question — the 'sirve' missing-exchange class), and retains what was actually sent instead of "[Task failed] Unknown error". DB status stays `needs_context` (analytics intact), `trackTaskOutcome` unchanged, background-agent failure formatting keeps precedence, crash-path/absent `result` degrades to the generic line. Ruled OUT: yesterday's `b0fa5c4`/`ab5fdd2` (neither touched these paths), provider (both SDK calls completed), stale build. qa-auditor PASS-with-warnings; W1+I3 folded pre-commit, sibling sweep covered by the audit (all other `task.failed` emitters omit `result` and are not needs_context producers; no other `finalAnswer` producer). Tests: +5 router specs (heavy finalAnswer, null-finalAnswer fallback, needs_context question delivery + retain content, no-text generic, W1 raw-JSON guard); 68/68 router + 26 dispatcher green; tsc 0. eval:gate N/A (event plumbing + delivery preference — no model-id/system-prompt/tool-description change). Deployed newPid 2957207. Noted, not a defect: that one-line comment cost ~~278k tokens (~~$1.35) on heavy — classifier working as designed, flagged to operator as a cost observation.)

> Last updated: 2026-07-11 (**Fix: "sirve" answered the morning brief correctly — and the operator got total silence.** `/diagnose` of "Jarvis got stuck after answering the 06:00 brief with 'sirve'" (the §17 verdict contract's FIRST live use, 08:02 MX). Jarvis wasn't stuck: the verdict path worked end-to-end (anchored regex matched, §13 forced-tool classifier ran — its first live run, enabled by yesterday's probe fix — brief `promoted` at 08:02:48, §17 got its first real data point), but THREE layers each said nothing: (a) the parallel chat task's model answered the bare verdict with **7 completion tokens** — a naked `STATUS: DONE` that `parseRunnerStatus` strips to `""` (cost-ledger proof: 89,244 prompt → 7 completion tokens; the 08:14 resend hit a **byte-identical 100%-cached prompt** → deterministically silent again); (b) `handleTaskCompleted`'s chat branch was `if (resultText)` with NO else — a completed-but-empty reply was dropped with timers already cleared, not even a log line; (c) `promote.ts` deliberately returned no `reply` for binary outcomes, assuming (a) would ack. Ruled OUT: the 07-10 eval-probe deploy (`ab5fdd2` — chat call was a normal 1-turn fast-runner success, not the infer/probe path) and any task hang (both tasks completed in ~13s). **Fix (3 parts)**: (1) `applyBinaryResolution` returns a deterministic ack (`✓ Brief conservado.` / `🗑️ Brief descartado.`; `expired` stays reply-less — it fires on ANY owner message past TTL, an interjection there is noise); (2) new `router.interceptBriefingVerdict` — a bare verdict with a resolvable pending brief is resolved SYNCHRONOUSLY, acked, pushed to the conversation thread, and the chat pipeline is SKIPPED (the old path burned ~$0.65/verdict across 3 LLM calls to produce nothing; non-verdict messages keep the fire-and-forget resolver for §13 pushback + TTL sweep; resolver race → falls through to normal dispatch, never fabricates an ack); (3) `handleTaskCompleted` empty-text else-branch — warn log + fallback `✓` + pushToThread (breaks the byte-identical-prompt loop) + trackTaskOutcome, so NO completed chat task can ever end in silence again. Minor residual noted during diagnosis: one unattributed ~43k-token 1-turn LLM call per owner inbound (benign, pre-existing) — worth identifying someday; `email:comunidades` IMAP LOGIN failing every 60s (separate issue, surfaced to operator). **qa-auditor PASS-with-warnings; all three folded pre-commit**: W1 — imperative-shaped verdicts (`archívalo`/`descártalo`/`skip` — plausible standalone instructions about PRIOR context, e.g. "archive that email") must NOT be swallowed: new `isExclusivelyBriefVerdict()` gates the synchronous consume to pure rulings (`sirve`/`útil`/`no sirve`/`no me interesa`…), imperatives keep old fire-and-forget resolution (+ack) while the instruction still executes; W2 — intercept moved AFTER `interceptPendingConfirmation` so a destructive-confirmation decline phrased "no sirve" reaches the confirmation flow, not a brief discard; W3 — unconsumed `mockReturnValueOnce` survives `clearAllMocks` (short-circuited path never called the mock) → explicit `mockReset` around every intercept spec. Known residual (auditor-verified benign, documented): empty-text fallback sends a bare static `✓` even for background-agent/community chat tasks — fine while static, don't inherit for a non-static fallback. Tests: +2 promote specs (ack contract, swallow-vs-imperative), +7 router specs (intercept × 6 incl. expired/race/imperative, empty-text fallback); tsc 0. eval:gate N/A (no model-id/system-prompt/tool-description change — router plumbing + deterministic strings).)

> Last updated: 2026-07-10 (**Nightly-eval "drift" root-caused: tool_selection had measured NOTHING since the 2026-05-10 SDK cutover — fixed with an SDK selection probe; hardening-sweep residuals closed.** Asked to investigate the 66.9→58.2 nightly composite slide flagged as a "possible slow prod regression." It was neither slow nor a regression: `infer()` on the claude-sdk path **silently dropped `request.tools`** (`queryClaudeSdkAsInfer` hardcoded `toolNames: []` and returned no `tool_calls` key), and the tuning eval runner is the ONLY infer()-with-tools consumer — so the eval's 50%-weight tool_selection subscore had scored a model that **never saw the tools** for two months, pinned at an arithmetic **silence floor** (only `not_tools`-only cases + the greeting case can pass on zero calls). The visible "drift" was the floor itself decaying: `case-miner.ts`'s 90-day retention (`DELETE … < now-90d`) was deleting the dense April cohort of auto-passing `not_tools` mined cases faster than mining replaced them (313 mined on 07-05 → 301 on 07-10). **Proof, three independent ways**: floor arithmetic predicts 58.2 — the last two nightlies measured 58.1/58.2 exactly; journal `cache_diag` shows `toolsN=0` on every eval call; the 07-06 gate breakdown (ts 25.18/scope 92.54/class 100) reproduces its 60.35 composite to the decimal. The REAL break was the **05-12 cliff (77.4→63.0)** — coincident with the cutover, absorbed as "new normal," never decomposed. Collateral: the nightly loop burned ~$18/wk mutating tool descriptions against a model that couldn't see tools (every experiment "delta 0.0"), and the model-swap eval gate would have PASSED the exact Sonnet-5 tool-adherence regression it was built to catch. Everything else was eliminated deterministically first (free): case set stable since April, per-case scoped tool sets byte-identical 06-27↔HEAD (one case 29→32), definitions identical (only shell_exec +184 chars), variant frozen since 04-07, no nightly ran during the Sonnet-5 window. **Fix at the seam** (`claude-sdk.ts`+`adapter.ts`): `queryClaudeSdkAsInfer` gains `tools?:` → **selection-probe mode** — definitions registered as no-op inline stubs (`buildProbeTools`), `maxTurns=1`, first-turn `tool_use` mapped to openai-shape `tool_calls`; `inferViaClaudeSdk` forwards `request.tools` on both legs; `buildMcpServer`'s collision guard rescoped to names actually registered in the server (the registry-wide check was overbroad and would reject probe stubs). **Second bug caught by the verification run itself**: probes whose model went straight to tool_use (correct!) ended `error_max_turns` with zero prose → classified provider-FAILURE → 51 probes **tripped the claude-sdk circuit breaker mid-eval** and cascaded onto the Haiku leg. Fixed: single-turn tool_use counts as provider-alive — **scoped to `maxTurns===1` per qa-audit W1** (a multi-turn zero-prose tool loop is a wedge and keeps its BLOCKED/failure classification; counter-test pins it). **Verified: two consecutive real gate runs scored composite 76.88 / tool_selection 57.95 with IDENTICAL subscores** (was 60.35/25.18), the second with zero breaker events; incumbent recaptured via `--update-baseline` (the 07-06 incumbent 60.35 superseded — it was measured against the blind harness). qa-auditor PASS-with-warnings, W1 folded pre-commit. tsc 0; 307 inference + 188 tuning/messaging-scoped tests green. **Sweep residuals also closed this session**: `.env`→600; stale 386 MB `mc.db.bak-orphan-cleanup` deleted; SOCIAL_TOOLS dead stub names pruned (+2 tests de-vacuoused); watchdog **Check 9d** — out-of-band ritual-wedge detector (`time()-max(mc_ritual_last_success_timestamp)`>1800s→Telegram, live-verified at 58s); VACUUM moot (freelist 35 pages); still open: AV key rotation, resume goal-FAILED persistence (capability, not hardening), container-limits watch. Memory: `feedback_eval_silence_floor`.)

> Last updated: 2026-07-10 (**§17 check 6a was measuring ENGAGEMENT, not endorsement — brief-acceptance rebuilt.** Operator asked to "make 6a a pass" since the morning briefs had been good. Investigating instead of complying found 6a was not measuring brief quality _or_ label calibration: `resolveBriefingOnOperatorReply` fires from `router.ts:1238` on **every inbound owner message**, and the rule was _"anything that isn't a discard promotes"_ (the rendered footer said exactly that: _"Responde lo que sea para conservar este resumen"_). So `proposed_briefings.status` recorded **whether the operator texted Jarvis**, uncorrelated with a brief's color. Evidence: all **28/28** promotions in the 30d window came from unrelated messages (`promoted_by_message_id` NULL on every row — nobody ever replied _to_ a brief), and the single `discarded` was the 2026-07-09 message _"Dejamos para después el Denue americano… Cierra ese tema"_ (about the DENUE project), which missed `DISCARD_RE` and was then judged a discard by the **V8.2 LLM classifier**. Green and red briefs both scored **100%** acceptance; the "0.8× structurally stuck" ratio was noise about noise (really 1.0× — two `superseded` green briefs were being counted as _rejections_). **This is a safety issue, not cosmetic**: §17 passing is a documented blocker on V8.3 L≥3 autonomous execution, and `judgment-linkage.ts` lets only green/yellow judgments autonomous-execute — 6a is the ONLY evidence the colors mean anything. **Fix (4 parts)**: (1) `promote.ts` — new `classifyOperatorVerdict()`; a brief resolves only on an explicit verdict matched against the **WHOLE message** (`^…$`, `u` flag, questions excluded, DISCARD tested before ACCEPT so "no sirve" can't promote); unrelated messages leave it `pending`, silence expires it; the LLM classifier may now only ESCALATE an explicit accept into a discard, never resolve on its own; PUSHBACK still reaches the classifier (it feeds §17 check 5). (2) `render.ts` — footer advertises the vocabulary (`Responde *sirve* o *descarta*`). (3) `v82-activation-gate.ts` 6a — score only briefs carrying a real verdict (`superseded`/`expired`/`pending` are the ABSENCE of a verdict, not rejections); new `GATE_V82_MIN_ACCEPTANCE_BRIEFS=3` per color; `redRate===0` → `Infinity` → **pass** (perfect discrimination previously fell through to `insufficient_data` — the gate could not recognize the behavior it rewards); new `GATE_V82_ACCEPTANCE_SINCE=2026-07-10` cutover so the poisoned rows never score. (4) **Cascade caught by qa-auditor** — §13 check 2's `promoted/generated` promote-rate would have COLLAPSED once briefs began expiring unruled (silence read as rejection), un-passing the §13 gate fixed earlier the same day; rebased to `promoted/ruled` with a `GATE_MIN_RULED_BRIEFS=3` floor, so a single ruling can't decide the gate. **qa-auditor R1 = FAIL** (C1: my first fix used an `ok|dale|listo|confirmo` substring allow-list — everyday Spanish; `"dale prioridad al CRM"` still promoted the brief. W1: the §13 cascade. W2: accented `útil` silently failed the ASCII `\b`). **R2 = PASS-with-warnings**, both new residuals then closed too (W-A interrogative `"¿sirve?"`→accept; W-B §13 n=1 floor). **Live-verified: §13 ✅ PASS (cache-read 88.2%/39 runs; promote-rate 85.7% over 7 ruled); §17 ⏳ INSUFFICIENT DATA** — _"not enough operator-ruled briefs since 2026-07-10: 0 green / 0 red (need ≥3 of each; only 'sirve'/'descarta' verdicts count)"_ — the honest verdict, replacing a false FAIL. **eval:gate NOT owed** (the footer is operator-facing text, not a model prompt; regexes are deterministic — confirmed with the auditor). tsc 0; 137 briefing tests green (+13). **Operator action: answer the 06:00 brief with `sirve` or `descarta`** — 6a scores the next 7 days forward. **Known residual (documented in code):** this is a keyword contract, not a reply-binding one; `promoted_by_message_id` + `IncomingMessage.replyTo` exist for the robust fix but no adapter populates `replyTo`. Memory: `feedback_briefing_acceptance_was_engagement`.)

> Last updated: 2026-07-10 (**§13 activation gate now PASSES — `heavy` excluded as a cold-start path (a measurement fix, NOT a prompt fix).** Asked to "fix the cache-read prefix to clear §13." Investigation refuted the premise: **no prefix defect existed.** `fast` — the path §13 exists to watch — was already at **80.5%/24h and 87.2%/14d**, above the ≥80% bar. The entire FAIL came from ONE `heavy` run dragging a **token-weighted** aggregate to ~78.3%. `heavy` fires **exactly once per day** (14/14 days, 1 run each), so every run is a cold start that must pay `cache_creation` — the _same_ 5-min-TTL argument that already excluded `reflection:%`. Worse, its ratio is capped by **turn count, not prefix health**: a cold N-turn run creates the prefix once and reads it N−1 times → cache-read ≤ (N−1)/N. `heavy` runs ~1.7–1.9 turns → a ~43–48% ceiling, and it measured exactly that every day (and 75.7–77.6% on 06-27/28 when it happened to run ~4.5 turns — same cache, different N). **Decisive consequence: the ceiling is INDEPENDENT of prefix size**, so shrinking `heavy`'s ~350k-token cold prefix would cut cost but move the ratio by ~0 — _no prompt-prefix change could ever clear §13_. The gate's own 2026-05-27 docstring assumed `heavy` sat on "the high-frequency path"; live data says otherwise, so the exclusion was applied inconsistently. **Fix** (`activation-gate.ts`): new exported `GATE_COLD_START_AGENT_TYPES = ["heavy"]`, added to the cache-read query as `AND agent_type NOT IN (…)` alongside the `reflection:%` prefix filter; a second dated spec-correction note records the turn-count mechanism. To keep the exclusion from _hiding_ a real regression (qa-auditor W1), the gate now also returns a **non-gating** `excludedColdStart {runs, cacheReadPct, costUsd}`, rendered by `mc-ctl briefing-gate` as an `ℹ excluded cold-start` line. **Live-verified: §13 ✅ PASS — cache-read 85.9% over 33 runs; `ℹ excluded cold-start (heavy): 1 run, 42.5%, $8.20 — not scored`.** qa-auditor PASS-with-warnings, every rationale claim independently re-verified against `mc.db`; its 3 Info findings were factual errors in my own docstring and were fixed (the "43-55% every single day" claim is contradicted by 06-27/28; 78.6%→~78.3%; $7.43→~$8). **eval:gate NOT owed** — no model-id, system-prompt, or tool-description touched (SQL filter + const + comments only); confirmed by the auditor. 13 gate tests (+2, incl. one proven to fail without the fix) · 124 briefing tests green · tsc 0 · deployed `--drain` (newPid 141412). **Trade-off accepted, stated plainly**: `heavy`'s ~$8/run cold start no longer gates §13. Also of note, §17 remains FAIL on its sole blocker (acceptance 6a, 0.8×, structurally stuck) — unaffected by this change. Memory: `feedback_gate_cold_start_exclusion`.)

> Last updated: 2026-07-06 (**Fix: skill-discovery Auto-skill misroute — host-only `skill_save` sent to the sandbox.** `/diagnose` of the follow-up flagged by the PR-misroute fix (below). `src/intelligence/skill-discovery.ts` spawned its auto-save task with `agentType:"auto"`, so the classifier (detecting on `title+description`) matched `\bgit\b` in the detected tool-pattern (_"file edit + git commit + git push"_) → `"coding task → nanoclaw"`. But the task's actual work is `skill_save`/`skill_list` — HOST tools that write the host `mc.db` and are **not registered in the nanoclaw container** (confirmed by a failed run's own output: _"skill_list/skill_save tools are not registered in this sandbox"_); the sandbox's `MC_DB_PATH` is a throwaway `/tmp/mc.db` anyway. Net: **0 skills persisted 2026-06-28..07-06**, 12 failed + 3 hollow-"completed" runs + reaction-retry spam. **Fix**: submit with explicit `agentType:"fast"` (in-process host runner where the tools are registered + write the host DB); explicit override bypasses the misclassification and keeps reaction retries on fast too. Regression test asserts the Auto-skill submitTask carries `agentType:"fast"`. Same misroute CLASS as the PR bug — **route a task by what it DOES, not what its text looks like.** **Second half (qa-auditor catch, follow-up commit):** routing to `fast` was necessary but not sufficient — the task's prompt instructions were also out of sync with `skill_save`'s schema (`trigger_text`→`trigger`; omitted the REQUIRED `description`; both `NOT NULL`), so a model following the prose would still fail the save. Aligned the instructions to the real required params. qa-auditor PASS-with-warnings (fast routing verified correct end-to-end: host tools registered + write host mc.db, no confirm-stall, benign retries). tsc 0; 4 skill-discovery tests green; deployed dist verified. Memory: `feedback_skill_discovery_host_tool_misroute`.)

> Last updated: 2026-07-06 (**Fix: truncated-title `\bPR\b` misroute — plain chat → nanoclaw sandbox → "[Task failed]".** `/diagnose` of "Jarvis having a tough time with a simple request." A plain Spanish question (_"…Su **precio** actual…"_) was routed to the `nanoclaw` coding sandbox and failed. **Root cause** (proven by replaying the exact stored title through the compiled classifier — full msg→`fast`, truncated→`nanoclaw`, 2000/2000): the router truncates chat titles to 60 chars **for display**, the cut split `precio`→`pr`, and `STRONG_CODING_PATTERNS`' **case-insensitive** `\bPR\b` (pull-request abbrev) matched the orphaned `pr` → `isCodingTask=true` → sandbox has nothing to author → task failed. All 10 nanoclaw failures that day were misroutes; the runner itself was fine. **Fix A+B+C** (`classifier.ts`+`dispatcher.ts`+`router.ts`): (A) `\bPR\b` split to its own **case-sensitive** pattern (real PRs are "PR"; kills the whole Spanish `pr…`-fragment class, keeps `PR #42`/`pull request`); (B) new `detectionText` on `ClassificationInput`+`TaskSubmission` — messaging now classifies on the **untruncated** message (both router chat sites pass `msg.text`/`taskText`), eliminating the entire truncation-forged-signal class (`gitano`→`git` etc.); (C) dispatcher **fast-fallback** — a messaging chat that lands on nanoclaw and fails with no error re-runs ONCE on `fast` in-process (same taskId → reply still delivers). qa-auditor PASS-with-warnings; all addressed pre-commit: W1 (rescued run re-stamped `agent_type='fast'` + dual cost rows so a fast success isn't booked as a nanoclaw success — which would bias the classifier BACK toward the misroute), W2 (container slot freed before the fast re-run), W3 (added discriminating fix-B tests where title and detectionText classify differently), R1 (fallback gated on `!result.error` so error-bearing failures surface honestly). 78 classifier tests + dispatcher/reactions green (159 scoped); tsc 0. **Eval gate**: the committed baseline (63.35) was a PROVISIONAL 257-tool nightly seed, explicitly "NOT comparable" to the standalone 160-tool gate; a pristine-incumbent run scored an IDENTICAL **60.35** (classification 100, the only dim this change touches) — the fix is provably gate-NEUTRAL, the FAIL was pure baseline staleness. Recalibrated `eval-baseline.json` to the gate-native incumbent (60.35) per the file's own instruction. Memory: `feedback_truncated_title_pr_misroute`. **Open follow-up** (flagged, not fixed): internal skill-discovery "Auto-skill" tasks also misroute to nanoclaw (their `skill_save` can't persist in the sandbox's throwaway `/tmp/mc.db`) — separate misroute.)

> Last updated: 2026-07-06 (**V8.3 Phase 7 — §14 v1 activation gate SHIPPED. All V8.3-internal phases (0–7) now COMPLETE.** New `src/lib/v8-3/activation-gate.ts` `evaluateV83Gate(db)`: the 6 §14 v1 checks (schema 5 objects · V8.2 dep · 6 caps seeded · ≥7 shadow decisions/7d · linkage integrity = 0 L≥3-with-NULL-judgment · reversibility coverage = 0 L≥3-without-reversal_op). Verdict precedence: a structural breach (5/6) or missing substrate → `fail`; thin shadow → `insufficient_data` (cadence trap); else `pass`. `proposed_at` windowing normalized via `datetime()` on both sides (qa-auditor: more correct than the spec's literal SQL). Surfaced by `mc-ctl v83-gate` (`scripts/v83-gate.ts`, read-only, exit 0/1/2). **Verified live: substrate all-green, 0 shadow decisions → insufficient_data** (correct — canary armed, no confirmed delete yet). qa-auditor PASS (0 Critical/0 Warning). 151 v8-3 tests green (+7). **Remaining to actually FLIP L≥3** (all now OUTSIDE V8.3-internal code): (1) V8.2 §17 pass — needs the acceptance affordance (buildable) + measurement window; (2) 7-day shadow accumulation (decisions must flow — currently 0); (3) operator's first L1→L2 promotion (a small guarded command, deferred — gated on §14 green + §17). [[project-v83-autonomous-execution-gates]].)

> Last updated: 2026-07-06 (**V8.3 Phase 6 — §12/§14 consent linkage (the bilateral-consent gate) SHIPPED dormant.** New `src/lib/v8-3/judgment-linkage.ts` `checkJudgmentLinkage(judgmentId, nowIso, db)`: an autonomous (L≥3) decision requires a linked V8.2 judgment that is (a) green/yellow (red can NEVER autonomous-execute), (b) CRITIC `verdict='approved'` (reuses `criticVerdict`), (c) surfaced in a PRIOR delivered brief. Fails CLOSED on every miss (null id / not-found / red / unapproved / null-trail / not-delivered). Prior-brief compare normalizes the ISO `nowIso` via `datetime(?)` against SQLite-datetime `delivered_at` (avoids a space-vs-`T` lexical wrong-pass; fails safe even if formats diverge). Pipeline: after the reversibility demote, an `if(effectiveLevel>=3)` block demotes to confirm on linkage failure (reason surfaced in the `autonomy_demoted` event). L≤2 never consults it — an operator-pull with `judgment_id NULL` is unaffected (R2 #9). **Inert for the live L1 canary** (never reaches the ≥3 block). qa-auditor PASS (0 Critical; W1 = a future L≥3 caller's pipeline-failure degrade must fail CLOSED, documented in `trigger.ts`; I1 datetime-comment corrected). 144 v8-3 tests green (+19; the L≥3 autonomous tests now seed a valid linked judgment). **Explicitly deferred** (no silent drop): the `jarvis_audit_decisions` operator tool (Phase 6's observability half) + judgment→action _relevance_ binding (validity is checked, not that the judgment proposed THIS action — pin at the L≥3 wiring). **L≥3 now needs only: Phase 7 (activation gate) + V8.2 §17 pass.** [[project-v83-autonomous-execution-gates]].)

> Last updated: 2026-07-06 (**V8.3 Phase 5 — prompt-injection defense (§8) SHIPPED dormant.** New `src/lib/v8-3/external-content.ts`: the `<external_content trust=…>` envelope (XML-escaped body → no tag breakout), `normalizeTrust` (fail-safe to `untrusted`), a DETERMINISTIC injection heuristic (`detectInjection`, 8 labeled vectors × EN+ES — NO LLM per R2 #6), `scanExternalContent` (scans every item regardless of declared trust), and the `EXTERNAL_CONTENT_STANDING_RULE` const. Pipeline step 4b: a trigger's `externalContent` is scanned BEFORE confirm/execute; a hit HALTS the decision as `interrupted` (`decision_events('interrupted', prompt_injection_suspected')`, never executes) and the row persists for the §8 metric. **Inert for the live canary** (`trigger.ts` passes no externalContent; documented wiring-contract: a future caller MUST handle the `interrupted` return, not swallow it as empty output). qa-auditor PASS (0 Critical; W1 English-only → **added Spanish patterns**, the deployment's real threat surface, +5 ES probes now caught; W2 trigger-contract documented; classifier documented as a TRIPWIRE in front of the confirm/reversibility boundary, not itself a guarantee). **Scope honesty**: the DETERMINISTIC half is built + pipeline-wired (dormant); the LLM-facing half (standing rule in a live system prompt + SDK-cache verify) is deferred to Phase 6 — V8.3 has no LLM site yet. 125 v8-3 tests green (+22); tsc 0. **L≥3 still needs: Phase 6 (V8.2 judgment-linkage / §12 critic) + Phase 7 (activation gate) + V8.2 §17 pass.** [[project-v83-autonomous-execution-gates]].)

> Last updated: 2026-07-06 (**V8.3 — closed the two reversibility safety seams (L≥3 prerequisites).** The Phase-3 audit flagged two seams that had to close before any capability could reach autonomous (L≥3) execution; both done, both hardening ONLY the future autonomy path (the live `jarvis_file_delete` canary — max_level=2, no sqlMutation — is byte-identical). **Seam (a)**: `runDecisionPipeline` now binds `buildReversalOp` to the capability's CANONICAL reversal strategy (`reversalStrategyForCapability` over the immutable `CAPABILITY_SEEDS`), not the caller's `trigger.sqlMutation.strategy` — a compensating-only capability (`northstar_sync`) can never be coerced into building a local `sql_inverse` (the 2026-05-12 resurrection risk); a mismatching trigger throws before anything is built or stored. Sourced from the seed constant, NOT a mutable DB column, so it can't drift — no schema migration. **Seam (b)**: the §7 reversibility demote moved OUT of the `sqlMutation` branch to fire GENERALLY — an L≥3 decision with no proven `sql_inverse` inverse (including a trigger that declared no mutation at all → `reversalOp=null`) now demotes to confirm, so there is no autonomous path without a proven undo, independent of the mutable `max_level`. qa-auditor PASS (0 Critical/0 Warning; live-canary non-impact verified). 103 v8-3 tests green (reworked 4 L≥3 tests to declare real sql_inverse mutations + 2 new seam tests); tsc 0. Remaining L≥3 blockers: Phase 5 injection defense (next) + V8.2 §17 pass. [[project-v83-autonomous-execution-gates]].)

> Last updated: 2026-07-06 (**V8.2 §17 grounding — day-log gather + metric-vintage fix.** Follow-up to the metric split, targeting the ~57% of unfixables that ARE real defects. RCA against live judgments (#36/#37/#39/#46) confirmed the root cause: the producer's evidence ledger (`gatherEvidence`, `decompose.ts`) was tasks + one subject-keyed KB FTS query, while the critic verifies against a far broader surface (5 arbitrary recall queries + SQL over 6 tables incl. day-logs) — so the author mis-cited or propagated stale "no work since `<date>`" claims the critic then disproved (e.g. #46: prose said "no execution since 2026-06-17" while day-logs 06-27/06-28 existed). **FIX B**: new `retrieveRecentDayLogs()` — deterministic base-table `jarvis_files` scan (`instr` literal match, date-shape-anchored GLOB `logs/day-logs/[0-9]{4}-...md`, newest-first, limit 3) appended in `gatherEvidence` when a subject is set; emits `kind:'kb_entry'` (dedups with the KB pass, inherits the uncited-source confidence filter). Verified live it surfaces the exact 06-27/06-28 logs the author was missing. **FIX A** (correctness bug in `b9bb209`): the gate's critic-unverified backward-compat matched only the newer `(critic could not verify)` marker, missing the older #38 vintage → miscounted an infra failure as a genuine defect; now matches EITHER that or the inner `CRITIC_NO_TOOL_CALL_MSG` (extracted to a shared const). qa-auditor PASS-w-W (W1 GLOB-anchoring fixed + regression test; I4 stale comment corrected). Stale comments in critic.ts/decompose.ts corrected. Follow-ups (flagged, not done): thread project SLUG for reliable day-log match on display-name subjects; authoring-faithfulness lever (prose overstating its OWN cited evidence — #36/#46-claim0). Full suite green via pre-commit; tsc 0.)

> Last updated: 2026-07-06 (**V8.3 canary ARMED + V8.2 §17 unfixable-metric split.** (1) V8.3 audit-ledger canary armed for `jarvis_file_delete` (systemd drop-in `V83_ENABLED=true`; observability-not-a-gate; L≥3 autonomy still blocked on Phase 5 + 2 reversal seams + V8.2 §17). (2) RCA of "why is §17 unfixable stuck at 30%": the metric conflated two failures under one label — ~43% (3/7) were critic INFRA failures (the critic LLM returned free text instead of calling `submit_critic_verdict`, escalated to `unfixable` only so it can't auto-approve), ~57% genuine producer grounding/citation drift. Fix: `escalationDisposition` now stamps a structured `unfixableReason` (`contradicted`/`unsupported`/`unverified`) persisted in `critic_trail_json`; the §17 gate (`v82-activation-gate.ts` check 4) EXCLUDES `unverified` from both numerator and denominator (`measuredVerdicts`), so a broken critic can no longer dilute the rate toward green, and surfaces `criticUnverified` separately. Backward-compat: old rows retro-classified via the `CRITIC_UNVERIFIED_MARKER` (self-retires in 7d). qa-auditor PASS (0 Critical/Warning; 2 Info, 1 closed with the all-unverified→insufficient_data edge test). +2 source files + gate/critic tests; 53 scoped green; tsc 0. eval-gate N/A (no model/prompt/tool change).)

> **Status note 2026-05-07**: Stabilization freeze (originally 2026-04-22 → 2026-05-22) **lifted 15 days early** by operator after 5/5 hardening dims closed and five distinct stability fixes shipped this week without rollbacks. Mission-control core can take new tools/adapters/runners again. Hindsight A/B/C strategic decision (`docs/planning/hindsight-strategic-options.md`) and queued Datatur/risk-surface work are unblocked. Re-benchmark planned for 2026-05-22 still stands. See `feedback_freeze_lifted_2026_05_07.md`.

## Overview

Unified AI agent orchestrator. Routes tasks by complexity to the right runner type. Single TypeScript process, SQLite, Hono HTTP server. Jarvis — a general-purpose strategic AI assistant with internet access, Google Workspace integration, adaptive intelligence, and long-term memory.

**Repo**: `/root/claude/mission-control/` | GitHub: `kosm1x/agent-controller`

## Metrics

| Metric        | Value                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| ------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Source files  | 529 (non-test .ts, 2026-07-05 post-refactor; 140,472 LOC — was 142,528)                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| Test files    | 389 (vitest, 2026-07-05 post-refactor: 388 passed + 1 skipped)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| Tests passing | 6619 (+1 skipped, +1 todo = 6621 total; full-suite pre-commit run 2026-07-05 post-refactor — dead modules took their suites with them, ~45 new invariant/safety tests added)                                                                                                                        |
| Type errors   | 0                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| Total tools   | **257** (live boot log 2026-07-05 post-refactor: jarvis_init one-shot removed; 4 ToolSource plugins active — memory source Hindsight-gated off). 157 deferred (~52% prompt token reduction)                                                                                                         |
| Dependencies  | 15 core + 2 messaging (hono, @hono/node-server, better-sqlite3, @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, node-cron, @opendataloader/pdf, pino, zod, prom-client, mammoth, @playwright/mcp, @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk, fingerprint-injector, @antv/infographic, playwright + @whiskeysockets/baileys, grammy) |

| v4.0.3  | Hermes v0.5 Reliability Patterns — (1) Turn-scoped signal cleanup: stale ALTO/SYSTEM nudges stripped before next LLM round. (2) Think-block exhaustion: strips reasoning XML, detects degenerate all-thinking responses, forces wrap-up. (3) SQLite WAL jitter retry: writeWithRetry with 20-150ms random jitter on SQLITE_BUSY, busy_timeout 5s→1s, periodic PASSIVE checkpoint. QA audit: 3 high findings fixed (selfAssess token tracking, JSON shape guard, insertBullet TOCTOU). 172 src, 69 test files, 756 tests                                                                | Done   | —         |

| v4.0.11 | 5-layer hallucination defense — scope gap (destructive→read+write), post-nudge persistence, Layer 0 (failed writes), passive voice detection (✅+participle), clitic pronoun confirmation, fast-path tool triggers (procede/ejecuta), poison filter tuning. 11 fixes, 16 DB purges. 859 tests | Done | `fa96771` |
| v4.0.12 | Fix commit\_\_delete_item invisible to LLM — destructive tools now visible whenever any COMMIT scope activates (scope gate removed, execution lock is real safety). Scheduled tasks pre-authorize destructive tools when explicitly listed. 862 tests | Done | — |
| v4.0.13 | Fix hallucination guard false positive on destructive tools — failed-then-succeeded tool calls (CONFIRMATION_REQUIRED → unlock → success) no longer trigger Layer 0. Expanded deletion confirmation patterns (DIRECT_DELETE_COMMAND, DELETION_ASK_PATTERN, CONFIRM_PATTERN). CONFIRMATION_REQUIRED message rewritten to prevent LLM "I don't have the tool" hallucination. schedule_task description updated with autoprune example. 8 poisoned thread entries purged. 871 tests | Done | `614a46d` |
| v4.0.14 | Fix hallucination guard Layer 3 false positive — completion-claim patterns ("acabo de actualizar", "he verificado", "just updated") split from impossible-narration patterns (FTP/SSH, ellipsis progress). Completion claims only trigger when NO write tools called. Impossible narration (fabricated connections, processing steps) still always triggers. 1 poisoned entry purged. 877 tests | Done | — |
| v4.0.15 | Fix poison filter false positives — `✅.*en progreso` matched status reports 80+ chars away (limited to .{0,80}, removed "en progreso" as trigger). `no puedo hacer` matched capability listings (narrowed to sentence-start + removed "hacer"). Legitimate diagnostic responses no longer stripped from thread buffer. 877 tests | Done | `6f249ff` |
| v4.0.16 | Meta scope for diagnostics — "autodiagnostico", "nivel operativo", "herramientas disponibles", "lista las tools" now load ALL tool groups so Jarvis can give accurate inventory. Coding scope expanded with language names (typescript, python, javascript, react, node.js) and programming verbs (rutina, función, implementa, refactor). Brave API key rotated. Google OAuth refresh token renewed. 880 tests | Done | `0d72001` |
| v4.0.17 | Fix conversational messages stuck in tool-nudge loop — fast-path fallback 5→8 words (short statements without tool triggers skip full pipeline). Nudge guard gated on `availableNonReadOnly.size > 0` (only fires when write tools in scope). Meta scope regex fixed for Spanish suffixes (autodiagnóstico). Coding scope expanded. 884 tests | Done | `ab3cce6` |
| v4.0.18 | COMMIT_READ always-on + QA audit fixes. Read tools moved to CORE_TOOLS via spread (dedup). Hallucination guard WRITE_TOOLS fixed: 11 phantom Google tool names replaced with 9 correct ones (calendar_create, gdocs_write, etc.). fullCount diagnostic now includes SPECIALTY+RESEARCH. Meta scope now includes commit_journal. case-miner maps research group. 10 new tests (meta completeness, detectActiveGroups). 894 tests | Done | `9449d7b` |
| v4.0.19 | Tool result eviction — fixes hallucination on long documents. Root cause: web_read truncated 58K docs to 10K (lossy head-only), adapter 12K eviction never fired. Fix: shared `evictToFile()` utility writes full content to temp file, returns preview + markdown TOC + file_path for file_read. Double-eviction prevention via `hasEvictedPath()`. Probabilistic cleanup (1h TTL). 9 new tests. 903 tests | Done | `70118a4` |
| v5.0 S1a | Guard upgrades — 4-layer doom-loop detection (content-chanting, canonical JSON fingerprint + outcome-aware, ping-pong cycle, n-gram Jaccard). Graduated 4-level escalation ladder (retry→escalate model→force wrapup→abort). Phantom action detection (ES+EN). Circuit breaker registry (CLOSED/OPEN/HALF_OPEN per service, /health integration). Session repair before inference (orphan removal, synthetic errors, dedup, merge). WRITE_TOOLS compile-time sync test. QA audit: consecutiveReadOnlyRounds reset fix, Map growth caps, timer cleanup. Hotfix: preserve user messages from poisoned exchanges for scope inheritance — provider timeouts were stripping COMMIT scope keywords from thread buffer. 7 new files, 63 new tests. 966 tests | Done | `b5b83b6` |
| v5.0 S1b | Memory upgrades — 4-level compaction pipeline (L0 truncate old tool results → L1 pair drain → L2 LLM summary → L3 emergency truncation). Mechanical auto-persist (response >2K + tools >3, Playwright always, question + >1K). Three-window spending quotas (hourly/daily/monthly, fixed-boundary SQL, pre-call wouldExceedBudget, /health threeWindow, /metrics 4 new gauges). QA audit: compaction boundary guards, dispatcher window reporting, auto-persist error logging. 5 new files, 33 new tests. 999 tests | Done | `14d09c4` |
| v5.0 S2 | Concurrent task isolation — per-task execution context (TaskExecutionContext + createTaskExecutor), replaces shared globals for destructive locks + memory rate limits. Provider failure → completed_with_concerns (was silently "completed"). inferWithTools returns exitReason + roundsCompleted. New task_history builtin tool (#138) — LLM queries own past executions. Concurrency metrics (mc_tasks_active gauges). 5 new files, 12 new tests. 1011 tests, 138 tools | Done | `42861b8` |
| Scope fixes | Follow-up inheritance threshold 50→80 chars. Tightened referential phrases (require verb context to avoid false-positive on new topics). PDF direct trigger for research scope ("lee este PDF", ".pdf"). QA audit: bare "lo que" no longer triggers inheritance. 7 new scope tests. 1017 tests | Done | `db0515d` |
| v5.0 S4 | CRM integration — crm_query builtin tool (#139) queries CRM REST API (pipeline, cuota, descarga, actividades, equipo, alertas, vp-glance). VP-level JWT auth. Conditional registration (CRM_API_TOKEN). hasCrm scope gate. "crm" scope group for sales/pipeline keywords. QA audit: fresh token per call, non-JSON guard, scope env gate. 2 new scope tests. 1019 tests | Done | `5a3987c` |
| Jarvis File System | Internal knowledge base — jarvis_files SQLite table with tags, qualifiers (enforce/always-read/conditional/reference/workspace), priority ordering, related_to chains. 6 new tools (#140-145): jarvis_file_read/write/update/list/delete + jarvis_init. Auto-injection in fast-runner (8K char budget). Filesystem mirror at data/jarvis/. Auto-persist + compaction summaries write to jarvis_files. Restructured to Layer 0 (src/db/jarvis-fs.ts) — seedDirectives on boot. QA: updateMetadata extracted, no raw DB in tools. 12 new tests. 1031 tests, 145 tools | Done | `97d89af` |
| CRM ↔ Jarvis | Bidirectional integration — POST /api/jarvis-pull endpoint for CRM agents to request analysis. Role-based depth (AE: 3 bullets/300 tokens, VP: full/2000 tokens). CRM persona templates updated with mandatory flow (doc link first, commentary separate). QA: JSON parse guard, fresh token per call | Done | `e6c19c5` |
| Context Pressure Awareness | Jarvis self-aware of context window usage — estimateTokens() extracted, advisory injected at 70% capacity, compaction metadata (level + removedCount) in inference return type. always-read SOP teaches Jarvis to warn users and be concise. /compact-jarvis Claude Code skill. QA: division-by-zero guard, always-read budget exemption, 5 new estimateTokens tests. 1036 tests | Done | `26f2077` |
| v5.0 S5b | Knowledge maps for Prometheus — 2 new tools (knowledge_map, knowledge_map_expand), 2 SQLite tables, LLM-generated 8-12 node domain overviews (concept/pattern/gotcha), wikilink-in-prose links, 7-day TTL, max 60 nodes/depth 5. Planner injects map context, reflector scores against gotchas. file_delete tool (#148) with path restriction + depth guard. QA: deleteMap before regeneration, MAX-based nextNodeSeq, isStale uses updated_at, prefix root guard. 1074 tests, 148 tools | Done | `a5de2f2` |
| Google Drive CRUD | Full Drive file management — gdrive_upload (multipart .md upload + update by file_id), gdrive_delete (trash with protected folder guard), gdrive_move (addParents/removeParents), gdrive_list parent_folder_id filter, gdocs_replace (clear + rewrite). Stale loop signature-aware fix. Obsidian sync protocol rewritten with correct tool workflows. FTS5 enrichment contamination purged. 1076 tests, 156 tools | Done | — |

## v5.0 — Scalability + New Capabilities

Full design: `V5-NORTHSTAR.md` (909 lines, themes, code examples, open questions). Execution tracking: `V5-ROADMAP.md`. Intel Depot: `V5-INTELLIGENCE-DEPOT.md`.

External pattern sources: Crucix (delta engine, alerts), aden-hive/hive (compaction, doom-loop, quality gate), PraisonAI (ping-pong, escalation, circuit breaker), OpenFang (session repair, phantom detection, spending quotas), HyperGraph (knowledge maps), Feynman (research verification), OpenMontage (video production, TS reimplementation).

| Session         | Theme                     | Scope                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Source                       | Status   |
| --------------- | ------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | -------- |
| S1a             | Guard upgrades            | Doom-loop detection, escalation ladder, circuit breaker, session repair, WRITE_TOOLS sync test                                                                                                                                                             | hive + PraisonAI + OpenFang  | **Done** |
| S1a.1           | Token budget overload fix | web_read MAX_CONTENT 10K→5K, dynamic budget escalation (>60 tools → 40K browser-tier budget). Root cause: 78 scoped tool defs (~15K tokens) + web_read content (~3K) exceeded 30K ceiling → hallucination cascade on forced wrap-up                        | Diagnosis                    | **Done** |
| S1a.2           | Truncated args salvage    | Adapter auto-salvages content from truncated tool args to temp file, returns file path for retry. `gdocs_write` and `file_write` gain `content_file` param (same pattern as `wp_publish`). Prevents empty docs from 14K+ inline content hitting max_tokens | v4.0.1 pattern               | **Done** |
| S1a.3           | MCP auto-reconnect        | Failed MCP servers retried every 60s (up to 10 attempts). Telegram alert on degradation and recovery                                                                                                                                                       | Incident 2026-04-02          | **Done** |
| S1b             | Memory upgrades           | 4-level compaction, auto-persist, spending quotas                                                                                                                                                                                                          | hive + OpenFang              | **Done** |
| S2              | Concurrent task isolation | Per-task execution context, provider failure status, task_history tool, concurrency metrics                                                                                                                                                                | S2 analysis + prod diagnosis | **Done** |
| S3              | Embedding-based scoping   | **Deferred to v6.0** — regex accuracy 92%+ after targeted fixes. Embeddings not justified at current scale                                                                                                                                                 | —                            | Deferred |

VP-level JWT, hasCrm scope gate                                                                                                                                                      | v4 carry                     | **Done** |

## Rituals

| Ritual               | Time                       | Delivery                                                                                         |
| -------------------- | -------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Signal intelligence  | 6:00 AM Mexico City        | Email (fede@eurekamd.net) + user_facts                                                           |
| Morning briefing     | 7:00 AM Mexico City        | Email (fede@eurekamd.net) + Telegram                                                             |
| Nightly close        | 10:00 PM Mexico City       | Email (fede@eurekamd.net) + Telegram                                                             |
| Skill evolution      | 11:00 PM Mexico City       | Analyzes outcomes, deactivates underperforming skills                                            |
| Evolution log        | 11:59 PM Mexico City       | Appends to docs/EVOLUTION-LOG.md                                                                 |
| Overnight tuning     | 1:00 AM Tue/Thu/Sat        | Self-tuning loop (eval → mutate → sandbox → keep/discard)                                        |
| Proactive scanner    | 8AM, noon, 4PM, 8PM        | Telegram (max 2 nudges/day)                                                                      |
| Weekly review        | Sunday 8:00 PM Mexico City | Email (fede@eurekamd.net) + Telegram                                                             |
| Diff digest          | Sunday 8:00 PM Mexico City | Telegram (autonomous activity summary)                                                           |
| Evolution log commit | Sunday 3:00 AM Mexico City | Mechanical (no LLM) — pathspec `git commit` of docs/EVOLUTION-LOG.md for durability (2026-06-17) |

## Autonomous Improvement Safeguards

| Safeguard                | Description                                                                                                       | Enforcement                                  |
| ------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| SG1 — Weekly Diff Digest | Aggregates all Jarvis-authored changes (PRs, directives, patterns, commits) from past 7 days. Mechanical — no LLM | Cron Sunday 8 PM, Telegram HTML              |
| SG2 — HTTP Kill Switch   | POST /api/admin/kill-autonomous disables loop at runtime + cancels running tasks. Optional passphrase             | X-Api-Key + X-Kill-Passphrase                |
| SG3 — Immutable Core     | 10 files + src/api/ directory that Jarvis can NEVER modify, even on jarvis/\* branches                            | Checked in file_write, file_edit, shell_exec |
| SG4 — Directive Cooldown | Max 1 directive proposal per 48h (configurable via DIRECTIVE_COOLDOWN_HOURS)                                      | safeguard_state DB table                     |
| SG5 — Pre-Cycle Git Tag  | Tags HEAD as pre-auto-YYYY-MM-DD before each autonomous improvement cycle. Prune >30 days, keep min 10            | execFileSync in scheduler                    |

| 2026-06-27 (V8.2 §17 — citation gate scored per distinct claim, not evidence row) | `568a970` | **Targets the §17 citation-resolver dimension (the second §17 gap).** Ground-truth investigation: the check counted `attributed_claims` ROWS, but the normalized schema stores one row PER evidence ref, so a multi-source claim is K rows. On the live DB, 24 contradicted rows are only **3 distinct claims** (~8 refs each) — over-weighting them Kx dragged row-grain to **74.5%** while the epistemic **claim-grain is 85.7%**; the metric also swung 85.1%→74.5% on evidence-volume churn alone (pure artifact). Fix collapses rows to distinct `(judgment_id, claim_id)` before the rate: a claim is a hit iff EVERY row resolved (`MIN(resolver_status='resolved')`). Mirrors the §17 6a brief-grain recalibration + the claim-grain `countContradictions` §12 already uses — check 3 was the lone row-grain outlier. **Honest, not number-chasing**: every contradicted claim still counts as one full non-hit, gate STILL fails (85.7% < 95%); the fix only stops Kx over-counting + makes the number stable. `evaluateV82Gate` runs ONLY via tsx scripts (`briefing-gate.ts`, `judgments.ts`), never in-service `dist/` — **no deploy needed**, `mc-ctl briefing-gate` already reads 85.7%. The 3 residual contradictions are the SAME two root causes from the absence-guard ship: judgment 32 = author absence over-claim (fixed `ddf43f4`, deployed — ages out ~~7d); judgment 19 = critic vlcms↔vlmp conflation FALSELY contradicting a TRUE VLMP stall (known follow-up, also helps unfixable). 14 gate tests + 118 briefing-suite + 6629 full-suite green; qa-auditor PASS. | 2026-06-23 (V8.2 producer — parallelize judgment assembly; fix deadline starvation) | (this ship) | **Fixed the daily `caller signal already aborted` fault dropping 1 of every 3 shadow judgments.** Root cause (via `/diagnose`): `runJudgmentAssembly` (`produce.ts`) assembled the ≤3 selected judgments **serially**, ~2 min each (decompose→author→2-loop critic = 3–6 sequential Sonnet calls) ⇒ ~6 min > the 5-min `JUDGMENT_ASSEMBLY_DEADLINE_MS` pass cap in `morning-surface.ts`. The 3rd judgment's author/decompose then hit the already-fired `signal.abort()` and threw → `written:2 of attempted:3` every run + daily error noise (NOT an abort-plumbing bug — the plumbing is correct; it was serial-under-deadline starvation). Fix: serial `for`-loop → **`Promise.allSettled`** over `selected` (judgments are independent — own row/claims/critic loop; `better-sqlite3` synchronous so writes can't interleave). Wall-clock now ≈ slowest single judgment (~2.5 min) vs the serial sum, so the 5-min cap reverts to a rarely-hit backstop. Preserved: per-judgment isolation (one rejection never sinks siblings), `judgmentIds` in selection (priority) order, and a pre-aborted-signal short-circuit (no work). 3 new tests (concurrency barrier, selection-order-vs-completion-order via deterministic deferreds, abort short-circuit); 12/12 produce + 287 v8-2/triggers blast-radius green; typecheck + build clean. qa-auditor PASS (0 Critical); folded W1 (doc accuracy: deadline now all-or-tail loss if it fires — verified no §17 consumer diffs `attempted−written`) + W3 (replaced real-timer test deltas with deferreds); deferred W2 (no concurrency limiter — dormant at default cap 3; documented add-if-raised-to-6). **DEPLOYED + VERIFIED LIVE 2026-06-23** (`181c522`; `./scripts/deploy.sh` newPid 840849, 258 tools, health 200): one live producer pass (`run-judgment-producer.ts --run --construct --limit 3`) **attempted 3 / wrote 3 in 137.5s, zero `caller signal already aborted`** (judgments table 11→15; critic ran on all three — #13 unfixable×2, #14/#15 approved). Confirms the fix end-to-end: parallel wall-clock (~2.3 min) sits well under the 5-min cap. Memory `feedback_serial_under_deadline_starvation`. |

| 2026-06-17 (/diagnose — evolution-log self-truncation + durability) | `6c2d82f` (prevention + truth-restore) · `a15e58f` (durability ritual) | **`/diagnose` of "Jarvis trouble writing the evolution log."** The daily evolution-log ritual TRUNCATED its own 45 KB log — the prompt offered `file_write` (whole-file overwrite) as an "append", the model wrote only the new entry → the whole log was destroyed, then it reconstructed lost days from the task DB and tried (blocked) to git-commit the "restore". **Prevention (`6c2d82f`):** removed `file_write` from the ritual tools (poka-yoke), mandated `cat >>` heredoc append, added an anti-git-recovery guard; `shell.ts` append-only gate on `RITUAL_WRITABLE_DOCS` blocks `>`/tee/mv/cp overwrite of the log (best-effort, NOT airtight — `>\|`/truncate/sed -i/relative-path still slip the regex, round-2 audit → durability is the real backstop); truth-restored the file from HEAD (real 06-04→06-14 + genuine 06-15/16; diff vs HEAD +44/-0). **Durability (`a15e58f`):** new weekly MECHANICAL cron `scheduleEvolutionLogCommit` (Sun 03:00 MX) → `commitEvolutionLogIfDirty` does a pathspec-scoped `git commit -- docs/EVOLUTION-LOG.md` so each week's entries become recoverable. NOT an LLM ritual — the git TOOLS throw on `main` (branch gate), so it's raw `execFileSync` git (round-1 audit C1 = LLM version dead-on-arrival → rewritten → round-2 PASS). Pathspec scoping closes the `git_commit` dirty-index sweep (C2); `--no-verify` so a red suite can't block durability; local commit only. Full suite 6243 green. Deployed (newPid 4170780; `evolution-log-commit: scheduled` confirmed). Memory `feedback_evolution_log_truncation`. Follow-ups (queue): `git_commit` bare-commit pathspec fix, WRITE_INDICATORS hardening. |

Tried Option A (port-80 IP catchall via Caddy) but `http://187.77.25.101` already taken by `crm-pulso` (port 3010). Pivoted to strictly-better path: nip.io subdomain `salones.187.77.25.101.nip.io` matching the existing crm-pulso pattern — Caddy auto-provisioned Let's Encrypt cert (nip.io is on Public Suffix List for per-subdomain LE buckets), TLS works on port 443 which every carrier passes, drop-in-replaceable when operator buys real domain (one-line Caddyfile hostname swap). Added single block to `/etc/caddy/Caddyfile`, `caddy validate` + `systemctl reload caddy` clean, verified HTTPS 200 to admin URL, crm-pulso routing unchanged. **Operator confirmed external access works.** Handoff to Jarvis: `NEXT-INSTALL-SYSTEMD-2026-05-23.md` (6.7 KB) instructing him to retire tmux + `useradd salones-wa` + install systemd unit with `User=salones-wa` + `UMask=0077` + verification checklist + RESOLVED section template. Jarvis can `useradd` himself (he has root). **What this session-extension covered**: 4 KB docs (AUDIT, RE-AUDIT, NEXT-INSTALL, plus the original AUDIT was later RESOLVED-appended by Jarvis), 1 Caddyfile block, 0 mission-control code changes, no salones-wa commits by me (Jarvis's repo). **Audit value model**: 12+ deployable bugs prevented + product-correctness preserved including: P0-2 visit_count inflation that would have silently destroyed the reactivation product flow within 1 hour of deploy; ADMIN_TOKEN `changeme` fallback exposing public admin panel on any env misconfig; LFPDPPP-relevant opt-out regex misclassifying cancel requests as unsubscribes; phantom-campaign rows on disconnected Baileys; HTML-injection-via-pushName XSS surface. **Tasks closed**: #234 audit, #235 re-audit, #236 third audit. **Tasks still open from earlier session**: #226 AV key rotation (P0, 1-2h, natural next pickup), #229 NorthStar compass-reframe (blocked-on-operator), #230 S3-C1 WhatsApp disconnects rate (blocked-on-evaluator), #232 retry-after (telemetry-gated), #233 P3 hygiene long-tail. Memory: `feedback_salones_wa_audit_arc_2026_05_23.md` (warm-tier) — 6 connective lessons spanning the audit arc + handoff cadence + nip.io pattern. |

| 2026-05-30 (mc-deploy false-pending — file-mtime reality-check) | (this commit) — `scripts/audit-always-read.ts` + `.test.ts` (35/35): deploy-state reality-check switched from git commit-timestamp (`log -1 --format=%ct -- src`) to **newest non-test `src/**`file mtime** vs`dist/index.js`; new exported `newestSrcFileMtimeMs()`+ 5 regression tests. Also`jarvis-kb/directives/kb-sync-routine.md`bash check rewritten (file-mtime, not`%ct`) and `projects/agent-controller/estado-actual.md`snapshot corrected to`AL DÍA`(both DB rows re-upserted`CAST(readfile AS TEXT)`). | **Operator: "Jarvis insists deploys are pending."** Recurrence of the 2026-05-28 always-read false-pending. `estado-actual.md` (re-synced the same day, 14:35Z) still claimed `mc-deploy PENDIENTE` for `b7ddba3`/`7d8ca81`/`adab87d` — all compiled into the running `dist/` and live since 2026-05-29 06:32:55Z. Root cause: **deploy-then-commit ordering** — `deploy.sh` builds the working tree and routinely runs BEFORE the `git commit`, so a commit's `%ct` (06:39) sits AFTER the build (06:32) even though its code is already shipped. The `audit-always-read.ts` tripwire shipped to catch this shared the exact blind spot (passed the false PENDIENTE; then flagged the corrected `AL DÍA` as STALE — which confirmed the RCA). Script runs via `npx tsx`, so the fix is live without an mc-deploy. Memory: `feedback_always_read_kb_drift.md`. |
| 2026-05-29 (/diagnose — evolution-log false "API timing out") | (this commit) — `src/tools/builtin/shell.ts` `execSync`→`promisify(exec)`; `src/rituals/evolution-log.ts` prompt; `shell.test.ts` +5 tests | **`/diagnose` of "evolution log reporting API timing out".** `EVOLUTION-LOG.md` reported "API unreachable / HTTP 000 / self-call deadlock" every night (05-22→05-28) while `/health` served 200 in ~20ms externally. **Root cause (2 compounding):** (1) `shell_exec` used synchronous `execSync`, which freezes the Node event loop for the command's full duration — the `evolution-log` ritual runs IN-PROCESS (fast runner) and its prompt told it to `curl localhost:8080/health`, but the frozen loop can't answer its own loopback request → HTTP 000. (2) A prompt "verification gate" added after earlier false alarms (curl before writing "unreachable") was structurally impossible to pass in-process, so it manufactured the false alarm nightly. **Fix:** `execSync`→async `promisify(exec)` (non-blocking; kills the deadlock at source for all in-process runners) + folded audit improvements: distinct timeout exit_code `-2` w/ message, non-empty stderr surfaced on success, `error.code` (not `.status`) exit-code read. Ritual prompt: dropped the loopback health-check, reworded so rationale stays true post-fix, fixed duplicate step numbering. **qa-auditor** PASS WITH WARNINGS → all 7 findings folded; headline catch: the async fix had falsified my own prompt's "guaranteed to deadlock" claim in the same bundle (W1). Added 5 direct tests for the previously-uncovered exec contract (success shape, stderr-on-success, real non-zero exit, truncation, timeout `-2`). Suite 5895 pass (1 pre-existing pdf-read TMPDIR). Deployed `deploy.sh` (newPid 2422245), `/health` 200, messaging router up 2ch. Memory: `feedback_in_process_runner_sync_exec_deadlock.md`. |
| 2026-05-28 (late — queue #232 Retry-After honoring) | `7d8ca81` — `src/inference/http-error.ts` adds `resolveRetryDelay(info, expBackoffMs) → {delayMs, source}` + `DEFAULT_MAX_RETRY_AFTER_MS=30s` + `ABSOLUTE_MAX=10min`; `src/inference/adapter.ts:1078` retry-delay path consumes the helper; `+10 tests` (30 total). | **Behavioral half of the 2026-05-23 rate-limit-header capture.** Adapter retry loop was discarding the parsed `Retry-After` and using flat exp-backoff `min(1000*2^attempt, 8000)` — a server saying `Retry-After: 30` got retried in 1-8s and hit 429 again. Fix: prefer the server hint when present, capped at `MC_RETRY_AFTER_MAX_MS` (default 30s, ABSOLUTE_MAX 600s clamps typoed env). Log line gained `(source=retry-after\|exp-backoff)` for journalctl correlation. Telemetry gate widened so the `rate_limit_hit` structured log fires on 5xx-with-hint too (RFC 7231 §7.1.3). **Scope (load-bearing)**: OpenAI-compat fallback + direct Anthropic Messages only; Claude Agent SDK path (prod default since 2026-05-10) short-circuits before this loop and runs its own retry policy. **R1 audit** PASS WITH WARNINGS — folded W1 (5xx telemetry asymmetry → widened log gate), W2 (claude-sdk scope undisclosed → JSDoc paragraph added), S1 (placeholder `afterEachEnvCleanup()` with misleading comment → real `afterEach()` hook). R1-prompt-suggested extra test added (`MC_RETRY_AFTER_MAX_MS=999999999` → clamps to ABSOLUTE_MAX 600000). **R2 audit** PASS — all 3 folds verified clean line-for-line; 4 SUGGEST items all forward-looking (S1 `2^attempt` safe-int moot under 8000 cap; S2 legacy plain-Error path bypass intentional; S3 `prometheus/executor.ts:701` goal-level retry uses its own curve, different layer; S4 SDK JSDoc claim accurate). Pre-commit suite 5891/5892 (1 todo) green. Memory: `feedback_retry_after_over_exp_backoff.md` (4 reusable rules: caller-supplied fallback decouples from curve, source-tagging for journalctl correlation, mandatory cap with absolute clamp, scope-disclose in JSDoc). |
