| 2026-05-27 (evening — @MexicoNecesario schedule + doctrine) | `ce1b666` (track canonical X publisher) + `scheduled_tasks.description` SQL rewrite for id `63bfd0bd` + `jarvis-kb/projects/mexico-necesario-ac/docs/protocolo-publicacion.md` (registered `jarvis_files` qualifier=enforce priority=30) | **3 consecutive failures of the daily `MexicoNecesario — Tweet Diario` schedule today (13:00 MX scheduled + 13:53 + 14:03 manual reprompts) — /diagnose found the schedule prompt said "use shell_exec y twurl o el CLI disponible" but `twurl` was never installed; canonical Playwright publisher `scripts/post-mexiconecesario.cjs` existed on disk (5947 bytes, 2026-05-20) but was untracked AND never referenced by the prompt. Successful runs the prior 6 weekdays depended on the LLM stumbling onto `curl localhost:8080/api/tools/invoke` to bypass scope and fetch the `auth_token`+`ct0` cookies from `user_facts` — pure LLM-exploration luck, not architecture: identical `toolsHash=84a4c042` + `systemPromptHash=18ed59e49938` on passing vs failing runs, only turn count differed (24 turns 4 min passing vs 13 turns 1 min failing). **Fix** (4 layers): (a) `git-add` the canonical script (`ce1b666` pushed `origin/main`) so KB sweeps / janitor jobs can't make it disappear; (b) rewrite `scheduled_tasks.description` for id `63bfd0bd` to name `node /root/claude/mission-control/scripts/post-mexiconecesario.cjs < /tmp/mn-tweet.txt` explicitly with full exit-code branch table (0/2/3/4/5/6 → action) and explicit anti-pattern list ("NO twurl, NO curl directo a API, NO /api/tools/invoke"); (c) mirror doctrine into Jarvis KB at `jarvis-kb/projects/mexico-necesario-ac/docs/protocolo-publicacion.md` registered in `jarvis_files` with `qualifier='enforce'`, `priority=30`, `related_to` cross-link to plan-editorial — auto-loaded when the project is in scope so ad-hoc operator asks hit the same path as scheduled fires; (d) audit memory `feedback_schedule_prompt_canonical_path.md` indexed in MEMORY.md. Cookie probe (no publish) confirmed alive pre-fix: `PROBE_OK: account=/MexicoNecesario, compose box visible` (12d-old, ~18d margin remaining; next rotation due ~2026-06-14). Tonight's tweet shipped via Jarvis manually with the new instructions on first attempt (operator sent the directive; Jarvis followed the rewritten schedule semantics). **Lesson\*\* ([[schedule-prompt-canonical-path]]): underspecified prompts ("use whatever CLI is available") work only on lucky high-exploration LLM runs and silently fail otherwise. Diagnostic signature for this class: identical `toolsHash` + diverging turn counts across passing vs failing runs. Schedules must name the canonical exec by absolute path; untracked service-repo scripts become invisible to LLM at execution time, so git-track everything in `scripts/`. Pin [[x-posting-brittle-path]] for the cookie-refresh half of the system (Playwright + ~30d cookie life), [[forced-structured-output-via-mcp-tool]] for analogous fix shape (replace "LLM might do the right thing" with a deterministic mechanism). |
| 2026-05-24 (afternoon — recurring-blocker staleness fix) | (this commit) — `src/detection/recurring-blockers.ts` + `signals.ts` staleness lifecycle | **Jarvis morning ritual confidently recommended `docker build mission-control:latest` as highest-leverage; /diagnose proved the image already existed (built 2026-05-23 14:05) and all 3 surfaced clusters were stale.** Last failures 1d / 2d / 10d ago; the fixes (`d9f4d15`, `6020c61`) already shipped 2026-05-23. **Root cause**: `detectRecurringBlockers` had no staleness lifecycle — `resolved_at` existed but was never written (file comment L20-22 explicitly deferred). The judgment-prompt's `signalsOf` rendered only `summary` (no dates), so the LLM judge had zero temporal context and reached for the rebuild command quoted verbatim from CLAUDE.md "Invariants" — exact failure shape as `feedback_websearch_summary_hallucinations` (confident recommendation from under-grounded structured input). **3-part fix**: (a) auto-resolve UPDATE inside upsert tx, marks `resolution_signal='auto-stale'` when `last_seen_at < now-3d`; (b) UPSERT also clears `resolved_at = NULL, resolution_signal = NULL` so a genuine recurrence re-surfaces (audit C1 catch — without this clause the resolved state was a one-way trap); (c) `RecurringBlockerSignal` gained `lastSeenAt`, summary now ends `(last seen Nd ago)` so the LLM judge gets explicit temporal evidence even when the gate misses an edge case. **qa-audit caught C1 + W1 + W2 all folded same-bundle**: C1 (one-way trap on resolved clusters), W1 (`daysSince` ISO-Z double-Z corruption — narrowed parser to check `/Z$/` first), W2 (missing regression test for re-surface-on-new-failure — added explicit test that fails against pre-C1-fix code). **Tests +5** in `recurring-blockers.test.ts` (auto-resolve, no-resolve-with-fresh, no-re-surface-on-stale-rerun, RE-surface-on-new-failure, days-ago summary hint); judgment-prompt fixture got `lastSeenAt` field. Scoped suite green: 21/21 across 2 files; `--changed` green 49/49. **Live verification post-deploy** (PID 1951973): manual `detectRecurringBlockers()` invocation dropped the 10d-old timeout cluster (`6c0965bc` → `resolved_at=2026-05-24 16:21:21, resolution_signal=auto-stale`); the other 2 clusters correctly remain surfaced (1d/2d, <3d gate) with new days-ago hint and will auto-resolve in 1-2 days unless they re-fire. Tomorrow's 06:00 MX briefing should no longer treat them as urgent. **Pattern**: structured detection signals must carry temporal context to the LLM judge or the judge will confabulate fixes from documented runbooks — generalizes `feedback_orchestrator_owns_identity` to "orchestrator owns _temporal context_" too. |

| 2026-05-24 (/diagnose Fix P6 — budget soft-cap mode)              | (this commit) — new `BUDGET_ENFORCE` env, dispatcher gate now logs warn + falls through when soft-cap, /health exposes enforce/mode                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | **Fix P6 from /diagnose Phase 4 (budget gate inert despite exceeded windows).** Operator request: "soft cap to $400, non-enforcing". Pre-fix: `dispatcher.ts:308` had a single-flag (`budgetEnabled`) gate that either blocked tasks hard or did nothing — operator had set `BUDGET_ENABLED=false` to silence the hard blocks, but that also silenced visibility. /health showed `dailySpend $45 / $30 limit, exceeded:true` and `monthlySpend $576 / $400 limit, exceeded:true` for 24h+ with zero `task blocked: budget exceeded` log lines, confirming the gate was completely off. **Fix**: dual-flag design. (1) `config.ts:88-105` documents the new model: `budgetEnabled` = gate fires at all (default false, backward-compat); `budgetEnforce` = block-vs-warn switch (env `BUDGET_ENFORCE`, default false). Cost recording is unaffected by either flag. (2) `dispatcher.ts:325-394` reshapes the gate: when `budgetEnabled && isAnyWindowExceeded()`, compute the breaching window (hourly > daily > monthly precedence), then either log `info` + updateTaskStatus 'blocked' + return (if enforce) OR log `warn` "budget soft-cap exceeded (tracking only, task proceeds)" + fall through to dispatch. (3) `dispatcher.ts:30-31` adds per-window 60s warn cooldown so an operator over budget all month doesn't get 60-100 warn lines/hour burying other warnings (auditor W2). (4) `health.ts:86,102-114` exposes `enabled`, `enforce`, `mode` ("enforce" | "soft-cap" | "disabled") on the budget payload — `enforce` is normalized to `enabled && enforce` so impossible combos (BUDGET_ENFORCE=true with BUDGET_ENABLED=false) report `enforce:false, mode:"disabled"` consistently (auditor W3). **qa-audit caught C1+W2+W3+W4+R2 same-bundle, all folded**: **C1** = original JSDoc claimed the gate "emits a `task.budget_exceeded` event" but the dispatcher only logs (no event bus call); fixed by dropping the false clause from JSDoc (alternative — actually emitting the event — was deferred as not in operator request); **W2** = warn rate-limit (above); **W3** = /health enforce normalization (above); **W4** = soft-cap test only verified "log called + no block" which would pass even for a buggy "log then return without dispatching" implementation; fixed by capturing `runnerExecute` as top-level mock and adding positive assertion `expect(runnerExecute).toHaveBeenCalled()` to soft-cap/disabled/no-breach paths and `not.toHaveBeenCalled()` to enforce path; **R2** = same pattern, paired with W4. **W1 (no hard upper bound on soft-cap)** = operator-policy call; operator's "non-enforcing" was explicit, no implicit ceiling added. **W5 (dummy registerRunner global mutation)** = low priority per auditor, documented in test comment. **SV1 (mc_budget_soft_breach_total Prometheus counter)** = deferred, out of operator-request scope; can add later when alerting becomes necessary. **R1 (drainAsync timing fragility)** = documented as "assumes the gate runs within 2 microtask ticks"; replace with awaitable hook if dispatcher gains pre-gate awaits in future. **Tests +5** in new `src/dispatch/dispatcher-budget-gate.test.ts` (disabled / soft-cap / enforce / no-breach / multi-window-precedence); uses `vi.hoisted()` for shared mock state, `drainAsync()` helper for the fire-and-forget `dispatchTask`. Scoped suite green: 20/20 across affected files; broader 184/184 across `src/dispatch`, `src/api`, `src/budget`. **Live verification post-deploy**: PID 1266392 (was 1221558), /health 200 in 28ms, budget object now includes `enabled:false, enforce:false, mode:"disabled"` — operator must set `BUDGET_ENABLED=true` in `.env` and restart to activate the soft cap mode (BUDGET_ENFORCE defaults to false = soft cap; set BUDGET_ENFORCE=true to revert to hard enforce). **Watch points**: (a) after operator sets `BUDGET_ENABLED=true`, `/health.budget.mode` flips to `"soft-cap"`; warn lines fire at most once per window per 60s; tasks continue to dispatch; (b) if operator later wants hard enforcement, single env flip `BUDGET_ENFORCE=true` + restart reverts to the pre-2026-05-24 behavior; (c) when current monthly spend resets at month boundary (2026-06-01 MX), the soft-cap warns will stop until the next breach. Memory: `feedback_budget_soft_cap_2026_05_24.md` (warm-tier) — 4 connective lessons: (a) "single-flag gate" coupling is brittle — `budgetEnabled` was overloaded for both "track" and "block", forcing operators to either accept blocks or lose visibility entirely; dual-flag design lets each axis be tuned independently; (b) when the operator asks for "soft cap", the design must include observability — log + /health surface + (ideally) Prometheus, not just "don't block"; (c) rate-limit defensive logs proactively when the breach can persist for days (60s cooldown beats unbounded spam); (d) tests for fire-and-forget async paths need positive assertions on the dispatched effect (runner called or not called), not just absence of the failure mode they're avoiding. |
| 2026-05-24 (/diagnose Fix P3 — task-retry field forwarding) | (this commit) — reactions manager preserves agentType + tools + ritualId on retry; dispatcher persists tools/ritualId when no tags; swarm-runner retry forwards agentType | **Fix P3 from /diagnose Phase 4 (skill-evolution ritual back-to-back failures).** Re-diagnose corrected the original `/diagnose` write-off (which deferred P3 as "single occurrence 2026-05-20"). DB query showed the failure is recurring: 8 of the last 13 days had `Skill evolution — <date>` failures, always in PAIRS — first a heavy attempt, then a nanoclaw second attempt spawned ~5min later. Same shape for 2026-05-12, -13, -14, -15, -20, -21. **Root cause traced**: `reactions/manager.ts` `handleTaskFailed` is the path that spawns the second submission via `retry` / `retry_adjusted` action paths. Both paths dropped `agentType`, `tools`, and `ritualId` from the original failed task — the retry submission then went through the classifier with no hints, and the heuristic ("long description (220 words, +4); fast+flash negative 78% → tier upgrade (+1)") picked nanoclaw. Nanoclaw structurally can't run the skill-evolution workload (5min container cap + occasional docker-image gaps). **Compounding bug**: `dispatcher.ts:256-258` only persisted `metadata` when `submission.tags` was truthy, so ritual submissions like `createEvolutionRitual` (which have `tools` but no `tags`) had `metadata=NULL` at INSERT time — even with the reactions-manager forwarding fix, the tools list was already gone from the DB by retry time. **Three-layer fix shipped**: (1) `dispatcher.ts` persists metadata when ANY of `tags                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | tools                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |     | ritualId`is set, with conditional spreads so each field appears only if defined; (2)`reactions/manager.ts` `retry`+`retry_adjusted`both extract`parseTaskMeta(taskId, task.metadata)`and forward`agentType: task.agent_type ?? undefined`, `tools: meta.tools`, `ritualId: meta.ritualId`, `tags: meta.tags`; (3) `swarm-runner.ts:225`swarm-retry path forwards`failedTask.agent_type`for the same anti-pattern at a sibling call site (auditor W1 fold — same bug class, same forwarding gap). **Companion fix to yesterday's`ef5b04e`**: yesterday added `ritualContext`ALS exemption at the dispatcher's runner.execute() seam; that fix ONLY covered cron-direct ritual submissions. Reaction-retried rituals lost their`ritualId`because it was never persisted to TaskRow, so the exemption never fired on retries. This fix closes the gap end-to-end. **qa-audit caught 4 Warnings, all folded same-bundle**: W1 = swarm-runner.ts:225 had the identical anti-pattern (sub-task retry drops agentType) — fixed; W2 = added inline note at dispatcher.ts:504`\_isRequiredToolRetry`reminding future refactors to preserve ritualId when changing the`{...submission}`spread to explicit fields; W3 = extracted dense IIFE to module-scoped`parseTaskMeta()`helper, eliminating eager parse work on non-retry decisions (suppress/escalate); W4 = added`console.warn`on malformed metadata so corruption surfaces in journalctl (otherwise the silent`{}`fallback would let a corrupted ritual cascade right back to nanoclaw — the failure mode this fix exists to prevent). **R3 deferred** (zod-validate metadata shape against TS interface): low-likelihood (only triggers on deploy-time bad write); defer until a real corruption is observed. **Tests +9** (3 dispatcher + 3 reactions + new mock task shapes): dispatcher persists tools-without-tags, persists ritualId, leaves null when none set; reactions retry + retry_adjusted forward all 3 fields for ritual mock; retry of non-ritual with null metadata doesn't crash. Scoped suite green: 82/82 across 4 affected files (dispatcher + reactions + scheduler + flailing-guard); 299/299 across 12 files touching TaskSubmission/submitTask. **Live verification post-deploy**: PID 1221558 (was 1173802), /health ok db+inference ok in 28ms. **Watch points**: (a) next 23:00 MX skill-evolution ritual (today, 2026-05-24) — if heavy planner JSON-shape fails again, the reactions retry should now spawn a SECOND heavy attempt (not nanoclaw); verify in`tasks`table via`SELECT title, agent_type, status FROM tasks WHERE title LIKE 'Skill evolution — 2026-05-24' ORDER BY created_at`; (b) historical recovery — the heavy planner's "missing 'goals' array" is stochastic (succeeded 2026-05-22 + -23 with the same prompt), so a second heavy attempt should win >50% of the time; if it doesn't, the planner needs separate hardening (separate fix); (c) yesterday's `ritualContext`flailing-guard exemption now extends to reaction-retried rituals as a positive side-effect — same shape ALS wrap fires because`submission.ritualId`flows from forwarded metadata → dispatcher`dispatchWithSlot`→ ALS wrap → runner. Memory:`feedback_ritual_retry_field_forwarding_2026_05_24.md`(warm-tier) — 4 connective lessons: (a)`/diagnose` Phase 4 deferral can be wrong — single-occurrence assumption was incorrect (had I queried the DB instead of the journal, the 8-of-13-day pattern would have been visible immediately); (b) compounded bugs — TWO layers had to both fix for the cascade to close (the dispatcher metadata persistence AND the reactions-manager forwarding); (c) "fixing seam X without grep-sweep for siblings" — qa-audit W1 caught the identical anti-pattern at swarm-runner.ts:225 in the same audit pass, folded same-bundle per CLAUDE.md bug-fix discipline; (d) corruption-on-silent-fallback rule — when a fallback exists for parse/lookup failures, log the failure or it becomes invisible to operators (W4 fix). |
| 2026-05-24 (/diagnose Fix 1) | (this commit) — flailing-guard ritual exemption via AsyncLocalStorage | **Fix 1 from /diagnose Phase 4 (P1+P2 evolution-log misattribution).** Operator opened session asking about EVOLUTION-LOG warnings recurring 2026-05-22 + 2026-05-23 ("API unreachable at log-writing time"). Structured `/diagnose` enumerated 7 problems and confirmed 3 root causes. **Root cause for P1+P2**: the 23:59 MX evolution-log ritual runs ~15 sequential `mc-ctl db "SELECT ..."` and ~3 sequential `curl http://localhost:8080/api/...` calls assembling the daily entry. The token-based flailing-guard correctly flagged the repetition (legitimate signal at tokens `select` and `localhost` hitting 3-strikes), short-circuiting the ritual mid-flight. Jarvis then misattributed the blocked tool calls to network failure, writing "API unreachable" in the log even though `curl /health` was responding in 43ms. **Architecture**: per-task ALS at the dispatcher's `runner.execute()` seam (single wrap point, not per-tool — addresses the original `flailing-guard.ts` author's "heavy plumbing" objection by locating the wrap at the dispatcher boundary). New `ritualContext` (AsyncLocalStorage) in `src/tools/flailing-guard.ts`; `checkFlailing` + `recordCall` both short-circuit when `isInRitualContext()`. New `ritualId?: string` on `TaskSubmission` (`src/dispatch/dispatcher.ts`); `dispatchWithSlot` wraps `runner.execute(input)` in `ritualContext.run({ ritualId }, ...)` when set. `src/rituals/scheduler.ts` `executeRitual` tags `template.ritualId = ritual.id` before submit. The exemption is symmetric (both check AND record bypass): recording a ritual's failing-but-retried tool calls would slowly poison the 10-entry / 5-min buffer for non-ritual traffic that follows. Sub-task ALS inheritance is intentional for the current ritual set (all `fast` agentType, no swarm dispatch) and documented in the head comment + JSDoc; non-applicable to the swarm/batch path until a ritual is rewritten to spawn sub-tasks. **Companion prompt fix**: `src/rituals/evolution-log.ts` adds a "How to describe data-collection problems" section forbidding "API unreachable" language without `curl -sS -m 5 http://localhost:8080/health` returning non-2xx — explicitly distinguishes (a) healthy+non-empty, (b) healthy+empty, (c) actually unreachable. The pattern recurred for 3 evolution-log entries (2026-05-19, -22, -23) and would have re-recurred at the next 23:59 MX run without this prompt fix even with the guard fixed (Jarvis was being trained by his own prior bad log entries). **qa-audit caught C1 + C2 same-bundle, both folded**: **C1** = sub-task inheritance silently bypasses the guard for any swarm/batch child of a ritual — auditor recommended option (i) document + test (current ritual set doesn't fire it). Folded by adding the inheritance paragraph to the head comment + JSDoc, plus a regression test pinning "nested ritualContext.run preserves outer scope" invariant. **C2** = my "buffer not polluted" test was false-passing — used `exitCode=0` records inside the ritual, but `checkFlailing` skips `exitCode===0` records when counting strikes regardless of context, so the test would pass with or without the exemption. Folded by switching to `exitCode=1` (the realistic case: flaky `memory_reflect` or transient SQLite lock fails during ritual). **W1+W2** also folded: added test for fire-and-forget async propagation (mirrors the `dispatchTask(...).catch(...)` pattern at dispatcher.ts:275) + test for throw-leakage (verify `isInRitualContext()` returns false after `ritualContext.run` throws). **W3+S1+W5** style cleanups: snake_case → inline ternary; field renamed `ritual` → `ritualId` for parallelism with `parentTaskId`/`taskId`/`runId`. **W4+W6 deferred** with rationale (would require all 11 ritual factories taking ritualId as constructor arg; circular import risk for typed union). **Tests +8** in `src/tools/flailing-guard.test.ts` (isInRitualContext outside/inside; checkFlailing returns null inside; recordCall no-op for failing calls inside; no-leakage-after-return; async propagation through await boundary; fire-and-forget propagation; throw unwind; nested run preserves outer scope). Scoped suite green: 70/70 across 3 affected files (flailing-guard + dispatcher + scheduler); 247/247 across 10 files touching changed code. **Live verification post-deploy**: PID 1173802 (was 300619), /health ok db+inference ok in 32ms, ritual scheduler restarted with 7 rituals + 7 mechanical jobs + 4 triggers, evolution-log scheduled (59 23 \* \* \*, tz=America/Mexico_City). **Watch points**: (a) next 23:59 MX evolution-log run — verify absence of `[shell-guard] FLAILING` lines during the ritual task in journal AND the new entry does NOT contain "API unreachable" language unless `/health` actually failed; (b) yesterday's `mc_swarm_subtask_retry_total` counter (#231 shadow) + `recall_coherence_suppression_rate` drift signal (#227) both still active. **Diagnose deferred items not in this fix**: P3 skill-evolution failure (single occurrence 2026-05-20, no recurrence — defer); P5 `agentic-crm.service` masked (verified operator-intentional 2026-05-20 19:46 emergency mask after Docker control-group cleanup failed, not Jarvis-accidental — no action); P6 budget exceeded ($45.18/$30 daily, $576/$400 monthly with `task blocked: budget exceeded` log line absent in last 24h — either `BUDGET_ENABLED=false` in env or `isAnyWindowExceeded()` reads stale; operator-decision pending). Memory: `feedback_evolution_log_misattribution_2026_05_24.md` (warm-tier) — 4 connective lessons: (a) flailing-guard signal/noise inverts for rituals (legitimate repetition vs LLM flail); (b) "fix at the dispatcher seam, not per-tool" — single ALS wrap-point avoids the original author's "heavy plumbing" objection; (c) prompt rules carry as much weight as code for misattribution failures; (d) symmetric exemption (skip both check AND record) — one-sided exemption would poison the buffer for non-ritual traffic. |
| 2026-05-23 (session wrap) | (this commit) — docs wrap row + session memory | **Session 2026-05-23 closing summary** — 5 ROI-ranked queue items shipped in 3 commits, all PASS-WITH-WARNINGS audits, all warnings folded same-bundle: `eff1f23` (#224 cache split + #225 phantom-$0 cost-ledger), `3d7bb68` (#227 Conway-P3 drift signal activation + #228 Haiku-downshift plumbing), `410ef5b` (#231 swarm per-sub-task retry policy in shadow mode). **Tests today**: 5710 → 5779 (+69 across the 3 ships). **Pre-commit sweeps**: 3 full-suite runs, all green. **Deploy cadence**: 3 deploys, each ~2s newPid transition, /health ok db+inference ok at every checkpoint. **Audit folds same-bundle**: 12 findings caught and folded (3 Critical from wrapper-vs-direct-seam bypass + side-effect-taint logic gap; 4 Warnings; 5 Recommendations / SVs). 3 Warnings deferred with rationale. **Pattern most heavily reinforced today** (cross-shipped 3×): **wrapper-vs-direct seam bypass** — every "shared utility" fix needs an explicit audit-check of every direct-call site that bypasses the wrapper. Caught at `eff1f23` (fast-runner direct-call built its own systemPrompt + packed actualCostUsd unconditionally — bypassed both #224 and #225 wrappers), reinforced at `410ef5b` (taint-check logic gap on `destructiveHint:false` reversible-but-non-idempotent tools that would have passed through as retry-safe). Sister patterns adopted today (cross-shipped 2-3×): **env-direct flag pattern** (not Config singleton) for plumbing flags (AUX_HAIKU_ENABLED, SWARM_SUBTASK_RETRY_ENABLED both use `process.env.X === "true"` literal — same idiom as `heavyRunnerContainerized`, avoids `loadConfig()` test-env trap); **shadow ship + classifier-runs-always** for cost/reversibility-implicating changes (#228 plumbing default OFF; #231 classifier runs regardless of flag for observability); **honest JSDoc beats aspirational** (3 separate JSDoc-accuracy folds today: aux-haiku referencing non-existent config field, shadow-A/B-harness claim without infrastructure, taint check claiming destructive-required veto when actual logic was different). **Memory new this session (warm-tier, NOT indexed per retention rule)**: `feedback_sdk_cache_and_phantom_cost_2026_05_23.md` (5 lessons), `feedback_drift_signal_activation_and_aux_haiku_plumbing_2026_05_23.md` (4 lessons), `feedback_swarm_subtask_retry_policy_2026_05_23.md` (4 lessons), `feedback_session_2026_05_23_5item_ship_arc.md` (5 lessons, this wrap). **Tasks closed**: #224, #225, #227, #228, #231. **Tasks remaining**: #226 (AV key rotation, P0, 1-2h, no blockers — natural next pickup), #229 (NorthStar compass-reframe, blocked on operator), #230 (S3-C1 WhatsApp disconnects rate, blocked on evaluator infra), #232 (honor retry-after, telemetry-gated), #233 (P3 hygiene long-tail). **Watch points active**: (a) 2026-05-24 ~00:00 UTC first `fast` traffic post-deploy — `cost_ledger.cache_creation_tokens` should drift down as cache prefix hits cross-task (#224 lever); (b) 2026-05-24 05:03 MX daily nanoclaw ritual — first live test of #224 cache fix + #225 cost gating through one task; (c) 2026-05-25 — `mc-ctl audit-claim cache-hit --window=24h` to verify 70%+ hit-rate on full 6-section assembly (baseline 42.4% from T-02); (d) within 7 days — drift signal `recall_coherence_suppression_rate` first weekly evaluator tick (#227, should stay at 0.0 well below 0.08 trip); (e) **2026-05-30 — 1-week shadow window for #231 closes**, acceptance-criteria check (shadow retry rate <15% AND provider_transient >50% of would-retries AND zero false-positive `skipped_side_effect` rows); (f) daily — `cost_ledger` grep for new phantom-$0 rows (should stay at 5 historical + 0 new per #225). **Repo end state**: 1 branch (main), 0 stashes, untracked files limited to pre-existing X-posting scratch + tracker + agent-memory (none mine). Service active PID 300619, /health ok, 255 tools, drift signal `enabled=1 baseline=0.0` live, `mc_swarm_subtask_retry_total` counter registered on `:8080/metrics`. |

| 2026-05-23 (very late) | (this commit) — swarm per-sub-task retry policy (shadow mode) | **Queue #231 closure.** Bounded per-sub-task retry for the swarm runner — Hermes v0.13 shape adopted with our annotations. Currently swarm sub-task failures cascade to dependents; transient flake on goal-3-of-5 wastes goals 1+2 and cascades 4+5. **Locked design from 4-question dialogue with operator**: Q1 policy at swarm-level + counter primitive at dispatcher-level; Q2 use existing `Tool.idempotentHint`/`destructiveHint`/`readOnlyHint`; Q3 shared budget (`tasks.retry_count` single counter, dispatcher's `_isRequiredToolRetry` increments it); Q4 `tasks.retry_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0` column. **Plus 4 operator follow-ups**: MAX_RETRIES_PER_GOAL=1, bundle hallucination recovery (Option A wording: "⚠️ IMPORTANT: The previous attempt at this sub-task failed because the response narrated tool calls without actually invoking them. You MUST call the tools you reference. Do not describe results — produce them."), ship in shadow mode (env-flag `SWARM_SUBTASK_RETRY_ENABLED=false` default), leave `_isRequiredToolRetry` flag in place. **Architecture**: new `src/runners/swarm-retry-policy.ts` pure-classifier module with 3 veto gates in cheapest-first order (budget cap → failure class via regex matchers → side-effect taint via tool-annotation lookup). Failure-class taxonomy: provider_transient (429/5xx/timeout/ECONNRESET/circuit-open) + hallucination → RETRY; tool_error / max_rounds / [timeout after Ns] / needs_context / cancelled / unknown_failure → terminal. **Swarm wiring**: `syncSubTaskStatuses` gains optional 4th `retryContext` (backward-compat preserved). When provided, the `failed` branch calls new `attemptSubtaskRetry` helper which classifies, records Prometheus counter `mc_swarm_subtask_retry_total{decision, reason, recovery_mode}`, logs structured `[swarm-retry]` line, and (when env flag is literal `"true"`) re-spawns via `submitTask` with `retryCount: predecessor+1` + tracker reset to pending + goalTaskMap rewired. Shadow mode: classifier still runs and logs the would-decision (`decision: "shadow_skipped"`), goal marked FAILED as before. **Dispatcher**: `TaskRow.retry_count`, `TaskSubmission.retryCount?`, INSERT plumbs the new column, `_isRequiredToolRetry` path passes `retryCount: 1`, new `runs.tool_calls TEXT` column populated from `result.toolCalls` (default `"[]"`), new `getRunToolCalls(taskId)` helper. **qa-audit caught C1 + SV1 + W1 + W3 same-bundle, all folded**: **C1 (CRITICAL, pre-activation)** — `findSideEffectTaint` originally required `destructiveHint:true` as a third clause, but three production tools (`jarvis_file_update` appends accumulate, `jarvis_file_move` second-call ENOENT, `submit_report` duplicate row) have `readOnly:false, idempotent:false, destructive:false` (reversible-but-non-idempotent) and would have passed through as retry-safe. Fix: drop the destructive requirement; veto is now `NOT readOnly AND NOT idempotent`. 3 regression tests pin the three production tools by name. **MUST land before SWARM_SUBTASK_RETRY_ENABLED=true is flipped** otherwise `jarvis_file_update` double-writes are the first thing the operator sees. Caught in shadow → no production impact. **SV1**: JSDoc said "most specific first"; actual order is cheapest-first. Reworded. **W1**: Sanitization sentinel collapsed unknown labels to real buckets (typo `skipped_taint` would have bucketed as `skipped_terminal`); fix uses distinguished `unknown_label` sentinel + 4 prometheus.test.ts pins. **W3**: Shadow rows lost recovery_mode info (always "none" when env flag off, even though classifier said retry-plain/retry-hallucination); fix preserves recoveryMode in shadow rows. **W2 (concurrent storm bypasses MAX_CONCURRENT_SUBTASKS), W4 (.catch path test), W5 (retry-of-retry integration test) DEFERRED** with rationale in `docs/planning/swarm-retry.md` under "qa-audit folds". **Tests +45** (32 classifier + 8 swarm integration + 5 prometheus sentinel). **Live verification post-deploy**: PID 300619 (was 178114), /health ok db+inference ok, counter `mc_swarm_subtask_retry_total` registered on `:8080/metrics`. **Acceptance criteria** for flipping shadow→enabled at 2026-05-30: shadow retry rate <15% of swarm sub-tasks AND provider_transient class >50% of would-retries AND zero `skipped_side_effect` false positives in rationale logs. Memory: `feedback_swarm_subtask_retry_policy_2026_05_23.md` (warm-tier) — 4 connective lessons: (a) cheapest-first veto order is a perf decision, not a "most specific" decision — document the actual axis; (b) `destructiveHint:false` does NOT mean "safe to replay" — verified C1 finds via production grep; (c) shadow ship + classifier-runs-always is the right shape for a behavior change with cost implications — collects the would-decision distribution before lifting; (d) ALTER TABLE + schema.sql parity discipline. |

| 2026-05-23 (late night) | (this commit) — drift signal activation + Haiku-downshift plumbing | **Two queue items bundled — ROI-#4 (#227) + ROI-#5 (#228) from today's to-do list.** **(#227 — S6-recall-audit-dormant closure)** `recall_audit` infrastructure was dormant for the drift-signal half since 2026-05-10 (HINDSIGHT_ENABLED=false flipped logRecall off-path), but V8.1 Phase A wired `logRecall + applyOutcomeBias` into `SqliteMemoryBackend` on 2026-05-20. 14 days of fresh `source='sqlite-primary'` rows (213 over last 7d, all `mode='coherence'`, 0 excluded / 980 results = 0.0 baseline) are now in the audit table. Activated the Conway-Pattern-3 drift signal `recall_coherence_suppression_rate` (Spine 6 seed): live `drift_signals` UPDATE (`enabled=1`, `baseline_value_json='{"value":0.0}'`, swapped `baseline_query` from `awaiting:recall-audit-active` sentinel to the activation SQL `SELECT CAST(SUM(excluded_count) AS REAL) / NULLIF(SUM(excluded_count + result_count), 0) FROM recall_audit WHERE created_at > datetime('now','-7 days') AND (mode = 'coherence' OR mode IS NULL)`); seed file `src/lib/s3/seed-signals.ts` updated to match so fresh DB seeds also produce the activated state. Trip threshold stays at the documented absolute 0.08 (Conway coherence-drift watchpoint). Cadence weekly; first eval cycle after deploy. **(#228 — Haiku-downshift plumbing)** Aux LLM callers (scope-classifier on EVERY incoming message; prompt-enhancer 1-2× per enhance flow) hardcoded Sonnet for ~$3/M-input vs Haiku ~$0.40/M-input — 5-8× cost lever on hot paths. Shipped PLUMBING with default OFF: new `model?: string` field on `InferenceRequest` + `InferWithToolsOptions`; `inferViaClaudeSdk` + `inferWithToolsViaClaudeSdk` now honor `request.model ?? SONNET_MODEL_ID` (retry leg still uses Haiku regardless — when primary IS already Haiku the caller's downshift intent is preserved, no Sonnet escalation). Updated JSDoc at adapter.ts:803-820 (was `request.model is NOT honored` warning). **Aux call-site annotations**: `scope-classifier.ts:107`, `prompt-enhancer.ts:265,407` (3 sites) — all behind `process.env.AUX_HAIKU_ENABLED === "true"` flag, default off. Direct env-read (not `getConfig()`) — same idiom as `heavyRunnerContainerized` and avoids the `loadConfig()`-throws-on-missing-MC_API_KEY-in-test-env trap that broke an earlier draft of this fix (6 test failures cleared by switching from `getConfig()` to env-direct). Lazy dynamic import of `HAIKU_MODEL_ID` only fires when the flag IS true (zero import overhead in steady state). **qa-audit caught W1 + R1 + R2 same-bundle**: W1 = JSDoc still referenced the non-existent `config.auxHaikuEnabled` field after the env-direct refactor (drift between rework and doc), folded by updating adapter.ts:130 + 823. R1 = JSDoc claimed "1-day shadow A/B clears 95% match" without a shadow harness existing in the codebase, folded by reframing the JSDoc to "operator-flipped after manual validation" + queueing the shadow harness as future work. R2 = "~33 callers" / "~35 callers" inconsistency, folded by deleting the count from the JSDoc. **W3 deferred** (env-flag gate untested at call sites) — auditor acknowledged it's not a precedent break (matches the untested `heavyRunnerContainerized` pattern), and adding the test means non-trivial mock setup in `scope-classifier.test.ts` which today tests only `parseScopeGroups`. Tracked as a follow-up. **W2 acknowledged** (community-reply-gate.ts is another structurally-similar aux candidate not in the original 3-site scope) — Tier-2 downshift candidate for next pass. **Tests +6** (5 in `adapter-claude-sdk-routing.test.ts` for the model-plumbing contract + 1 amended in `seed-signals.test.ts` flipping the assertion from `enabled=0` to `enabled=1` + adding the new `baseline_query` regex check). Full pre-commit suite green. **Live verification post-deploy**: PID 178114 (was 88896), /health ok db+inference ok, 255 tools loaded. Drift signal queried live: `enabled=1, baseline=0.0` confirmed. **Watch points**: (1) next weekly drift-signal evaluator tick — recall_coherence_suppression_rate should report 0.0 and stay below 0.08 trip threshold; (2) operator decides when to flip `AUX_HAIKU_ENABLED=true` after manual A/B against scope-classifier output (Set<string> equality) and prompt-enhancer CIRICD JSON match — every percentage-point match means real $/mo savings on the hottest aux line. Memory: `feedback_drift_signal_activation_and_aux_haiku_plumbing_2026_05_23.md` (warm-tier) — 4 connective lessons: (a) "dormant + waiting" signals are operational debt with a deterministic closing condition; (b) env-direct flag pattern is the safe default for plumbing flips that aren't worth a Config singleton roundtrip; (c) seed file as drift-tolerant source of truth (idempotent INSERT-IF-MISSING means live drift is preserved while fresh DBs reseed to canonical state); (d) honest JSDoc beats aspirational JSDoc — don't claim a shadow-A/B harness exists when the actual gate is operator judgment. | 2026-05-23 (night)          | (this commit) — flattenMessagesForSdk cache split + costAuthoritative for abort-path cost-ledger                                                                                                               | **Two bundled fixes ROI-#1 + ROI-#2 from today's to-do list.** Both target `src/inference/claude-sdk.ts` and share one deploy. **(#224 cache fix)** Root cause located 2026-05-22 in `cache_diag` (commit f219340): fast-runner painstakingly splits prompt into 6 sections (`essentials`, `kbResult.stable`, `variableDescPart`, `kbResult.variable`, `precedent`, `deferredCatalog`) but `flattenMessagesForSdk` (claude-sdk.ts:698) concatenated all `role:"system"` messages into one `systemPrompt` blob, and the SDK places exactly ONE `cache_control` marker at the end (verified against `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` sdk.d.ts:1472 — `systemPrompt: string                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | { type: 'preset', ... }`, no multi-block surface). Any byte-drift anywhere in 71K bytes → full cache miss. **Fix**: new `ChatMessage.cacheable?: boolean`field.`cacheable === false`system messages route to a user-prompt PREFIX (no cache marker, byte-drift stays local).`flattenMessagesForSdk`+ the inline fast-runner direct extractor at`fast-runner.ts:1108-1126`both honor the hint — shared via new exported`splitSystemMessagesByCache(messages)`helper. Fast-runner tags`variableDescPart`, `kbResult.variable`, `precedent`with`cacheable: false`; stable sections (`essentials`, `kbResult.stable`/`kb`, `deferredCatalog`) stay defaulted. **Expected savings**: 30-40K cache_read tokens per heavy task; targets the $754/mo `fast`Sonnet line (T-02 baseline corrected this to ~$495/mo on day-1 of Sprint 1, but the leak class is the same). **(#225 phantom-$0 cost fix)** 5 phantom-$0 rows over 14d on`fast`Sonnet (IDs 4925, 5101-5103, 5493 in cost_ledger), all matching the 2026-05-23 07:41 UTC symptom: progress lines report`51 turns, 35 tool calls`but the`Completed:`line writes`$0.00 / tokens=0 [TIMED OUT]`. Root cause: claude-sdk.ts catch block (line ~620) preserves partial `streamingText`but never updates`usage`/`costUsd`/`durationMs`— they stay at init zeros, dispatcher's`costUsdOverride: 0`spread overrides calculateCost() with phantom $0. **Fix**: (a) per-turn`usage`accumulator in the`assistant`-message branch reads `message.message.usage`(per-call Anthropic Messages API totals); summed across turns, matches`result.usage`when terminal fires; (b) new`ClaudeSdkResult.costAuthoritative: boolean` flag set true ONLY in success/error subtype branches (where a terminal result message fired) — Max-plan legitimate $0 stays attributable; (c) adapter wrappers (`queryClaudeSdkAsInfer`, `queryClaudeSdkAsInferWithTools`) AND fast-runner direct call all gate `cost_usd`/`actualCostUsd`emission on`costAuthoritative`so abort paths fall back to`calculateCost()`over the now-non-zero accumulator. **qa-audit caught both C1 + C2 in the fast-runner direct-call path** (fast-runner builds its own systemPrompt at line 1108 and packs`actualCostUsd`unconditionally at line 1239 — bypasses both fixes); folded same-bundle by extracting`splitSystemMessagesByCache`+ gating`actualCostUsd`on`costAuthoritative`+ 7 new unit tests on the helper. **W1 fold**: added thrown-mock test exercising real`catch (err)`branch (prior tests covered natural-exit only). **W2 fold**: JSDoc documents the`cacheable:false`ordering precondition (all variable system messages land at userPrompt head regardless of original position; intentional for current call sites, callers needing mid-conversation injection should emit as`role:"user"`). **W3 cite-fragility**: kept the SDK-source citation but the line number stays; failure mode would just be `grep`re-finding`systemPrompt?: string`in a future SDK version. **Tests +15** (4 #224 cache routing + 5 #225 abort-cost + 1 W1 catch-branch + 7 splitSystemMessagesByCache extraction-helper unit tests). **Live verification post-deploy**: PID 88896 (was 3942177), /health ok db+inference ok, 255 tools loaded. **Daily budget at $44.97 on $30 cap when deployed** — exactly the symptom #225's phantom-rows could explain (silent uncategorized real-spend going through as $0); the fix doesn't recover the lost attribution on old rows but stops the leak going forward. Next 24h: watch for non-zero`cost_usd`on any abort-class run + watch`cost_ledger.cache_creation_tokens`on`fast`rows to drop relative to baseline as the cache prefix starts hitting cross-task. 2026-05-24 05:03 MX nanoclaw daily ritual will be the first live test of both fixes through one task. Feedback memory`feedback_sdk_cache_and_phantom_cost_2026_05_23.md`ties together 5 lessons: (1) wrapper-vs-direct seam pattern — every "shared utility" fix needs a matching audit of every direct-call site that bypasses the wrapper (C1+C2 were caught only because the auditor explicitly checked the direct path); (2) sentinel-as-value (cost_usd=0 doubling as "Max-plan $0" AND "no data") forces a dedicated authoritative-flag; (3) SDK surface verification before designing fix (Option 2 multi-block was non-viable per sdk.d.ts:1472 — would have wasted hours); (4) test the catch path with a real throw, not just iteration-exit (W1); (5) variable content placement at user-prompt prefix is "intentional reorder" for current call sites, document the precondition (W2). |

Confirmed: `parsePayload`extract behavior-preserving for ALL production cases; single source for`"Container timed out after"`substring (no upstream wrapping that could false-positive the circuit-breaker); heartbeat`clearInterval`ordering safe;`NANOCLAW_TIMEOUT_MS` env name consistent. 3 Info/Recommendation findings surfaced; folded the only test-coverage one (`3e922bc`— bare`{type:"progress"}`without`elapsedMs`must still be recognized as progress, qa-r2 W3). Only unique uncommitted content found was `scripts/backup-db.sh`retention`+7 → +3`policy delta in stash@{2}; operator chose to keep 7-day retention. **All 7 deleted refs (4 branches + 3 stashes) have SHAs saved for 30d reflog recovery if anything turns out unexpectedly load-bearing.** **Repo end state**: 1 branch (main), 0 stashes, 0 modified files of mine, X-posting scratch + tracker untracked as pre-existing (not mine). Branch-switch ritual no longer needed for future commits. **Tests 5694 → 5710** (+16: 1 from`3e922bc`qa-r2 W3 fold + 15 from Jarvis's Rumi-SOP tests). **Live production**: PID 3942177 active, /health ok, no service restart (test + docs commits only). Session-wrap memory`feedback_session_2026_05_23_substrate_arc.md`ties together the all-day arc (morning image fix → masked timeout class unmasked → R2 catch the parser-test gap → Jarvis WIP rescue → hygiene). | 2026-05-23 (late afternoon) |`6020c61`— nanoclaw 300s timeout fix + heartbeat sentinels + circuit-breaker extension                                                                                                                        | **Second nanoclaw blocker resolved same day — distinct from the image-prune fix earlier today.** Morning ritual flagged 5 timeouts on 2026-05-14 (3 of them self-referential "Investigate recurring failure: 'Container timed ou...'" tasks — same deadlock class as image-missing). The 2026-05-14 cluster was the original substrate failure; the image-prune from 2026-05-15 onward masked it for 9 days by failing tasks at spawn-time BEFORE they could time out. Today's image fix unmasked the latent timeout — verified imminent recurrence at tomorrow 05:03 MX Skill-evolution run had I not addressed it. **Root cause**:`nanoclaw-runner.ts:24`hardcoded`CONTAINER_TIMEOUT_MS = 300_000`for a workload (orchestrate() Plan-Execute-Reflect loop) that heavy-runner runs successfully at 400-870s (and uses 900s timeout). nanoclaw and heavy run the SAME orchestrate() workload but nanoclaw was sized 1/3. **Structural compounding**: container.ts's "activity-aware" timer comment was misleading — workers only emitted ONE sentinel pair (at completion), so resetTimer fired exactly once, making the timer a wall-clock cap in practice. **Three-layer fix (A+B+C)**: **(A)** new`nanoclawTimeoutMs`config field (env`NANOCLAW_TIMEOUT_MS`, default 900_000 = matches heavy); hardcoded constant deleted; nanoclaw-runner reads `config.nanoclawTimeoutMs`. **(B)** 60s heartbeat in both `nanoclaw-worker.ts`AND`heavy-worker.ts`(symmetry with HEAVY_RUNNER_CONTAINERIZED dormant path) —`setInterval`emits`{type:"progress", elapsedMs}`sentinel pair;`clearInterval`on both success+error paths;`heartbeat.unref()`so the timer doesn't block process exit; final result objects gain explicit`type:"result"`discriminator. **(C)** container.ts host-side parser now has`parsePayload()`pure helper (extracted from inline closure per qa-audit W1) that returns`{kind:"progress" | "result"}`; closure delegates and applies imperative state. Backward compat: untyped payloads fall through to result branch (older worker code keeps working). **(D)** `autonomous-improvement.ts`circuit-breaker matcher extended from image-missing-only to ALSO match`"Container timed out after"`(the exact`container.ts`template) — prevents tomorrow's predicted 3-task self-referential deadlock from forming. Reason string + tests updated. **Audit PASS WITH WARNINGS, 0 Critical**: W1 (parser test gap, critical-adjacent — FOLDED by extracting`parsePayload`+ 6 unit tests covering progress, result, error result, untyped backward-compat, near-miss type values, malformed JSON), W2 (line-anchor doc drift in 2 comments — FOLDED by removing line-specific anchors, citing function names), W3 (near-miss boundary test — FOLDED with explicit "Inference timed out" / "IMAP poll timed out" must-NOT-trip-breaker test). R1-R5 verified clean. **Tests 5680 → 5694** (+14: 6 parsePayload + 3 circuit-breaker variants + 2 progress-related). **Live production verification**: rebuilt mission-control:latest image (2.16GB,`keep=true`re-verified — bridges with this morning's fix); PID 3942177 active, /health ok db+inference ok; deployed dist/ grep confirms`parsePayload`, `nanoclawTimeoutMs`, `Container timed out after`, `isNanoclawCircuitOpen`shipped; docker image contains worker heartbeat code. **One in-flight`fast`chat task lost to restart** ("Chat: The name of the book is THE COMPLETE GUIDE TO BUYING A SECO...") — per`feedback_check_inflight_tasks_before_restart`known cost. **Separate ticket queued**: SDK cost-ledger phantom-turns anomaly added to`docs/planning/next-sessions-queue.md`— 2026-05-23 07:41 UTC heavy-runner SDK timeout reported`51 turns, 35 tool calls, $0.0000, tokens=0`(cost ledger writing $0 for real work done; candidate cause of today's budget cap exceedance). Five reusable lessons preserved warm-tier in`feedback_nanoclaw_timeout_recurrence_2026_05_23.md`. |
| 2026-05-23 (afternoon) | `d9f4d15` — nanoclaw image-prune recurrence fix | **9-day silent blocker resolved.** `/etc/cron.d/docker-image-prune` (daily `47 0 * * *` UTC `docker image prune -af --filter "until=24h"`) was removing `mission-control:latest` because mc API runs in-process via systemd, not as a container, so the image had no live container reference. Every nanoclaw task since at least 2026-05-15 (10 in 14d, all daily 05:03 MX "Skill evolution — YYYY-MM-DD" + 3 auto-improvement tasks) failed with `Container exited with code 125: Unable to find image 'mission-control:latest' locally → pull access denied`. The autonomous-improvement loop spawning fix-this-recurring-failure tasks ALSO routed nanoclaw → self-cancelling deadlock, no operator alert. Heavy-runner unaffected (runs in-process by default per `feedback_heavy_runner_containerized_not_perf`); CRM unaffected (uses separate `agentic-crm-agent:latest`). **L1**: rebuilt `mission-control:latest` (2.16GB, `keep=true` label verified baked in). **L2(a)**: added `LABEL keep=true` to `Dockerfile` production stage; updated `/etc/cron.d/docker-image-prune` to `--filter "label!=keep=true"` with a leading comment explaining the relationship. **L3.1**: new `imageExistsLocally(image)` helper in `container.ts` (uses `execFileSync`, not `execSync`, to avoid shell interpretation); `nanoclaw-runner.ts` runs this pre-flight before `spawnContainer()` and fails loud with a rebuild instruction including the canonical script path; new Prometheus counter `mc_nanoclaw_image_missing_total` (no labels, non-zero post-fix means prevention layer regressed). **L3.2**: `isNanoclawCircuitOpen()` in `autonomous-improvement.ts` queries last 3 nanoclaw tasks in 24h; if ALL failed matching `"Unable to find image"` OR `"exited with code 125"` (latter string is the actual `container.ts:221` template — qa-audit C1 caught the original `"exit code 125"` substring matched zero production rows), returns null and skips spawning. Fail-open on DB errors. **L3.3**: new canonical `scripts/build-mc-image.sh` (verifies the `keep=true` label is baked in via `docker image inspect`, exits 1 if missing); legacy `scripts/build-nanoclaw.sh` annotated with relationship comment. **W2 fold (qa-audit)**: same pre-flight applied to `heavy-runner.ts` for the dormant `HEAVY_RUNNER_CONTAINERIZED=true` path; future flag-flip can't silently re-open the same recurrence. **Audit PASS WITH WARNINGS — 7/11 findings folded same-bundle**: C1 (dead matcher), W2 (heavy-runner parallel gap), W4 (race-window doc), W5 (fail-open doc), S1 (doc `401` → `pull access denied`), R2 (test fixtures mirror production strings), R3 (cron file comment). 4 deferred low-priority: W1 (cron-timing doc nit), W3 (pino vs console nit), W6 (pre-existing ALLOWED_PATHS scope), R1 (runs.error fold). **Tests 5672 → 5680** (+8: 2 nanoclaw-runner pre-flight + 5 circuit-breaker variants + 1 heavy-runner pre-flight). **Live production verification**: PID 3887834, /health ok db+inference ok; deployed dist/ grep confirms `imageExistsLocally` and `isNanoclawCircuitOpen` shipped to both runners. **Stray image**: `nanoclaw-agent:latest` (1.48GB upstream qwibitai reference clone, 2026-05-21) identified as unused research artifact — left in place per operator decision. **Backups**: `/root/claude-backups/nanoclaw-fix-2026-05-23/` (7 files); branch-switch discipline: operator was on `jarvis/fix/rumi-sop-scope-trigger` with 176-line WIP staged on kb-injection — stashed it cleanly before committing to main (per `shared_worktree_branch_inheritance` rule). Five reusable lessons preserved warm-tier in `feedback_nanoclaw_image_recurrence_2026_05_23.md`. |
| 2026-05-23 (overnight) | `003e5e8..c2ab4fc` (5 R-items) + `072dd56..071c1cb` (3 T-items) — Sprint 1 fast-runner hardening | **Sprint 1 closed — 8 commits across 8 tasks, fully scoped to RELIABILITY + SPEED + EFFICIENCY (cost not a lever while on SDK).** Sprint was re-planned mid-flight after T-01's SDK probe revealed `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk@0.2.101` (49 minor versions behind upstream 0.3.150) does NOT expose `cache_control` markers, `diagnostics.previous_message_id`, per-tool `strict:true`, or server-side `tool_search_tool` — killing 4 of the original 5 high-leverage items. | 2026-05-23 (late) | `011eed8..HEAD` — Tier-A `focusTopic` parameter on `compress()` | **Tier-A cherry-pick from Hermes April Tier-2 #1 §7 — adopted the interesting bit of Hermes's `/compress [focus topic]` (topic-biased L2 summarization) at our auto-compaction layer, without the plugin slot scaffolding.** Added optional 5th positional arg `focusTopic?` to `compress()` (`context-compressor.ts`); defensive 200-char clamp + whitespace-as-absent + warn on overflow; prompt addendum lands BEFORE `STRUCTURED_COMPACT_PROMPT` on the initial path and BEFORE `LANGUAGE_RULE` on the PRESERVE+ADD update path so language directive stays final-instruction on both (most-recent-wins heuristic, ordering pinned in tests for drift detection). Addendum phrasing is additive-only ("ALSO prioritize ... does NOT permit dropping facts") — floor-preserve invariant intact in update path. No-op short-circuit (existingSummary + empty newMiddle) untouched — no LLM call, no upsertFile, no `[FOCUS:]` leak into in-memory `[CONTEXT SUMMARY]` (tested). `[FOCUS: ...]` marker prepended ONLY to the persisted `compaction/${ts}.md` body so an operator scanning long compaction histories can attribute summary posture — NOT in-memory so it doesn't silently re-anchor the next cycle. Plumbed through `compactConversation` (`compaction-pipeline.ts`, 5th positional arg) → `InferWithToolsOptions.compressionFocusTopic` (`adapter.ts`, parallels existing `compressionContext` field) at BOTH `compactConversation` call sites (normal path + 413 retry). **Path divergence documented**: openai-path only — SDK path delegates context to the SDK itself so `compress()` never fires there, field is a documented no-op under `INFERENCE_PRIMARY_PROVIDER=claude-sdk` (current default). Audit caught it as W1 (same class as v7.9 audit M1 for `compressionContext`), JSDoc now explicitly documents the divergence. **No runner-side scope→focus auto-wiring this commit** — primitive only; policy lands in a follow-up after live L2 cycles validate the prompt addendum is effective. **Tests**: +8 new (6 compressor + 2 pipeline plumbing); scoped suite 42/42, dependent test files 174/174. **Audit PASS WITH WARNINGS** (W1 + W2 folded same-bundle, S1 + R1-R4 acknowledged/skipped low-priority). qa-auditor independently verified all 4 Critical-class requirements from the brief: additive intent, no-op short-circuit untouched, defensive clamp + whitespace, both adapter call sites. Closes Tier-A no-gate item from the next-sessions queue; ships the `focusTopic` cherry-pick previously queued at `docs/planning/pluggable-context-engine-design.md` §7 first bullet. |

Then the cache_diag investigation (`f219340..d0d5d05`) located the root cause for the $754/mo `fast` Sonnet line: `flattenMessagesForSdk` (claude-sdk.ts:698) collapses every `role:"system"` message into a single `systemPrompt` blob, so the SDK creates ONE `cache_control` block — any byte change anywhere (variable KB, README size shifts) busts the entire 71K block. (4) `802d1a7` rate-limit header capture — new `inference/http-error.ts` (`HttpError` + `parseRateLimitHeaders` + `formatRateLimitLog`), both fetch sites throw `HttpError`, catch block emits structured `[inference] rate_limit_hit provider=X status=429 retryAfterMs=N ...` log line on 429 only; added `LANGUAGE_RULE` anchored on USER-role turns with code-switching tiebreak + verbatim-quote exception + `UPDATE_LANGUAGE_ADDENDUM` for continuity; (7) `686f91c` compressor anti-thrashing guards (#189) — multi-summary defensive collapse (was: only first SUMMARY_PREFIX detected → summary-of-summary on subsequent) + no-op LLM short-circuit (middle-contains-only-summary skipped infer entirely); shipped JSDoc transparency + `resolveVisionModel` foot-gun throw with operator-recovery hint; (9) `27800c1` swarm zombie-status detection (#191) — `syncSubTaskStatuses` now recognizes 3 terminal statuses the dispatcher writes but sync ignored (`completed_with_concerns` → tracker `completed`; `needs_context` → `failed`; `blocked` → `failed`); pre-fix the worst case left a SUCCESSFUL `completed_with_concerns` sub-task waiting up to 10 min then counted as failure in reflection. **Tier-2 research closures (4 notes, no code)**: (10) `78e1cf4` ACI benchmark methodology (Hermes #2) — Hermes's `tool_use_enforcement` explicitly excludes Claude family; our recurring ACI failures already have direct layer-1/2/3 fixes; decision: don't build the benchmark; (11) `c90081d` memory provider ABC comparison (Hermes #4) — Hermes's `MemoryProvider(ABC)` optimizes for third-party plugin ecosystem (Honcho/Supermemory/agentmemory); ours optimizes for in-house multi-bank with audit + trust + outcome semantics; decision: don't adopt; two cherry-picks queued (`onPreCompress?` optional + fast-runner prefetch); (12) `1eb69a8` pluggable context-engine design (Hermes #1) — no concrete second compressor on the roadmap; copy-pasteable `ContextEngine` interface preserved in §6 for trigger-fire adoption; (13) `c9023b3` `/compress` vs compact-jarvis (Hermes #3, LAST in queue) — reframed: `compact-jarvis` is READ-ONLY diagnostic (not compression); Threaded `cost_usd?` through `InferenceResponse.usage`, `actualCostUsd?` through Prometheus `TokenUsage`, summed across every inference call inside `orchestrate()`, surfaced via heavy-runner's `tokenUsage` so dispatcher's existing `costUsdOverride` path activates. swarm-runner's hardcoded `{0,0}` is a separate gap (flagged). (2) **Monthly budget cap** 200 → 400 in `config.ts` default (env override still honored) — observed MTD $405 against documented $200 was forcing daily false `exceeded:true`. (3) **kb-backup NUL strip** — 40 files/day dropping to Supabase with SQLSTATE 22P05 ("\\u0000 cannot be converted to text"); added `stripNulBytes()` before serialization (non-destructive). Bundle B `aed876d` (3 fixes + 4 audit folds): (4) **/health inference probe SDK-aware** — under `INFERENCE_PRIMARY_PROVIDER=claude-sdk` the openai-style `inferencePrimaryUrl` fetch always returned null (stale), reporting `inference: unreachable` while Sonnet served traffic; new branch checks `~/.claude/.credentials.json` size > 0 (free, fast, no third-party dep). Acknowledged tradeoff: stale/revoked token still passes (audit W3 deferred). (5) **hindsight-cost-pull noise gate** — \*/5min ritual was emitting 288 empty `series=0 cost=$0` log lines/day under SDK cutover (HINDSIGHT_ENABLED=false steady state); now skips cron entirely with single startup line unless `HINDSIGHT_COST_PULL_ENABLED=true` overrides. When scheduled, only logs runs where `series>0` so failures still surface. (6) **`mc_whatsapp_disconnects_total{reason}` Prometheus counter** — 58 Baileys flaps in 7d (~8/day) too high for log-per-event, right shape for `rate()` in Grafana. Bumped from `close` handler AND initial-connect / reconnect catches (audit W1: silent-flatline risk on degrading session — exactly inverted from detection intent). Numeric Baileys statusCodes translated to enum names (`loggedOut`/`restartRequired`/etc.) for Grafana readability; `KNOWN_WA_REASONS` cardinality guard. qa-auditor on bundle B caught W1+W2+R2+W4, all folded in-bundle; W3 deferred with documented rationale. **Tests 4830/4831 → 4832/4833** (pre-commit full-suite).
