| 2026-04-29 | (this session) | **Session 119: Kernel reboot 6.8.0-107 → 6.8.0-110, disk reclamation, Hindsight check-up scheduled.** Infra-only session, no MC source changes. **Reboot**: `apt` had staged the 110 kernel since Session 104; `shutdown -r +1` triggered the reboot, new `scripts/post-reboot-check.sh` ran via `@reboot` cron 90s after services settled and Telegrammed `ALL GREEN`. Independent verification — 4 services active, 11 containers running healthy, kernel `6.8.0-110-generic`, MC + hindsight + prometheus endpoints HTTP 200, CRM prereqs (`crm-net` network + `nanoclaw-agent:latest` + `agentic-crm-agent:latest`) survived intact this time (Session 104 had needed Watchdog Check 6 to rebuild them). 1 zombie regenerated within 3 min from supabase-studio's known next-server reaper bug (~1/day rate, doesn't justify `init: true` change). **Package upgrades**: 4 upgradable, 0 security — `ubuntu-pro-client` + `ubuntu-pro-client-l10n` 37.1 → 37.2 applied; `cloud-init` 24.1.3 → 25.3 and `iproute2` 6.1.0-1ubuntu6.2 → .3 deliberately held by Ubuntu's phased rollout (`iproute2` at `phased 10%`, not in our bucket). Phasing is the safety net for non-security regressions; left to resolve naturally. **Disk 76% → 72%, ~3 GB recovered.** Audited drift since Session 104: `/var/lib/docker` 14 GB with 7.8 GB build cache, `/var/log` 1.5 GB (951 MB journald). Build cache split: 4.98 GB **shared** (referenced by current `nanoclaw-agent` + `agentic-crm-agent` images, hot path for incremental CRM rebuilds) vs 2.80 GB **private** (orphaned, last used 3-5 days ago). Ran the safe pair: `docker buildx prune -f` (default mode strips only private — preserved shared) recovered 2.8 GB, `journalctl --vacuum-size=200M` recovered 758 MB. Aggressive `prune -a -f` would have pulled another 4.98 GB but next CRM rebuild ~2-3 min instead of ~30s, not worth it at 72%. **Hindsight CPU runaway — measured, deferred 2 weeks.** `/var/log/watchdog.log` shows 77 `Hindsight CPU >95% restart` actions in last 7 days (avg 11/day) — chronic pattern, not a recent flare. Each restart succeeds and CPU returns to <1%, but the cycle repeats every few hours; Watchdog Check 4 is containing it. Rather than dive into a deep diagnosis tonight, scheduled new `scripts/hindsight-checkup.sh` to fire once on **2026-05-13 18:00 UTC** via cron `0 18 13 5 *`, classifies next 7-day rate as RESOLVED (<2/day) / STEADY (2-5/day) / ESCALATE (≥5/day), and on ESCALATE tails docker logs to surface top 10 most-repeated log patterns + recent error/warn/timeout lines. Telegrams headline + writes report to `data/hindsight-checkup-{date}.md`. Self-removes from cron after first fire. **Two new tracked scripts**: `scripts/post-reboot-check.sh` (kept around for re-use before any future planned reboot — add `@reboot` line, run, self-removes) and `scripts/hindsight-checkup.sh` (one-shot, scheduled). Both pass `bash -n` + `shellcheck --severity=error` per the lint-scripts CI workflow added in Session 104. **Patterns captured in memory**: `feedback_oneshot_cron_pattern.md` (date-pinned cron + self-remove is the right shape on this VPS — `at(1)` not installed, `atd` daemon not running) and `reference_remote_vs_local_scheduling.md` (`/schedule` cloud agents can't reach VPS files/docker/.env; do not provision a third SSH key into the cloud sandbox just to make them fit — keep VPS-local work local). Open carry-forward unchanged: backup encryption decision (VeraCrypt vs 7-Zip), MC daily budget overrun, `src/inference/vision.ts:51` latent null-guard. |
| 2026-04-25 | `33491be`, `d662bde`, `740b54e`, `cc22e10` (radar) | **Session 107: pgvector drift class closed + shell-guard false-positive sweep + radar fetch tooling.** Three threads landed in one session, all driven by user concerns surfacing while iterating with Jarvis on the Williams-Entry-Radar universe expansion. **(A) pgvector CHECK drift** — the v6.4 correction-loop and v7.13 PDF ingestion had been silently failing every upsert for an unknown duration (HTTP 400 / SQLSTATE 23514) because `kb_entries_type_check` and `kb_entries_qualifier_check` allow-lists were never updated when those features shipped. Tests passed because `pgvector.test.ts` mocks `fetch`. Live audit: 0 corrections + 0 PDF entries in pgvector ever. Fix shape: idempotent guarded `DO $$ ... $$` migrations (re-run = no-op via `pg_get_constraintdef LIKE '%''X''%'` check), TS literal-union types `KbEntryType` / `KbQualifier` derived from `as const` arrays so the compiler refuses unknown literals at typed call sites, `coerceKbType` / `coerceKbQualifier` boundary helpers that emit `console.warn` on non-empty unknowns (silent on empty/null — absence ≠ drift), JSON-schema `enum:` on the LLM-driven `kb_batch_insert` tool plus coercion in execute() before `pgBatchUpsert`, `qualifierToSalience` typed `(KbQualifier) => number` with `_exhaustive: never` default so adding a qualifier without updating the salience map is a compile-time error, canonical schema snapshot at `schema/kb_entries.sql` (with `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector` for fresh-env replay), 10 new contract tests pinning array contents + coerce returns + warn-on-unknown + silent-on-empty. **(B) Shell-guard false positives** — Jarvis tried to fetch tickers; three legitimate workaround attempts blocked. `2>/dev/null` matched the WRITE_INDICATORS regex `(?:>\s*\|...)(\/[^\s]+)` and was rejected as "redirect to system directory" because `/dev/null` isn't in `ALLOW_WRITE_PREFIXES` — exempted via tightened DENY_PATTERN regex `>\s*\/dev\/(?!(?:null\|stderr\|stdout)(?:\s\|[\|;&]\|$))` (negative lookahead with hard terminator so adversarial suffixes like `/dev/null.bak` still hit the deny path) plus a `WRITE_INDICATOR_EXEMPT` set in the second-stage loop. `/root/claude/williams-entry-radar/` added to `ALLOW_WRITE_PREFIXES` so heredoc-based file writes inside Jarvis's autonomous build no longer reject. +8 tests including 3 adversarial path-suffix variants verified safe. **(C) Operator-sanctioned ticker fetch** — universe expanded from 79 → 159 tickers in radar PR #1 (`53d1219` on EurekaMD-net/Williams-Entry-Radar, merged 2026-04-25), but new tickers had 0 weekly bars. Mc has no `AV_API_KEY` (it lives only in `/etc/williams-radar.env`), so `node fetcher.ts` subprocess threw on the first call. Added `scripts/fetch.sh` wrapper that sources the env file (ENV_FILE pinned to `/etc/` allowlist to close an RCE primitive — audit W1, `set -u` re-asserted post-source, repo-local `./node_modules/.bin/tsx` not `npx tsx` so fresh-VPS runs never hit npm registry — audit S1) and runs a new `src/fetch-tickers.ts` that calls `fetchAll(getUniverseTickers())` with the existing 1.1s throttle (60s/1.1s ≈ 54 req/min, 28% headroom under AV Premium's 75/min ceiling, well above the undocumented 5 req/sec burst floor — earlier no-throttle attempt failed 15 of 20 calls with "Burst pattern detected"). Live verification: 157 tickers, 125 cache + 32 fetched + 0 errors; idempotent re-run 157 cache + 0 fetched. CLAUDE.md off-limits list extended to mark `src/fetch-tickers.ts`, `scripts/fetch.sh`, `src/fetcher.ts`, `src/cache.ts`, `src/db.ts` as run-don't-edit for autonomous agents. **Session metrics**: tests 3761 → 3783 (+22 across 4 commits), service redeployed twice (active, 252 tools), no startup errors, no regressions. **Anchor tag**: `pre-jarvis-universe-2026-04-25` on radar's `2edce1b` — recovery snap-back if anything ever drifts in the signal logic. Detailed memory: `feedback_pgvector_check_drift`, `feedback_shell_guard_maturity` (this session). |
| 2026-04-25 | `18893f6` | **Session 106: `projects` scope group — project tools no longer silently miss the prompt.** User flagged Jarvis "denying" project ops: "Reactiva el proyecto Williams Entry Radar" returned acknowledgment but project status stayed `archived`. Three retries with progressively explicit phrasing (third attempt literally said "usando la herramienta project*update") produced the same outcome each time. Diagnosis (full /diagnose run): `project_update` is `deferred: true` in `src/tools/builtin/projects.ts`; pre-fix the only push site was inside `if (activeGroups.has("coding"))` at `scope.ts:1088`. Semantic classifier correctly identified intent and routed to `northstar_write` — which doesn't push project tools, so the LLM's tool palette had `northstar_sync` (always-on via MISC_TOOLS) but no `project_update`. LLM faithfully called the available NorthStar sync tool, destructive flag fired in logs, project row in `data/mc.db` untouched. Three poisoned `correction-loop` entries got persisted by the rephrase loop teaching the system the wrong lesson — though all 3 actually failed at the pgvector CHECK constraint (code 23514) and never made it to disk; latent bug in correction-loop noted as separate scope. **Fix**: introduced `projects` as a first-class scope group distinct from NorthStar (projects can link to goals via `commit_goal_id` but neither owns the other; project lifecycle/credentials/URLs are not NorthStar concerns). `scope-classifier.ts` got a `projects` entry in `VALID_GROUPS` and an explicit system-prompt description marking it distinct from `northstar*_`. `scope.ts`got two new regex patterns (verb-noun + noun-verb orders) for the fallback path, removed`proyectos?\|projects?`from the NorthStar regex at line 391, and now pushes`project_get`+`project_update`when`projects`is active; meta scope expansion includes`projects`. **QA-auditor round caught two false-positive vectors in round 1**: loose `\w_` verb stems (`reactiv\w\*`matched "activamente" / "completamente" / "infografía"; the bounded {0,5} slop window made these activate`projects` on irrelevant prose) and missing accent tolerance for read verbs (`muéstrame`/`descríbeme`/`descríbeme`had no path through the alternation). Round 2 tightened to exact`\b`-anchored verb forms (`reactiva\|reactivar\|activa\|activar\|...`) and added `mu[eé]stra(?:me\|lo\|la\|nos)?`plus`descr[ií]beme`. Tests 3758 → 3761 (+10 net new in `scope.test.ts`: positive activation on reactivate/archive/status-update/credentials, negative isolation from JARVIS_WRITE_TOOLS, adverb non-firing, accent-tolerant read verbs, catastrophic-backtracking sanity check). Service deployed via `./scripts/deploy.sh`, 252 tools, active. **Out-of-scope follow-up flagged**: `react`in coding regex at`scope.ts:507`lacks`\b`boundary, so "reactiva" coincidentally still co-fires`coding`and pulls JARVIS_WRITE_TOOLS + 22 coding tools alongside the now-correct`project_update`— functional fix is complete; cosmetic prompt bloat on this verb only. Tighten to`react(?:\.?js)?\b`as a 1-character follow-up. Detail:`feedback_projects_scope_separation` memory. |
| 2026-04-24 | (no mc commits — local-only rotation) | **Session 105 follow-up: CRM_API_TOKEN rotation + .env mode hardening.** After restarting `agentic-crm` to ship the CRM-side `jarvis_pull` fix (separate session work in `kosm1x/crm-azteca`), Jarvis's CRM-status fetches started returning `401 {"error":"Invalid or expired token"}`. Diagnosis: CRM `dashboard/auth.ts` falls back to `crypto.randomBytes(32)` when `DASHBOARD_JWT_SECRET` is unset, generating a fresh secret on every process start — so `agentic-crm` restart silently invalidated mc's 30-day-`exp` token whose signature was suddenly orphaned. Mc's `CRM_API_TOKEN` decoded with `exp` = May 24 (still valid), but signature verification failed against the new random secret. Fix: pinned `DASHBOARD_JWT_SECRET` (64 hex chars from `openssl rand -hex 32`) in `crm-azteca/.env`, restarted CRM, minted fresh JWT for `per-001 / vp` against the now-stable secret (locally signature-verified before deploy), wrote new token to `mission-control/.env`'s `CRM_API_TOKEN` line, restarted mission-control. Audit found `mission-control/.env` and `COMMIT-AI/.env` were mode `644` (world-readable despite holding `INFERENCE_*_KEY` × 3, `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, `MC_API_KEY`, and now `CRM_API_TOKEN`); both tightened to `600`. CRM-side commit `0d9d65e` (kosm1x/crm-azteca) documents `DASHBOARD_JWT_SECRET` as required in `.env.example`. End-to-end verification: real `/api/tasks` submission ("Usa crm_query con endpoint pipeline") completed in 17s, used `crm_query`, returned `total_propuestas: 72 \| valor_total: $533,600,000 MXN` (matching a direct curl probe). No mc source changes — env-only update. Open follow-up: `auth.ts:verify` returns the same string `"Invalid or expired token"` for both signature-fail and exp-past, conflating two distinct failure modes; a 5-line edit there would have made tonight's diagnosis a 30-second job. Detail: `crm-azteca/docs/LEARNINGS-2026-04-24.md` §7 + `feedback_random_fallback_secret` memory. |

| 2026-04-22 | Dim-4 Resilience (2 commits, 2 audit rounds) | **Session 101 part 4: Dim-4 Resilience audit — 5 Critical + 2 Major closed via double-audit discipline.** 30-day hardening window, dimension 4/5. Round 1 surfaced 2 Criticals: (C-RES-1) `src/inference/claude-sdk.ts` had ZERO `circuitRegistry` calls — since 2026-04-22 Sonnet flip, Sonnet failures didn't trip the shared breaker used by fast-runner + Prometheus, so N consecutive 5xx each burned the 15-min SDK_TIMEOUT_MS; (C-RES-3) no startup reconciliation of orphaned tasks — graceful SIGTERM marks running/pending/queued → failed but SIGKILL/OOM/hard-reboot skips the handler and tasks stuck `pending`/`queued` never had `started_at` so `reactions/manager`'s 15-min stuck-check also missed them, permanent limbo. Plus 1 Major (M-RES-1): 7 static ritual failure paths (`morning-briefing`, `nightly-close`, `market-*`, `kb-backup`, `memory-consolidation`, `diff-digest`, `stale-artifact-prune`, `autonomous-improvement`) all `console.error` only — methodology required `events` row with category=schedule + type=failed. **Round 1 fixes landed**: circuitRegistry wired in `claude-sdk.ts` with tri-state `providerOutcome` classification (partial-content-with-error = success, zero-content + timeout = failure); new `reconcileOrphanedTasks()` at boot in `src/db/index.ts` + call from `src/index.ts`; new `"schedule"` category + `"schedule.run_failed"` event type + `recordRitualFailure()` helper wired into 7 catch blocks. **Round 2 (independent qa-auditor pass) caught 3 additional Criticals on the round-1 fixes themselves**: (C-RES-4) Prometheus `executeGraph` `Promise.allSettled` fan-out — when breaker is HALF_OPEN only 1 concurrent caller wins the probe slot, others throw "Circuit breaker OPEN" which `executor.ts::classifyError` didn't match as transient → spuriously escalates all non-probe goals; (C-RES-5) `recordRitualFailure`'s bare `catch {}` swallowed TypeError / SQLITE_MISUSE / payload-shape-mismatch — enforcement theater; (C-RES-6) `reconcileOrphanedTasks` flipped user-facing rows to `failed` with no `task.failed` event so reactions engine + user-notification pipeline never fired → regression to silent-task-loss. Plus Major (M-RES-4): `providerOutcome === null` default was inverted — "no signal" recorded as success instead of failure despite the code comment saying the opposite. **Round 2 fixes landed**: `"circuit breaker"` added to `TRANSIENT_PATTERNS` in `executor.ts` + `CB_COOLDOWN_MS`-sized backoff when breaker token matches; `recordRitualFailure` narrow catch with console passthrough; `reconcileOrphanedTasks` returns `string[]` via atomic transaction + `src/index.ts` emits `task.failed` events after `initEventBus`; inverted `providerOutcome` default. **Deferred**: M-RES-2 (SDK cred expiry distinct alert), M-RES-3 (running-container orphan reaper), W-RES-1 (`schedule_runs` stale status), W-RES-2 (Telegram 5-cap silent exhaustion), W-RES-3 (prometheus_snapshots leftover), W-RES-4 (`/health` cooldownRemainingMs), W-RES-5 (orphan-container reaper) — all 7 have explicit triggers. **Tests**: 3721 → 3730 pass (+9 regression guards). **Live smoke**: R3 end-to-end (injected `SMOKE-R3-ORPHAN-1` pending row → restart → reconciled to `failed` with forensic error + `task.failed` event persisted); R4 WAL integrity (hot-copy of live DB + WAL sidecar → `PRAGMA integrity_check=ok`, 2877 tasks + 4608 conversations intact). Full report: `docs/audit/2026-04-22-resilience.md`. Progress: **4/5 audit dimensions closed**. Dim 5 (Tool scoping) remains. |
| 2026-04-22 | `ec5c105` (1 commit, 2 audit rounds) | **Session 101 part 3: Dim-3 Security audit — 9 Critical + 7 Major closed via double-audit discipline.** Phase β of the 30-day hardening window, dimension 3/5. Round 1 direct audit surfaced 6 Criticals: (C-SSRF-1/2) `wp_media_upload`/`gemini_research` fetched LLM-provided URLs with no `validateOutboundUrl`; (C-PATH-1/2) `file_read` had zero path validation AND `validatePathSafety(read)` skipped the dangerous-file check by construction, so even callers that used it on read let credentials through; (C-PATH-3) `wp_media_upload` with local `image_url` starting `/` or `file://` did `readFileSync` on any absolute path and uploaded bytes to WordPress — direct secret-exfil to a publicly-hosted site; (C-INJ-1) `sanitizeToolResult` was wired on `src/inference/adapter.ts` (OpenAI path) but NOT on `src/inference/claude-sdk.ts` wrapTool — meaning since 2026-04-22 when claude-sdk became primary, every tool-result prompt-injection reached Sonnet unfiltered. Plus 4 round-1 Majors: (M-CTR-1) nanoclaw mounted `/root/claude/mission-control:rw`; (M-SHELL-1) shell DENY_COMMANDS missed reader commands; (M-RATE-1) no rate-limit on `/api/*` or `/a2a/*`; (M-CRED-FILTER) jarvis-dev SENSITIVE list too narrow. **Round 1 fixes landed**: new `READ_BLOCKED_PATHS` + `READ_BLOCKED_BASENAMES` in `src/tools/builtin/immutable-core.ts` (poka-yoke trailing-slash semantics to avoid `/root/.ssh` matching `/root/.ssh-backup`); `validatePathSafety` now enforces the read denylist regardless of operation; `fileReadTool` wired through it; `wp_media_upload` local path constrained to `/tmp/wp_content/` via resolve() check; `validateOutboundUrl` added to wp_media_upload URL branch and gemini_research URL branch; `sanitizeToolResult` imported + called in `claude-sdk.ts::wrapTool`; nanoclaw mount flipped `:rw` → `:ro`; jarvis-dev SENSITIVE widened 6 → 18 entries covering private-key filenames, cert extensions, auth dotfiles; new `src/api/rate-limit.ts` (IP-keyed sliding window, 300 req/min) applied to `/api/*` BEFORE apiKeyAuth so pre-auth traffic counts toward the limit. **Round 2 discipline** — independent `qa-auditor` subagent re-audited the round-1 fixes and found **3 additional Criticals round 1 missed entirely**: (C1) `pdf-read.ts` has the same SSRF + local-path pattern as wordpress/gemini, round 1 never looked at it; (C2) `gemini-research.ts` local branch at line 298 was still unguarded — round 1 fixed URL branch at line 266 only (**dual-dispatch half-fix class**); (C3) `contentFile` absolute-path exfil across **4 publish tools** — `google_docs_append_text`, `google_docs_apply_revisions`, `google_drive_create_or_update`, `wp_publish` — each accepts LLM-controlled `content_file` path → `readFileSync` → uploads bytes as document/post body. Plus 3 new Majors round 2 found: (M1-R2) shell_exec's DENY_COMMANDS blocked rm/mkfs/sqlite3 but allowed cat/head/tail/less/xxd/strings/awk/sed/grep/base64 — LLM bypassed READ_BLOCKED_PATHS trivially via `shell_exec("cat /root/.claude/.credentials.json")`; (M2-R2) `resolve()` without `realpathSync()` meant `ln -s /root/.claude/.credentials.json /tmp/safe.txt` + file_read(`/tmp/safe.txt`) bypassed the denylist — symlinks resolved after the string-match check; (M3-R2) `X-Forwarded-For` trusted unconditionally, so an attacker hitting the directly-bound :8080 (UFW-open) could rotate XFF per request to scatter rate-limit buckets across infinite keys. **Round 2 fixes landed**: validateOutboundUrl + validatePathSafety wired into `pdf-read.ts` URL + local branches; gemini_research local branch now calls validatePathSafety; all 4 contentFile sites (google-docs × 2, google-drive, wordpress wp_publish) call validatePathSafety before readFileSync; shell.ts DENY_PATTERNS extended with 6 reader-command × secret-path-family regex blocks (credentials.json, /root/.ssh/, /root/.gnupg+.aws+.docker+.kube+.config/gh, /etc/shadow+sudoers+ssh, /proc/self/environ+mem, /root/.npmrc+.netrc+.pgpass+.gitconfig+.git-credentials); validatePathSafety now probes BOTH `resolve()` AND `realpathSync()` (dual-probe catches path-traversal via resolve, symlink-hop via realpath); rate-limit reads socket `remoteAddress` unless `TRUST_FORWARDED_FOR=true` AND socket is localhost (127.0.0.1/::1/::ffff:127.0.0.1 — the Caddy topology); MAX_TRACKED_IPS=10_000 wholesale eviction cap added; `/a2a/*` also rate-limited (W3). **Live smoke verified all blocks**: `cat /root/.claude/.credentials.json`, `head /root/.ssh/id_rsa`, `base64 /etc/shadow`, `grep /root/.gnupg/…`, `cat /proc/self/environ`, `cat /root/.npmrc` all blocked; `cat /tmp/hello.txt` and `echo hi && curl https://example.com` pass (negative-case verified); `ln -sf /root/.claude/.credentials.json /tmp/sec_probe_symlink` → validatePathSafety blocked with credentials.json reason (realpath resolution working); rate-limit: 300 req pass, 301st returns 429 + `Retry-After: 58`. **Tests**: 3718→3721 (+3 regression guards for new read denylist — covers `.ssh/authorized_keys` blocked, credentials.json by basename, id_rsa in any directory, poka-yoke for `/root/.ssh-backup/` NOT blocked). Typecheck zero errors. **3 Warnings deferred with explicit triggers**: W1 admin `/drive-backfill`+`/drive-reformat` need X-Kill-Passphrase; W2 jarvis-dev SENSITIVE still misses authorized_keys/kubeconfig/.kube/.docker/.ovpn/.kdbx; W5 `/docs/raw/:file` percent-encoded brittleness. New `feedback_dim3_security_audit.md` with 7 generalizable rules, biggest: **"defense-in-paper" anti-pattern** (guards that look like they run but don't by construction/wiring/topology — 3 instances in one audit: read-mode skip by construction, SDK-path unwired sanitize, unconditional XFF trust). Methodology's double-audit requirement for Security + Resilience confirmed load-bearing — round 1 missed 3 Criticals. **30d hardening window progress: 3/5 dimensions closed.** |
| 2026-04-22 | `c88799d` (1 commit) | **Session 101 part 2: Dim-2 Speed audit — Hindsight recall timeout 5000ms → 1500ms + measurement corrections.** Phase β of hardening, dimension 2/5. **Headline**: fast-runner 7d p90 = **122s vs 20s methodology target** (6× over). Pre- and post-Sonnet-flip indistinguishable (p50 34s → 37s; p90 128s → 122s) — the bottleneck is not the model. Heavy-runner 7d PER phase p50: plan 28.5s, execute 190s, reflect 25.3s (extracted from `runs.trace` JSON — the methodology had assumed these weren't observable, but they ARE; `task_provenance` was the wrong table, `runs.trace` persists the orchestrator's phase_start/phase_end events end-to-end). The ≤45s "trivial task" PER target is architecturally impossible at Sonnet per-phase cost — plan + reflect alone = 53.8s floor before execute does anything. **Biggest leak fixed (S7 Critical)**: Hindsight recall empirically takes ~9s per query under load (agentic LLM-backed recall — Hindsight's `openai_compatible_llm` backend reports mental-model refresh calls at `llm=[agent_1_err=36106ms, agent_2_err=35628ms, final=19660ms] (91394ms)` — the recall path shares the same slow backend at ~9s). Jarvis's `DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS=5000` → **100% of Hindsight recalls time out** → SQLite fallback runs in ~300ms → **user pays 5.3s wall-clock for a 300ms answer** every memory recall. Verified in journalctl: `[memory] recall(hindsight) FAILED after 5022ms — falling back to SQLite` on 100% of attempts. Fix: introduced `RECALL_TIMEOUT_MS = 1500` (recall-specific; retain/reflect keep 5000ms since they're non-user-interactive). Per-recall savings: 5005ms → 1505ms ≈ 3500ms (~70% reduction). Verified live: 3 back-to-back probes against real Hindsight post-deploy confirmed 1505ms abort vs 5022ms pre-fix. **4 other probes deferred with explicit triggers** (S3/S4/S5/S6 all share one root cause: `toolMetrics`/`providerMetrics` are in-memory rolling-100-per-tool, lost on every restart; full catalog 7d p50/p90 per tool is not computable). Mechanical follow-up: persist to SQLite `tool_latency` table, scheduled for post-freeze P0. **Tests**: 3717→3718 (+1 regression guard for AbortSignal at 1500ms under fake timers). Typecheck zero errors. **7 deferred follow-ups with triggers**: T-Inst-1 per-turn fast-runner timing (`llm=/tools=/memory=/total=`), T-Fix-S7-Spillover re-measure p90 post-fix, T-Inst-2 surface PER `phase_durations` in dashboard, T-Goal-1 revisit 45s target, T-Spawn-1 nanoclaw e2e timing, T-Metrics-1 persist toolMetrics to SQLite, T-Metrics-2 full-catalog `/health`. New `feedback_dim2_speed_audit.md` with 5 generalizable rules, biggest: **re-probe upstream latency when a dependency's implementation shifts from fast-infra to LLM-backed** — client timeouts calibrated for vector-DB latency rot silently when the upstream becomes agentic; fallbacks that return correct-but-slow results mask broken primary paths indefinitely. |
| 2026-04-22 | `79ee21d` (1 commit) | **Session 101 part 1: Dim-1 Efficiency audit — 4 stacked observability bugs on the Sonnet path + prompt-cache fix.** Phase β of hardening, dimension 1/5. Before fixing, every efficiency measurement on the claude-sdk runtime path was nonsense: (1) `cost_ledger.model` always read `"qwen3.5-plus"` regardless of actual provider — `dispatcher.getModelFromTask()` returned `cfg.inferencePrimaryModel` (env-pinned, qwen-era stale value) even when runtime was Sonnet; (2) `cost_ledger.prompt_tokens` recorded only `usage.input_tokens` — under Anthropic prompt caching that's the uncached delta (~8 tokens), not the full prompt — the real total is `input_tokens + cache_creation_input_tokens + cache_read_input_tokens` per Messages API spec; (3) `scope_telemetry.tools_called` always `[]` on SDK path — `recordToolExecution()` was only called from the OpenAI branch of fast-runner, SDK branch returned 100 lines earlier; (4) **Anthropic prompt cache 0% hit ratio** — `identitySection()` embedded `Hoy es ${mxDate}, son las ${mxTime}` directly in the P1 (never-truncated) system-prompt prefix, so `mxTime` changing every second meant every single call rebuilt a unique prefix → 100% cache miss in production for the entire period since Sonnet became primary. **Fix**: extracted time/date into a per-call preamble `timeContextLine(mxDate, mxTime)` injected as the FIRST line of the user message (not the system prompt). `identitySection()` is now byte-identical across calls. `getModelFromTask()` branches on provider: returns `SONNET_MODEL_ID` under claude-sdk, falls back to tier-based logic for openai. Claude SDK result type extended with `cacheReadTokens`/`cacheCreationTokens`/`actualModel`/`costUsd`. Fast-runner SDK branch now threads all of this through `tokenUsage` to dispatcher, and calls `recordToolExecution` before returning (parity with OpenAI branch). New `costUsdOverride` pattern on `recordCost()` — when the provider reports an authoritative cost (SDK returns $0 under Max subscription), use it verbatim instead of re-computing via the local pricing table; added Sonnet/Opus/Haiku pricing entries at $0 to avoid false "budget exceeded" alerts. `DataLayer.stats()` method ships L1/L2/fetch/stale/inflight-dedup counters on `getDaily` (follow-ups queued for getWeekly/getIntraday/getMacro — mechanical). Hindsight `recall()` now logs per-call timing (`[memory] recall(hindsight) bank=X results=N Yms` on success, `FAILED after Yms — falling back to SQLite` on error) — this immediately surfaced the Dim-2 headline finding. **Live verification**: smoke-tested 2 Sonnet calls, measured cache hit **0% → 56%** on call 2, cost **$0.0578 → $0.0280** per turn (52% reduction, matches methodology expectation). Cost ledger now correctly labels `model='claude-sonnet-4-6'`; `prompt_tokens` now includes cache totals (first observed Sonnet call: 73,619 avg prompt vs pre-fix 8-token under-count). **Tests**: 3711→3717 (+6 regression guards — sum-three-input-fields, SONNET_MODEL_ID fallback, costUsdOverride under Max auth AND metered, identitySection byte-stability, timeContextLine format, router chat preamble injection, DataLayer stats counters). New `feedback_dim1_efficiency_audit.md` with 5 rules, biggest: \**Anthropic prompt cache is byte-exact — any `${...}` interpolation with changing content in the cached prefix breaks caching globally\*\*. Methodology counter-measure: always diff two consecutive same-shape calls' system prompts character-by-character when auditing prompt efficiency. |
| 2026-04-22 | `bd68fb1` + `350c90f` (2 commits) | **Session 100: Sonnet now primary across ALL runners (Docker-isolated paths fixed).** Pre-fix state: host had `INFERENCE_PRIMARY_PROVIDER=claude-sdk` but Docker-spawning runners (`nanoclaw-runner.ts`, `heavy-runner.ts::executeInContainer`) silently stayed on qwen — the provider flag wasn't forwarded into container `envVars`, and `~/.claude/.credentials.json` wasn't mounted. Nanoclaw had a 97% fail rate over 30d (91 failed / 3 completed) because `config.heavyRunnerImage` = `mission-control:latest` didn't exist on this VPS at all (the similarly-named `nanoclaw-agent:latest` was a stale April-9 image with no SDK). **Fix #1 (`bd68fb1`)**: forward `INFERENCE_PRIMARY_PROVIDER` in both runners' `envVars`; when provider is `claude-sdk`, also set `HOME=/root` and mount `/root/.claude/.credentials.json:/root/.claude/.credentials.json:ro`. Extended `container.ts` volume allowlist to include the exact credentials file path (not the whole `~/.claude/` dir — tight blast radius). All additions gated on `isClaudeSdk`; when rolled back to `openai`, container env + volumes are byte-identical to pre-fix behavior. **Fix #2 (`350c90f`)**: added `.dockerignore` excluding `node_modules`, `dist`, `data`, `backups`, `venv`, logs, and `.claude`/`.env*` from build context (saves ~2GB context transfer, removes accidental-leak surface if future COPY is widened). **Image rebuild**: `docker build -f Dockerfile -t mission-control:latest .` produced 2.15GB image with `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk`, both worker entrypoints (`dist/runners/nanoclaw-worker.js`, `dist/runners/heavy-worker.js`), and working `better-sqlite3` native module. **Tests**: 3709→3711 (+2 claude-sdk-branch assertions per runner). **Live smoke** — both workers PASS e2e through the full Prometheus PER loop on Sonnet: nanoclaw-worker took 4 Sonnet calls (planner+executor+reflector+wrap), 28s wall-clock, score=1.00; heavy-worker took 4 Sonnet calls, ~16s, score=1.00. `[claude-sdk] Completed: N turns, M tool calls, $X.XXXX` log lines confirm SDK path fired inside containers. **Runtime path matrix after fix**: fast-runner (Sonnet, in-process), heavy-runner in-process default (Sonnet), heavy-runner containerized when flag on (Sonnet), nanoclaw (Sonnet). Rollback: set `INFERENCE_PRIMARY_PROVIDER=openai` in systemd drop-in → all paths revert to qwen. **Sibling gap surfaced but not fixed this session**: `cost_ledger` records all calls as `model='qwen3.5-plus'` regardless of actual provider because `dispatcher.ts:547-555::getModelFromTask()` reads `cfg.inferencePrimaryModel` (env-pinned for back-compat) rather than branching on provider. Real Sonnet calls land at qwen pricing in the ledger — under-reports Sonnet volume 20-50x on `/health` daily-spend. Same class as v7.9 deferred W7; fold into a future Prometheus-hardening sprint. **Disk cleanup** (separate thread same session): `docker system prune -f` (~4KB reclaimed) + `docker builder prune -f` (no-op, cache in-use) + removed 71 stale git clones + 5 analysis dirs from `/tmp/` (≈4GB). Disk 72G→68G (75%→71%). Nothing touched in `data/`, running containers, or lightpanda/venv. 7 new patterns in `feedback_sonnet_container_routing.md`: env propagation audit on flag flip; Docker image-tag mismatch masks silent failure; 2-tier smoke (config + e2e); cost_ledger mislabel under claude-sdk; exact-path allowlist not parent dir; `.dockerignore` despite targeted COPYs; fail-loud at provisioning boundaries beats silent qwen fallback. |

`env` on timeout/ECONNRESET/rate-limit string matches; `agent` on classification-only or scope-only regression when mutation surface didn't touch those; `skill` otherwise — telemetry-only, never blocks a mutation); `src/tuning/gates.ts` (5-gate monotonic validator — `constitution` anti-overfitting, `regex_sanity` syntax check BEFORE size for scope\*rule mutations, `size` 2x length cap, `worthiness` single-case-fix 20% length ceiling, `safety` preserves designated keywords when originally present, `cooldown` 24h per-target lockout — env-driven config via `TUNING_SAFETY_KEYWORDS` + `TUNING_COOLDOWN_HOURS`); `src/tuning/confidence.ts` (GEPA v0.1.1 ConfidenceAdapter — per-case score stddev proxy until qwen logprob API stabilizes, returns 0..1 range); `src/tuning/trajectory-miner.ts` (SkillClaw — stable-failing cases across ≥3 experiments get promoted to `mined_test_cases` with source `trajectory`; upserts to `{case_id}-traj`; skips deactivated source cases; gated behind `TUNING_TRAJECTORY_MINE=true`); `src/tuning/self-review.ts` (Superpowers v5.0.6 — inline 4-item checklist replaces subagent-dispatch review at ~50x speedup; placeholder regex narrowed per R1 to `TODO:`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`<REPLACE>`/`<INSERT>`/`{{template}}`/`[TBD]`/`[example]`/`[placeholder]`/`<fill in>`/`<YOUR**>`— bare "TODO" in legitimate content no longer triggers; gated behind`TUNING_SELF_REVIEW=true`). **Modified**: `types.ts`adds`FailureSource`enum +`TuneVariantWithChildren`+`score_child_prop`strategy + optional`created_at`/`failure_source`/`confidence_avg`on`Experiment`. `schema.ts`adds idempotent`addColumnIfMissing`helper, new`getValidVariantsWithChildren`+`updateExperimentFailureSource`+`getLastExperimentForTarget`(SQL-filtered to regressed/rejected, env statuses never trigger cooldown).`parent-selection.ts`adds HyperAgents`score_child_prop`weighted by`score / (1 + child_count)`— prefers less-explored branches; falls back to`score_prop`when child_count absent.`overnight-loop.ts`default`parentSelection`flipped to`score_child_prop`(env-overridable via`TUNING_PARENT_SELECTION`), replaces `validateMutation`with`runGates`, adds self-review → failure classification → confidence proxy → trajectory miner in order; all 6 additions flag-gated so existing Tue/Thu/Sat 1AM cron is byte-identical behavior when flags are off. **Upstream sweep**: 10 core refs touched — GEPA v0.1.1 (9d delta, ConfidenceAdapter + `reflection_lm_kwargs`passthrough), SkillClaw (research paper, no repo, 4 patterns unchanged), Hyperagents (stable since March, all 5 parent-selection + staged-eval + archive patterns already implemented locally or now ported), Hermes/Prometheus v0.9→v0.10 (compression smart-collapse deferred v7.5.1), ACE (3-agent loop deferred v7.6), Memoria v0.2.3→v0.3.2 (budget pools + cooldowns + trust-tier folded into gate taxonomy), claude-mem v10.6→v12.3 (subagent labeling schema deferred v7.6 behind kb_entries.layer field), mempalace (N/A — Jarvis doesn't use mempalace runtime), Superpowers v5.0.4→v5.0.7 (inline self-review folded), context-engineering v2.0.0 (latent-briefing deferred v7.6). Sweep report:`memory/v75_upstream_sweep_report.md`. **Round 1 audit** (PASS WITH WARNINGS → fixed): 1 Critical — (C1) self-review `/\bTODO\b/`false-positive on legitimate "Track TODOs" content (narrowed to`TODO:`+ added alternates); 1 non-bug auditor claim (C2 staged-gate cost leak — cheap evals are deterministic, zero tokens by design, correctly not recorded). 5 Major — (M1) self-review reject hardcoded "skill" failure_source; routed through classifier; (M2)`getLastExperimentForTarget`didn't filter by terminal status, env errors over-blocked cooldown; fixed with SQL-level`status IN ('regressed','rejected')`; (M3) scope_rule mutation with invalid regex silently no-op'd in `applySandbox`producing falsely-passing experiment; added`regex_sanity`gate BEFORE size so ops see "invalid regex" not "too long"; (M4) cooldown`typeof last.created_at === "string"`defensive guard for null/undefined paths; (M5) trajectory miner source lookup didn't filter`active=1`, would re-promote soft-deleted cases; fixed in both UNION arms. 8 Warnings — half fixed (negative-cooldown clamp, pending-status classifier guard, placeholder pattern expansion, scope_rule regex-first ordering); half accepted as low-risk. **Round 2 audit** (MINOR-FIXES-NEEDED → fixed): 0 Critical. 2 Major — (MA-1) regex_sanity gate mislabeled as `size`in telemetry output (fixed with new`GateName` enum value); (MA-2) regex_sanity ran AFTER size gate so operators saw "mutation too long" before "invalid regex" (moved to fire before size). 6 Warnings — W2 placeholder-pattern gap (`XXX`/`[TBD]`/`<REPLACE>`/`<YOUR_TEXT>`) fixed; W1 comment added linking SQL status filter to TS guard to prevent drift; others accepted. **Live smoke** on `/tmp/mc-v75-smoke.db`with 3 mock experiments: exp-0 (new scope mutation) → ✓ KEEP +0.6, failure_source=null, confidence_avg=0.58; exp-1 (simpler scope regression) → ✗ STAGED GATE correctly rejects before expensive eval, failure_source="skill", confidence_avg=0.52; exp-2 (same as exp-0) → ✗ GATE-COOLDOWN "target regressed 0.0h ago; cooldown is 24h" — the new cooldown gate fires correctly on identical re-proposal within window. DB schema confirmed via`PRAGMA table_info`: both `failure_source TEXT`and`confidence_avg REAL`columns present, populated correctly per`sqlite3`query. **Metrics**: 3648→3709 tests (+61). 246 tools unchanged (v7.5 is cron-internal, no new user-facing tool). 371→376 source files (+5 new tuning modules). 210→214 test files (+4). 0 type errors. 0 new deps. Two additive ALTER TABLE migrations applied live via idempotent`addColumnIfMissing`. **Feature flags** (all default-safe): `TUNING_PARENT_SELECTION=score_child_prop`(new default),`TUNING_SELF_REVIEW=false`(opt-in),`TUNING_TRAJECTORY_MINE=false`(opt-in),`TUNING_SAFETY_KEYWORDS=""`(safety gate inert until populated),`TUNING_COOLDOWN_HOURS=24`. Existing `tune:run:dry`output shape unchanged when flags off. **Meta-finding** folded into new feedback memory: when an existing module has absorbed a roadmap's core patterns, a pre-plan sweep can shrink a "2-session from-scratch engine" into a "6-item surgical extension" — Stage 0 is cheap and often negative-cost. **Phase γ — v7.5 closes extended scope.** Remaining roadmap: Phase β-opt F10 (real-time crypto WS, optional, ~1 session parallel-capable), Phase δ F11 (live trading, gated on 30+d F8 paper record, earliest 2026-05-20), v7.5.1 deferrals (MAP-Elites, ASI diagnostics, Pareto specialization, triple-judge veto, Hermes compression), v7.6 deferrals (ACE 3-agent loop, claude-mem layer schema, latent-briefing KV compaction). |

ffmpeg-child kill is NOT wired until S1.1),`video_job_cleanup`(TTL cleanup +`du -sb` byte-count + safe jobId regex gate). **DB**: additive migration — 4 nullable cols (`manifest_json`, `frame_hash`, `cost_cents`, `ffmpeg_pid`) on `video_jobs`with PRAGMA-idempotent check; applied live, no reset. **Scope**: tightened bilingual regex — negative lookaheads on`video`/`render`/`mp4`(FPs on`"the video tag"`/`"render a React component"`/`"extract mp4 from pdf"`); required qualifier after `overlay`/`screenshot`/`tiktok`/`youtube`(Round-1 M4); added Round-2 W2`reel`singular +`reels de/para X`EN+ES arm (legit platform queries were silently dropping scope).`VIDEO_TOOLS`grew from 9 to 13. **Round 1 audit** (PASS WITH WARNINGS → fixed): 2 Critical — (C1)`SceneSpec.imagePath`typed-string, zero runtime validation, flowed verbatim into`ffmpeg -i <path>`enabling arbitrary-file-read (e.g.`/etc/shadow`) plus concat-file newline/quote injection into `file '…'`entries with`-safe 0`on; (C2)`video_job_cancel`read`row.ffmpeg_pid`but nothing in the codebase EVER WROTE it — tool description was a lie on the wire. 5 Major — (M1)`validateManifest`only checked half the fields, unsanitized`imageQuery`/`transitionToNext`/`voice`/`backgroundAsset`/`brandProfileId`landing in DB; (M2) size check ran AFTER`JSON.stringify`on hostile scene arrays; (M3)`video_transition_preview`leaked orphan`.mp4`on timeout/ffmpeg error; (M4) scope regex`overlay`/`screenshot`/bare-platform-names over-broad; (M5) zero tests for `video_transition_preview`/`runManifestPipeline`/compose_manifest happy path. 8 Minor — most addressable or deferred. **Round 2 audit** (PASS WITH WARNINGS → fixed): 0 Critical (no fix-for-fix regressions). 1 Major — (W1) impl plan still claimed worker-pool and ffmpeg_pid pid-kill wiring as S1 deliverables, contradicting code + tool description — classic readiness-gate dimension-3 drift per `feedback_v79_deferred_followups.md`. 1 Major — (W2) M4 fix regressed legitimate ES `reels de Instagram`/`hacer un reel para Instagram`/ EN singular`reel`. 6 Minor — n1 `\0`null-byte in forbidden-char list (fixed, cheap), others deferred/cosmetic. **Round 2 fixes**: added 3 deferred-with-trigger bullets to impl plan (worker-pool integration,`ffmpeg_pid`pid-kill wiring,`runManifestPipeline`unit tests); extended scope regex with singular-reel +`(?:reels?\|shorts?)\s+(?:de\|para\|of\|for)`EN+ES arms; added`\0`to`IMAGE_PATH_FORBIDDEN_CHARS`; 3 new positive tests for W2 regression guard + 1 new test for n1. **Live smoke**: full 3553-test suite green, typecheck clean, deploy.sh successful, `./mc-ctl db "PRAGMA table_info(video_jobs)"`confirms all 4 additive columns live (manifest_json/frame_hash/cost_cents/ffmpeg_pid), direct ffmpeg smoke with the`circleopen`xfade filter expression produced a valid 9,971-byte MP4 via ffprobe. **Metrics**: 3440→3553 tests (+113). 239→243 tools (+4, all deferred). 361→365 source files. 204→205 test files. 0 type errors. 0 new deps. | 2026-04-21                  | `bb78159`(1 commit)                                                                                                                                                                                            | **Session 95:`Due:`on non-task kinds maps to target_date — closes missing-due-date on NorthStar objective sync.** Jarvis (the LLM) diagnosed why objectives synced to COMMIT without their target date: the Xolo Rides objective file was written`Due: 2026-05-05`(user typo — objectives use`target*date`, only tasks have `due*date`), and `buildCreateFields`only assigned`due_date`for`kind === "task"`, silently dropping the value on non-task kinds. Jarvis stashed a proposed fix — add `"Due"`as an extra alias for`Target:`in`extractFieldWithAlias`at two sites — but the fix had a regression:`buildCreateFields`assigns`target_date`unconditionally, so a task file with its canonical`Due:`(what`buildFileContent:320`writes on every task with due\*date) would now POST with BOTH`target_date`and`due_date`, breaking the round-trip on every task push. **My fix**: kind-aware the two sites — `extractPatchFields`gets Due alias (safe via existing`allowed.includes("target_date")`guard which excludes tasks per`PUSH_FIELDS`); `buildCreateFields`wraps the whole target extraction+assignment in`if (kind !== "task")`, which also closes a pre-existing latent bug (stray `Target:`on a task file used to leak target_date into task POST). Two new tests: objective-Due→target_date propagates correctly (Xolo Rides shape), and a two-file regression covering both a canonical`Due:` task + a stray-`Target:`task asserting neither leaks target_date. QA audit: clean PASS, no criticals, two low-priority drift-risk recommendations deferred (alias-precedence comment, extracting shared kind→column mapping). Metrics: 3396→3398 tests (+2). 0 type errors. 0 new deps. Deploy required. | 2026-04-21                  | `2de85a1`(1 commit)                                                                                                                                                                                            | **Session 94: canary false-positive fix — scheduled/ritual email-miss detector stops flagging normal chat traffic.** User reported 18:00 canary broadcast "8 missed email sends in morning briefings". Diagnosis: query`metadata LIKE '%gmail_send%'`matched any task whose tool allowlist included`gmail_send`. The default chat task allowlist includes it — so every `completed_with_concerns` chat message ("Lista mi NorthStar", "Continúa", etc.) got counted as a missed email delivery. All 8 flagged tasks were user chat, zero were briefings; the one real scheduled email task in the window (`32f27f88`EurekaMD) had correctly sent and was not flagged. At threshold > 2 the alert fired daily on normal chatter, becoming load-bearing noise. **Fix**: rewrote the filter to match only tasks \_designed\* to email — metadata tags containing`"scheduled"` (dynamic ritual tasks) OR ritual title prefixes (`Morning briefing —`, `Nightly close —`, `Signal intelligence —`, `Weekly review —`, `Market morning scan —`) exported as `RITUAL_EMAIL_TITLE_PREFIXES`const; widened status filter from`completed_with_concerns`alone to`IN ('completed_with_concerns', 'failed')`— the`failed`case is the retry-already-exhausted state the canary most wants to catch (dispatcher downgrades rituals that miss`requiredTools.gmail_send`to`failed`); defensive `Array.isArray(toolCalls)`before`.includes('gmail_send')`since swarm-runner emits a number not an array. **Validated against live DB**: last 24h = 1 candidate, 0 misses (vs. old 14 candidates, 13 misses); 7-day window = 2 candidates, 0 misses. **Audit R1**: 5 Warnings (missing`Market morning scan`title prefix, blind spot on`failed`status, no adversarial test for the`"scheduled"`substring, ritual prefixes duplicated between ritual files and canary SQL with no compile-time link, swarm-runner`toolCalls` shape mismatch). All closed. **Tests**: 6 new — chat-with-gmail-in-allowlist excluded, scheduled-missing-gmail counted, ritual-titles-with-empty-metadata counted, ritual-with-FAILED-status counted (the retry-exhausted case), scheduled-that-sent excluded, adversarial-sneaky-metadata excluded (`tags: ["schedule-followup"]`, description contains the word "scheduled"). Metrics: 3390→3396 tests (+6). 0 type errors. 0 new deps. Deploy required (dist/ bundle). | 2026-04-21                  | `5bf6df1`(1 commit)                                                                                                                                                                                            | **Session 93: fix Jarvis-detected`## Descripción`sync bug (shipped broken) + unblock`jarvis_dev pr`gate.** Jarvis (the LLM) spotted that`buildCreateFields`was pushing`description=null`to COMMIT whenever an objective's description lived under a`## Descripción`section heading instead of an inline`Description:`line. He diagnosed the bug correctly, wrote a branch + a new helper`extractDescriptionSection`, staged tests, and tried `jarvis_dev action="pr"`— the gate rejected with "3 tests failed on main" so he concluded the failures were preexisting and backed off. Top-level Claude reviewed and found **two separate problems**: (1) Jarvis's`extractDescriptionSection`regex used`\z`— a Ruby/Python end-of-string anchor that JavaScript silently treats as literal`z`. For the Xolo Rides objective (description has no 'z' character) the match failed and the helper returned null — the exact bug it was meant to fix, shipping broken. 29/29 module tests passed because no test exercised the section-heading path. (2) `jarvis_dev`'s test gate `TIMEOUT_MS=120000`against a suite that runs ~106s on idle gave <15% headroom — under load,`execFileSync`throws`ETIMEDOUT`and the catch branch's fail-summary regex misparsed killed-mid-run vitest output as "N tests failed", producing the phantom "preexisting failures" message; verified independently that main was in fact clean. **Fix ships both together**: replaced`\z`with proper JS end-of-string via`(?:^ | \n)##[ \t]+(?:Descripci[oó]n | Description)[ \t]\*\n([\s\S]\*?)(?=\n##[^#] | $)` (no `m` flag — `$`must mean EOS;`[^#]`rejects`###`sub-heading prefix-collision). Raised`TIMEOUT*MS`120→300s (3x headroom), added`GIT_TIMEOUT_MS=60000`replacing hardcoded 30/10s overrides on push/commit/pull/gh, distinguish`ETIMEDOUT`/`SIGTERM`/`SIGKILL`from real test failure in`actionTest`catch so future killed runs don't misreport, tightened fail-summary regex to`/Tests\s+(\d+)\s+failed(?:\s+\|\s+(\d+)\s+passed)?/`(matches vitest's real summary line, no false-match on mid-run progress). Kept Jarvis's useful pieces: case-insensitive`extractField` (`i`flag),`extractFieldWithAlias(canonical, ...aliases)`for snake*case fallback, test POST-URL filter by`/\/rest\/v1\/(visions\|goals\|objectives\|tasks)\b/`to exclude pgvector contamination. **Audit round 1**: 1 Major (the fixed regex still prefix-truncated at`###`sub-headings — fixed), 3 Warnings (SIGKILL not classified as timeout,`push`still hardcoded 30s overriding the new constant, redundant`"status"`alias now that case-insensitive`"Status"`covers it). All closed. Added 3 new tests:`## Descripción`extraction end-to-end,`### Subsection`survives inside the section (regression guard for the`##`prefix bug), lowercase keys +`target_date:`snake_case alias. Metrics: 3387→3390 tests (+3). 0 type errors. 0 new deps. **The meta-lesson**: Jarvis did good diagnosis work but lost trust in his own PR gate's error message and over-trusted it as ground truth ("tests fail on main"). The gate was lying. Both sides got fixed.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| 2026-04-21                  | `077f2d6`(5 commits)                                                                                                                                                                                           | **Session 92: NorthStar bidirectional create propagation + Jarvis-can-delete scope fix + INDEX.md post-sync fidelity.** Three interlocking issues surfaced from live Jarvis use: (1) scope didn't route delete verbs to`northstar_write`so Jarvis responded "I can't delete NorthStar entries, only db.mycommit.net can" — fixed across regex fallback + LLM classifier + accented-clitic coverage; (2) INDEX.md was rebuilt from the pre-sync`commitData`snapshot so after a propagation sync it still listed just-deleted ghosts — fixed by sourcing INDEX from post-sync local state via`enumerateLocal(kind)`with commitData metadata fallback to local file parse; (3) sync had no local→COMMIT create path so "crea una meta" →`jarvis_file_write`→ run sync → skipped → Jarvis escalated to asking for Supabase credentials — fixed with a real two-phase sync: phase 1 POSTs new locals to Supabase with client-generated UUIDs +`modified_by: "system"`, phase 2 is the existing LWW + delete-propagation. Parent FK resolution accepts UUID or accent-insensitive title lookup ("Visión" / "Vision" / "visión" all resolve). Same-sync parent+child chaining works (vision processed before goal in KINDS order, injected into commitData so children resolve). Three audit passes caught: round 1 — two Majors (ordering + accent-insensitivity); round 2 — one Critical fix-for-fix regression (my skip-retry branch caused phase 2 to DELETE the successfully-created remote record); round 3 — three cosmetic warnings (unchanged double-count, test-assertion strength). Self-heal path added for stranded files: if the prior run POSTed but crashed before local file rewrite, next sync detects (journal + commit + matching-by-id) and calls `populateCommitIdInFile`to repair in place — converts what was a silent-delete path into a recoverable state. Two latent regex bugs caught during smoke:`extractField`'s `\s*`consumed`\n`(empty`COMMIT_ID:`line returned the next line's value);`populateCommitIdInFile`'s `\s*.\*$`replace gobbled the following`Status:`line. Both switched to`[ \t]*`/`[^\n]*`. 5 commits on main: `d429b3c`(feature) →`0bcfe99`(R1 M1+M2) →`e1eb180`(R2 C1 self-heal) →`077f2d6` (R3 warnings). Metrics: 3323→3387 tests (+64 across the day — includes scope classifier-path, INDEX post-sync, create-propagation, self-heal, accent-match, orphan-journal retry, content-preservation). 0 type errors. 0 new deps. Live smoke end-to-end: Xolo Rides goal the user created pre-fix now on COMMIT with id 4e1bf37a, linked to Libertad Financiera via vision\*id. Tool descriptions rewritten twice — once to stop Jarvis panicking on local-only creates (Part A), once to document the real bidirectional-create capability (Part B). Earlier in the day also: v7.3 Phases 2+3+5 shipped (SEO Telemetry + AI Overview + GEO Depth), NorthStar LWW sync rewritten (10 commits total for the day across Phase γ + sync subsystem). | 2026-04-21                  | `TBD`                                                                                                                                                                                                          | **Session 91: v7.3 Phase 2 + 3 + 5 — SEO Telemetry + AI Overview Monitoring + GEO Depth.** 4 new tools shipped + `seo_page_audit` extended. **Phase 5 (GEO Depth)** — `seo_robots_audit` (RFC 9309 parser across 28-entry AI-bot catalog, classifies allowed/blocked/unmentioned + contradiction warnings like "GPTBot blocked but Google-Extended open"), `seo_llms_txt_generate` (sitemap → llmstxt.org-format markdown, URL clustering by path segment, 5000-URL cap), extended `seo_page_audit` with Princeton KDD 2024 content-quality signals (cite/stat density per 1k words, quote presence, Flesch-Kincaid grade, keyword stuffing). **Phase 2 (SEO Telemetry)** — `seo_telemetry` combines PageSpeed Insights (Core Web Vitals + Lighthouse) with Google Search Console (28-day query-level clicks/impressions/CTR/position); graceful degrade on either side; optional `site_url` param for GSC `sc-domain:` Domain properties; Intl-based dateOffset in LA tz (GSC's reporting tz, not UTC). **Phase 3 (AI Overview)** — `ai_overview_track` fetches Google SERP via stealth browser + fingerprint-injector, multi-heuristic AI-Overview detection (data-attrid + aria-label + proximity-bounded text signal within 20KB top-slice), graceful degrade on anti-bot block with SerpAPI-fallback suggestion, semaphore of 2 to cap concurrent Chromium spawns. New tables: `seo_telemetry_snapshots`, `ai_overview_tracking`. **Round 1 audit** caught 3 Criticals (validateOutboundUrl returns string\|null, not throws — `try/catch` pattern I used was a no-op letting SSRF through in 3 tools) + 6 Majors + 12 Warnings + 8 Deferrables — all Criticals + Majors closed, most Warnings closed, cheap Deferrables closed. **Round 2 audit** caught 1 Major (detectAIOverview co-occurrence still loose at 80KB scope — organic SERP results with `data-attrid=` false-positived) — fixed by shrinking top-slice to 20KB + proximity-bounded regex (within 2000 chars). 2 new SSRF-guard tests per tool + recursive-sitemap threat model test. **Live smoke**: robots audit clean, llms.txt handles no-sitemap gracefully, PSI hits documented 429 rate limit unkeyed (graceful), AI overview headless Chromium OOM on this VPS (graceful, expected per v7.1 precedent). Metrics: 3323→3364 tests (+41). 232→236 tools (+4). 0 type errors. 0 new deps. Additive migration applied live. **Phase γ progress: 7/13 done** (v7.1 + v7.2 + v7.10 + v7.11 + v7.12 + v7.14 + v7.13 + v7.3 P1/P2/P3/P5 collectively ≈ 7 "items" done; v7.3 P4 Marketing Ads + v7.4 Video + v7.4.3 HTML-DSL + v7.5 Skill Evolution remain). | 2026-04-21                  | `4c58b5f` (3 commits)                                                                                                                                                                                          | **Session 90: NorthStar↔COMMIT sync — 2-way LWW with tombstones + field-remove semantics.** 3 commits: `03f0bd9` initial rewrite → `058aadf` closes qa-auditor round 1 (2 Majors + 6 Warnings + 3 Deferrables) → `4c58b5f` closes round 2 (field-remove + cosmetic counter). Fixes a 3-bug compound where the old sync let `db.mycommit.net` silently overwrite local edits. **Root causes**: (1) file-level rebuild in Step 1 (`buildFileContent`) clobbered every field including notes/dates whenever COMMIT differed; (2) `!didPull` gated Step 2 so once any field pulled, nothing could push; (3) the `updated_at` timestamp used for LWW was bumped on every sync-induced write, masquerading as an edit. **Also discovered via SQL probe**: COMMIT table triggers auto-bump `last_edited_at` on every PATCH — so timestamp-only LWW can't work; pivoted to `modified_by == "user"` + journal-stored `last_commit_edited_at` comparison as the unsynced-edit gate. **New**: `user_edit_time` column on `jarvis_files` (bumped only by real file*write/file*edit, not sync upserts via new `skipUserEdit` opts); `northstar_sync_state` journal table (commit_id PK, per-kind, tombstone tracking); rewritten `northstar-sync.ts` with record-level LWW + hard DELETE propagation both ways + bootstrap mode (empty journal → create-across-gap, no deletes). **Bug caught in live smoke**: two tasks with identical title "Probar en servidor local" collided on slug-only paths during bootstrap; run 2 saw orphaned journal row and issued spurious DELETE on COMMIT. Task restored from pre-sync DB backup; paths made collision-proof via `{slug}--{id8}.md`. **17 unit tests** (bootstrap, LWW 3 branches, delete propagation 3 branches, no-op stability, slug collision, PATCH-representation journal capture, modified_by isolation, field-diff, local-only-skip, concurrent reentrancy, field-remove on clearables, title/status preservation). **Round 1 audit** caught 2 Majors (PATCH response untested, modified_by gate not isolated) + 6 Warnings (timestamp format mixing, silent skip paths, hardcoded `order` column, reentrancy unprotected, bootstrap transition note, no dedicated skip test) + 3 Deferrables (full-field PATCH noise, sequential fetchTable, redundant tombstone on bootstrap) — all closed in `058aadf`. **Round 2 audit** (on `058aadf`) verified all 11 round-1 findings ✅ CLOSED with no fix-for-fix regressions; remaining 2 Warnings were semantic decisions, not bugs. **Field-remove semantics** (`4c58b5f`) enable "delete a Priority/Notes/Description/Target/Due line → next sync nulls the remote value." Title and Status preserved to prevent accidental nulling of core fields. **Live smoke across 3 deploys**: bootstrap pulled 80 + seeded journal, post-audit runs all clean no-op. Metrics: 3306→3323 tests (+17). 0 type errors. 0 new deps. Additive migration applied live at service boot. | 2026-04-18                  | `TBD`                                                                                                                                                                                                          | **Session 78: Weekly-first + watchlist auto-seed — F7.5 precursor.** Operator lock: F7 and F7.5 run on weekly bars only (5× less API drain, matches intended rebalance cadence, 10yr history in ≤520 weekly bars). Infrastructure sprint to unblock F7.5 (S10). **AV adapter** — new `fetchWeekly()` using `TIME_SERIES_WEEKLY_ADJUSTED`. **DataLayer** — new `getWeekly()` + generalized private `dbBars(symbol, interval, lookback)` with ordering-invariant comment; `WEEKLY_TTL_MS=5d`, `WEEKLY_L2_FRESHNESS_FLOOR=0.95`. **F7 defaults** — `windowM` 250→104, `windowD` 20→8 (weekly-bar semantics); `alpha.ts` period-query switched to `interval='weekly'`; padDays rebased with `7×` ratio + `Math.max(60, …)` floor. **New watchlist-seed module** (`src/finance/watchlist-seed.ts`): `seedSymbol()` idempotent-skip when sufficient rows, otherwise fetches weekly + `detectAllSignals` + `persistSignals`. **Auto-seed hook** in `marketWatchlistAddTool`: equity/etf/fx/commodity auto-seeds after insert; failures surfaced but don't roll back the row. **New `marketWatchlistReseedTool`** for manual refresh; scope regex extended for `reseed/refresh/refrescar/historial` ES+EN. **persistBars transactional** — wrapped in `db.transaction(...)` for 100×+ speedup on batch inserts. **qa-auditor rounds 1+2**: Round 1: 3 Critical + 5 Warning closed (C1 seedSymbol sync-throw guard; C2 persistBars non-transactional fsync thrash; C3 no end-to-end test; W1 reseed vocab; W2 padDays floor; W4 TTL + L2 freshness floor). Round 2: all 6 fixes verified, zero regressions, zero new Critical/Warning. **Live seed**: 10 symbols (SPY/QQQ/AAPL/MSFT/NVDA/TSLA/GLD/TLT/JPM/XLE) × 520 weekly bars = 5,200 bars + 1,260 signals in 12s. History: 2016-05-06 → 2026-04-17. **Live F7 smoke on weekly data**: `alpha_run` produced N=42 active signals (vs 4 on daily), N_effective=11.123, 42ms, Σ\|w\|=1.000000 exact. Top: MSFT RSI (short), SPY MACD (long), TLT volume spike (long). **Metrics:** 2605 → 2625 tests (+20). 326 → 329 source files. 176 → 178 test files. 200 → 201 tools. 134 → 135 deferred. 0 type errors. Zero new deps. Phase β S10 (F7.5 CPCV + PBO + DSR) unblocked; plan at `docs/planning/phase-beta/20-f7.5-impl-plan.md` amended for weekly-first. | 2026-04-17                  | `TBD`                                                                                                                                                                                                          | **Session 76: v7.13 Structured PDF Ingestion (Option B, MinerU-free) — Phase β S5 shipped.** Fifth Phase β session. Branch `phase-beta/v7.13-pdf-structured-ingestion`. **Option B scope**: minimum-viable pre-F7 structured ingestion using existing `@opendataloader/pdf` (Java parser, already in core deps) instead of the MinerU Python service proposed in v7.13 roadmap. **Rationale**: MinerU is a 1.5-session Docker + systemd + ML-models project; F7's alpha-combination algorithm doesn't hard-depend on ML-quality extraction (numeric signal series from F3/F5/F6/F6.5 are sufficient); zero-new-deps invariant held across S1-S5; MinerU can ship as v7.13-polish if F7/F8 telemetry shows retrieval quality ceiling. `chunk_position`. pgUpsert + pgBatchUpsert row-builders conditionally spread the new fields (safe forward-compat with unmigrated Supabase). External migration SQL documented for operator: `ALTER TABLE kb_entries ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS modality text DEFAULT 'text' / parent_doc_id uuid / section_path jsonb DEFAULT '[]' / chunk_position int` + 2 indexes. **New `src/kb/pdf-structured-ingest.ts`** (~270 LOC): `structureMarkdown` pure transform (exposed for tests + callers with markdown in hand); `sectionizeAndExtract` tracks markdown heading stack producing section_path per block; `looksLikeTableStart` + `isSeparatorRow` detect pipe-format tables; `chunkText` splits at paragraph boundaries then sentence boundaries (with CJK-aware `[.!?。！?]` + hard-slice fallback per audit C1); `buildPath` namespace/doc/section/position; tables kept as single chunks (preserve structure); max_chunks cap (default 500) with `truncated` flag. **New `src/tools/builtin/kb-ingest.ts`** (~230 LOC) — 2 tools: `kb_ingest_pdf_structured` (pdf_path → extract → chunk → embed sequentially → pgBatchUpsert; pgvector disabled guard; UUID validation on parent_doc_id per audit W3; migration-applied hint when all inserts fail per audit S1) + `kb_batch_insert` (pre-parsed entries path for programmatic ingestion). New **`kb_ingest` scope group** with activation pattern requiring verb (ingest/parse/extract/import) + strong file signal (pdf, .pdf, 10-k, earnings, filing, research paper) per audit C2; plus `kb_ingest`/`kb_batch` tool-verb shortcut. Both tools in WRITE_TOOLS (persist to pgvector). **qa-auditor PASS WITH WARNINGS**; closed 5 findings before merge: **C1** `chunkText` silently violated 1500-char limit on CJK / sentence-terminator-free content → added hard-slice fallback + expanded sentence regex to `.!?。！?`; **C2** scope regex over-matched "extract data from the document" → narrowed to require pdf/.pdf/10-k/earnings/filing/research paper OR direct tool verb, plus 4 negative test cases; **W1** tool schema enum listed `equation                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | image_caption`modalities that Option B doesn't produce → narrowed to`text
