                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | table`(MinerU polish will re-add); **W3**`parent_doc_id` accepted arbitrary string → UUID regex validation before ingest call; **S1** summary gave no hint when migration unapplied → conditional "confirm schema migration was applied" line when success=0 + failed>0. Deferred info items: W2 sequential→batch embedding, W4 cosmetic comment, R1-R3 recommendations. **Live smoke**: structureMarkdown on a 10-K-shaped markdown fixture produced 4 chunks (3 text + 1 table), section_path nested 1-3 deep, chunk_position sequential, table preserved verbatim in pipe format, parent_doc_id auto-generated as UUID. **Metrics:** 2464 → 2508 tests (+44: 25 ingester + 14 tools + 4 scope + 1 write-tools-sync). 318 → 321 source files (+3). 169 → 171 test files (+2). 195 → 197 tools (+2). 0 type errors. Zero new dependencies. Deployed live. **v7 Phase β: 7 of 12 done.** Next: **S6–S7 = F7 Alpha Combination Engine (2.5 sessions, single-focus)** — the algorithmic core. v7.13 MinerU polish deferred to post-v7.0-launch follow-up if F7/F8 retrieval telemetry warrants it. |
| 2026-04-17                  | `853ce8f`                                                                                                                                                                                                      | **Session 75: v7.0 F6 Prediction Markets + Whale Tracker + F6.5 Sentiment — Phase β S4 shipped.** Fourth Phase β session. Branch `phase-beta/f6-f6.5-external-signals`. 3 additive tables (`prediction_markets`, `whale_trades`, `sentiment_readings`). New `src/finance/prediction-markets.ts` — PolymarketAdapter for Gamma + CLOB read endpoints (no auth needed): fetchActiveMarkets, fetchEventGroup (with negRisk propagation to all markets in multi-outcome events), fetchMarketBySlug, fetchRecentTrades. Strict zod-shaped normalizer rejects malformed entries. persistMarkets with ON CONFLICT upsert on (source, market_id). New `src/finance/whales.ts` — extractWhalesFromTrades with $5,000 default "clearly not retail" threshold (overridable), attribution to taker address, persistence + queryRecentWhales with market/hours/minSize/limit filters. New `src/finance/sentiment.ts` — 3 adapters (alternative.me Fear & Greed, CoinMarketCap F&G with pro-key-or-public fallback, Binance premiumIndex funding rates for BTC/ETH/SOL), getSentimentSnapshot composite aggregator with per-source graceful degradation (Promise.all with catch-per-promise → degradedSources surface), classifyFearGreed 5-zone classification, contrarian interpretation at extremes. persistSentimentReadings with manual SELECT-then-INSERT dedup (SQLite NULL-symbol edge). 3 new tools wired to `finance` scope: `prediction_markets` (list/search/detail with event grouping), `whale_trades` (DB-only default + optional fetch_live via Polymarket CLOB), `sentiment_snapshot` (composite F&G + funding interpretation). Scope regex extended with 3 new activation patterns (prediction / whales / sentiment) ES+EN. New `cmcProApiKey` config binding (optional `CMC_PRO_API_KEY` env). Rate-limit ceilings added: polymarket 60/min (raised per audit W5), alternative_me 30/min, coinmarketcap 30/min, binance 120/min. **qa-auditor PASS WITH WARNINGS**; closed 4 findings before merge: **W1** CMC pro key was dead code → added to Config interface + loader + sentiment.ts uses typed getConfig; **W2** sentimentSnapshotTool never persisted → now writes F&G + funding readings to sentiment_readings on every call (F7 consumption path); **W3** scope false positives on bare "sentiment"/"sentimental" + "probabilidad" → narrowed with negative lookahead `(?!al)` excluding "sentimental"/"sentimentales" while preserving bare "sentiment" (accepts same false-positive tradeoff as "expansion" in F5); **W5** Polymarket 30/min ceiling self-throttled → raised to 60/min with comment. Deferred info items: R1-R7 (fetch_live test, category filter test, whale_trades cleanup ritual for F9, etc.). Live smoke: `prediction_markets` returned 5 real Polymarket markets with volumes+prices+resolution dates; `sentiment_snapshot` returned Fear & Greed = **21 (Extreme Fear)** from alternative.me (CMC + Binance geoblocked on VPS — graceful degradation surfaced in degradedSources + interpretation flagged "contrarian bullish"); `whale_trades` returned empty-DB with fetch_live hint. **Metrics:** 2413 → 2464 tests (+51: 15 polymarket + 9 whales + 14 sentiment + 10 tool + 3 scope), 166 → 169 test files, 315 → 318 source files, 192 → 195 tools. 0 type errors. Zero new dependencies. Deployed live. **v7 Phase β: 6 of 12 done (F1+F2+F3+F4+F5+F6+F6.5; remaining v7.13, F7, F7.5, F8, F9).** Explicit non-goals deferred per scope: Kalshi trading, SEC EDGAR insider filings, liquidation heatmaps, stablecoin flows, Polymarket write API (F11 territory). Next: S5 = v7.13 Structured PDF ingestion (pre-F7 enabler). Flagged follow-up: Binance funding alternate source (Bybit/OKX) for VPS-geoblocked environments; whale_trades retention ritual for F9. |
| 2026-04-17 | `e15273b` | **Session 74: v7.0 F5 Macro Regime + F3 Signal Detector — Phase β S3 shipped.** Third Phase β session, bundled F5+F3 per ordering-map Window B. Branch `phase-beta/f5-f3-macro-and-signals`. New `src/finance/macro.ts` (~200 LOC) rules-based regime classifier over 8 macro series (FEDFUNDS + CPI + UNEMPLOYMENT + TREASURY_YIELD×2 via AV, VIXCLS + ICSA + M2SL via FRED). 5 regimes: expansion / tightening / recession_risk / recovery / mixed — each with hard (0.85) / soft (0.55) / mixed (0.3) confidence. Trend helpers: linearSlope, classifyTrend (rising/falling/flat/normalizing with magnitude-scaled threshold), yoyChange (nearest-earlier-1yr match), seriesStalenessDays. Yield-curve alignment uses nearest-earlier-t2 lookup (not exact-date Map join — audit C1 fix so daily-vs-monthly cadence mismatch doesn't silently produce flat curves). Severity-ranked conflict resolution when multiple hard rules fire (recession_risk > tightening > expansion > recovery — audit W3). New `src/finance/signals.ts` (~280 LOC) 6 pure-function detectors over MarketBar[] + F2 indicators: detectMaCrossover (50/200 SMA golden/death cross), detectRsiExtremes (70/30 thresholds, fires only at zone-entry), detectMacdCrossover (histogram sign change), detectBollingerBreakout (20/2σ, prevInside guard), detectVolumeSpike (z-score > 2σ over 20-bar window), detectPriceThreshold (now operator-aware direction per audit I4). detectAllSignals aggregator + persistSignals (transactional INSERT OR SKIP via SELECT-then-INSERT dedup on symbol+type+triggered_at). New tools: `macro_regime` (8-series pull → classifier → formatted snapshot with staleness warning when ≥6/8 empty, audit W4), `market_signals` (single-symbol or whole-watchlist scan, 50-symbol cap + 3-consecutive-rate-limit early exit per audit W5, persists firings to market_signals table). Scope regex extended with 2 new activation patterns: macro vocab (yield curve, fed funds, inflation, unemployment, recession, VIX, CPI, GDP, M2, expansion, tightening, recovery ES+EN, audit I1) + signals vocab (signal(s), crossover(s), breakout(s), divergence, confluence, reversal, gap up/down ES+EN, audit I1). `auto-persist.ts` Rule 2b: `market_signals` added. **qa-auditor PASS WITH WARNINGS**; closed 8 findings: C1 yield-curve cadence mismatch, C2 JSDoc/impl doc drift (SMA vs EMA), W1 normalizing threshold scaled by magnitude, W2 MACD zero-grazing (ultimately retained sign-change logic — the strict product-based approach broke legitimate tests on synthetic data; documented tradeoff), W3 severity ranking, W4 staleness warnings, W5 scan cap + rate-limit early exit, W6 6 new positive scope tests + 1 negative case, I1 vocabulary gaps, I4 price_threshold direction. Live smoke: `macro_regime` returned `recession_risk (0.55)` with yield curve at -4.23 (AV data quirk — fedFunds stale 26k days flagged via audit-W4 warning surface); `market_signals SPY` detected 14 firings persisted (3 rsi_extreme oversold, 3 bollinger breakout, 1 macd_crossover bullish, 2 volume_spike, 1 overbought rsi, etc.). **Metrics:** 2361 → 2413 tests (+52), 164 → 166 test files, 313 → 315 source files, 190 → 192 tools. 0 type errors. | 2026-04-17                  | `TBD`                                                                                                                                                                                                          | **Session 71 Pt B: live API 400 incident + surrogate-safety hardening.** Production 400s from Claude API (`invalid_request_error: "no low surrogate in string: line 1 column ~70000"`) — every inbound task returned `1 turn, 0 tool calls, $0, ~~200ms, tokens=0`for ~30 min starting 17:24 UTC. Root cause:`router.ts`thread-buffer truncation`response.slice(0, 3000)`cut an emoji (surrogate pair, 2 UTF-16 code units) at the char-3000 boundary, stranding a lone high surrogate in the in-memory thread. Every subsequent prompt carried the orphan → JSON validator in Anthropic API rejected every request mid-body (column varied 68842 → 73551 across runs as the thread grew). **Fix shipped:** new`src/lib/unicode-safe.ts`(90 lines, 2 helpers, 18 tests) —`safeSlice(s, n)`backs off one code unit if the cut would strand a high surrogate;`sanitizeSurrogates(s)`replaces any lone surrogate with U+FFFD (zero-alloc fast path for clean strings). Applied at: **(primary defense)**`src/inference/claude-sdk.ts`— sanitize`systemPrompt`+`prompt`at the SDK boundary so any upstream truncation is scrubbed before the JSON serializer sees it; also swapped the 600-char tool-result truncation in`flattenMessagesForSdk`to`safeSlice`. **(source sites)** `src/messaging/router.ts`— both`THREAD_RESPONSE_CAP`slices now use`safeSlice`(prevents in-memory thread re-poisoning), plus day-log 500-char slice (durable jarvis_files write) and checkpoint-recovery 2000+500-char slices (re-injected into prompts after restarts).`src/memory/background-extractor.ts`(4 slice sites) +`src/memory/auto-persist.ts`(2 slice sites) — closes the OpenAI-adapter bypass path where enrichment/extraction prompts don't go through the claude-sdk sanitizer. **QA audit:** ran qa-auditor on first pass, PASS-WITH-WARNINGS — zero CRITICAL, 4 MAJOR (M2 day log, M3 extractors ×4 + auto-persist ×2, M4 checkpoint recovery) all closed in a single follow-up edit pass. Added 6 edge-case tests (lone low surrogate at position 0, consecutive lones with no separator, valid-pair-after-lone, empty string, 200KB clean-string perf smoke, JSON round-trip). Verified`conversations`+`jarvis_files`DB columns had zero pre-existing orphan surrogates (poison was in-memory only, cleared by restart). **Docs:** session 71 roadmap updates shipped separately in`dbce8d9`— 4 new sprints anchored (v7.3 Phase 5 GEO Depth, v7.13 Structured PDF Ingestion, v7.14 Infographic Generation, F11 Live Polymarket Trading Engine) + 7 scope enrichments folded (v7.1 QuantAgent, v7.5 ASI-Evolve, F6 polymarket Stage A, F7 de Prado methodology, F7.5 CPCV/PBO/Deflated Sharpe, F8 Nautilus research-to-live parity + VenueAdapter, F11 Nautilus advanced order types). Total roadmap scope: ~27 → 33.5 sessions across 4 tracks. **Metrics:** 2255 → 2273 tests (+18), 155 → 156 test files, 0 type errors. 2 deploys, zero rollbacks. Service restarted cleanly at 18:11:06 UTC. **Latent issue flagged (not fixed this session):** runs with`output`starting with`"API Error:"`are marked`status='completed'`because the runner finished — this masks API-level failures in`mc-ctl stats`. Needs a runner-level classifier that promotes API-error responses to `status='failed'`. | 2026-04-14                  | `68d4731`                                                                                                                                                                                                      | **Session 67 pt 11: v7.7.4 stale-artifact-prune audit follow-ups — live ship-blocker caught by qa-auditor**. Ran qa-auditor on commit`36c8002`(the v7.7.3 NanoClaw adoption) focused on the new`scheduleStaleArtifactPrune()`ritual — the other adoption items (.npmrc, env var, docs) were trivial. The audit returned FAIL with a CRITICAL live ship-blocker: Docker's`--filter name=^mc-`is NOT a prefix-anchor regex, it's a substring match. Combined with no post-filter, the ritual would have matched`mc-grafana`(exited 10 days ago on this host, verified live) and deleted the production monitoring container on the first`:17`cron tick after deployment.`mc-prometheus`+`mc-node-exporter`were one reboot-into-exited-state away from the same fate. Fixed with ~45 minutes of margin before the next cron fire. **Seven findings closed:** **C1** strict post-filter`/^mc-[a-z0-9-]+-\d{13}$/`matches only the exact shape from`generateContainerName()`— verified live that`re.test("mc-grafana")`returns`false`and`re.test("mc-task-1776000000000")`returns`true`. **C2** Docker CLI `name=`filter dropped entirely — never trusted for safety, TS regex is sole guard. Query now uses`--filter status=exited`only. **M1** per-id`docker container rm`with isolated try/catch; previous spread`rm ...ids`aborted the whole batch on first failure. Now each container attempted individually, successes/failures summarized. **M2**`DOCKER*UNAVAILABLE_RE`widened to silence`ENOENT`, `spawn docker`, `permission denied while trying to connect` — previous regex (`no such | not found | cannot connect`) missed these and would have logged errors every hour in environments without docker. **M3** `PRUNE_BATCH_CAP = 50`per cycle prevents E2BIG arg-list overflow on crash loops. **M4** extracted the filter as exported pure function`selectStaleContainersForPrune(listOutput, nowMs)`; 12 new tests cover mc-grafana/mc-prometheus/mc-node-exporter protection, valid runner matches, age gate, parse failures, batch cap, invalid chars. **MN1** age gate raised 1h → 6h so long-running heavy-runner tasks can drain stdout after exit without racing the next cron tick. 2225 → 2237 tests (+12). Service restarted on PID 4068497 before next cron tick. **Lesson: always run `docker ps`on the deployment target BEFORE shipping a reaper — the real host state would have caught this immediately.\*\* Three cheap adopt-now items from the NanoClaw v1.2.52 review + a meta-task. **Adoption:** (1)`.npmrc`with`minimum-release-age=10080`(7 days) — supply-chain defense against fresh-malicious npm releases (event-stream pattern). (2)`CLAUDE_CODE_AUTO_COMPACT_WINDOW=165000`in claude-sdk env passthrough — compact earlier than the 200k model ceiling to preserve context fidelity, operator override honored, SDK-enforced 100k-1M range. Adopted from NanoClaw v1.2.50. (3)`scheduleStaleArtifactPrune()`ritual — hourly sweep of exited`mc-*`containers older than 1h, defensive safety net for the`--rm`hook missing after process crashes, fires at`:17`to avoid cron collisions, silently skips if docker daemon unreachable. **NOTE: this shipped with a critical filter bug caught by the qa-auditor in pt 11 below — see that entry for the live blocker and fix.** **v7.5 upstream sweep directive:** new`memory/feedback*v75_upstream_sweep_directive.md`(130 lines) + a "Blocker" row in`docs/V7-ROADMAP.md`v7.5 scope table. Before any v7.5 (GEPA+SkillClaw) implementation starts, a full upstream sweep across 48+`reference\**.md`files must run, with the 10 core skill-evolution repos (GEPA, SkillClaw, Hyperagents, Hermes, ACE, Memoria, claude-mem, mempalace, Superpowers, context-engineering) read in depth via parallel`Agent subagent_type=Explore`calls. ~4 hour budget as its own half-day session. Without the sweep the skill-evolution engine is built against stale reference material from months earlier. Three durable reminders: dedicated memory file, MEMORY.md index entry, V7-ROADMAP.md v7.5 section callout + Blocker row. **Explore agent finding:** parallel Hermes v0.8 PR study (#6120, #5414, #5931) returned a crucial finding — the "self-optimized tool-use guidance" harness in Hermes v0.8 does NOT exist as reusable code. It's a marketing frame around hand-edited prompt constants and ad-hoc manual evaluations. The methodology is sound but there's no harness to lift. **Impact on v7.5 scope:** original estimate assumed we could lift the harness and cut v7.5 from 2 sessions to 1.5. That optimism is withdrawn — v7.5 stays at 2 sessions. Silver lining: we'll build TypeScript-first, SkillClaw-aware from day one instead of forking a GPT-tuned Python script. 2168 → 2225 tests (+57). MEMORY.md index refreshed for`feedback_prometheus_upstream`(130L, v0.9.0) and`reference_nanoclaw_upstream`(120L, v1.2.52). | 2026-04-14                  | `4e0f62e`                                                                                                                                                                                                      | **Session 67 pt 9: audit follow-ups marathon — v7.7 MCP security + Layer 4b widening + Rumi chain hardening + claude-sdk null coerce**. Ran three qa-auditor passes in parallel on the previous 24h of commits (v7.7 MCP server, Layer 4b cooldown guard, Rumi 4-bug chain). Findings: 4 critical + 11 major + assorted minor across all three. User directive "fix all, no deferrals" → shipped every finding in two logical commits. **Commit A — v7.7 MCP hardening\*\* (`src/api/mcp-server/`, `src/db/schema.sql`, `mc-ctl`): C1 `mc-ctl mcp-token create`client*name whitelisted to`^[A-Za-z0-9*.-]{1,64}$`(previously only`sed 's/\\'/\\'\\''/g'`with latent SQLi path through bash). C2 parameterized`datetime('now', ?)`binds in`jarvis_recent_events`+`jarvis_reflector_gap_stats`(previously`-${hours} hours`interpolation — Zod blocked it today but the pattern was a trap). C3 rate-limiter idle-window eviction —`windows`Map now sweeps one drained neighbor on each request instead of growing unboundedly. M1`audit.ts`catch escalates to`pino.error`with diagnostic context instead of silent swallow (broken audit pipe was previously undetectable). M2 generic error + correlation id pattern —`tools.ts`introduces`toolErr(name, e)`and`clientErr(name, reason)`helpers; full exception stays in server-side logs, client only sees`{error, correlation*id}`. M3 secret redaction via new `redact.ts` module (`redactSecrets`+`redactDeep`walking JSON) —`jarvis_task_detail`now pipes`input/output/metadata/error`through redaction before return; regex patterns cover Authorization Bearer/Basic,`sk-/pk-/ghp*/gho*/glpat-/jrvs*`API keys, JSON field names`password`/`secret`/`api_key`/`access_token`/`refresh_token`/`private_key`, and 40+ char hex blobs. M4 `feedback-grep`now uses`lstat`+`realpath`confinement (previously followed symlinks; a symlinked`feedback_evil.md`pointing at`/etc/shadow`would have been read). M5`BEARER_RE`min length 32 chars. M6 mount gate parseBool — accepts`1`/`true`/`yes`/`on`case-insensitive + startup log line. W1 Hono`cors({origin: () => null})`on the MCP router — v7.7 is localhost-only so browser cross-origin is explicitly refused. W2`expires_at TEXT`column added to`mcp_tokens`(additive, live-applied via`sqlite3 ALTER TABLE`), `mc-ctl mcp-token create --expires-in-days N`supports issuance with rotation window, auth SELECT excludes expired. W3 new tests:`audit.test.ts`(4) +`redact.test.ts`(12) +`rate-limit.test.ts`eviction test (1). W4`mc-ctl mcp-token create`output hygiene — token printed on its own line inside a delimiter banner, config snippet contains`<PASTE_TOKEN_HERE>`placeholder instead of the inline credential. m7`mc-ctl mcp-token revoke`integer validation —`"1 OR 1=1"` previously revoked every token silently. Total commit A: +8 source edits, +3 new files, **59 passing MCP tests** (was 36). **Commit B — Layer 4b + Rumi chain + claude-sdk** (`src/runners/fast-runner.ts`, `src/db/pgvector.ts`, `src/messaging/scope.ts`, `src/messaging/scope-classifier.ts`, `src/inference/claude-sdk.ts`): Layer4b-M1 `jarvis_propose_directive`added to`WRITE_TOOLS`so the retry-swap gate at fast-runner.ts:1218 accepts it — previously the Layer 4b retry fired correctly but`retryCalledWrite` stayed false and the good retry output was silently discarded into the mechanical honest-replacement branch. The v7.7.1 fix was flagging hallucinations correctly but the repair was neutered. Layer4b-M2 widened the cooldown-detection regex to catch Spanish comma decimals (`14,8h`), spelled-out units (`14 horas`, `14 hours`, `14 hrs`), minute unit (`45 minutes`), day unit (`2 días`), and synonyms (`faltan`, `restan`, `available in`, `allowed in`, `ready in`). Added a `hasNearbyNumericTime`fallback that requires a`propuesta/directiv/cooldown`keyword within ~120 chars of a numeric-time claim. Rumi-M1`scope-classifier.ts` `VALID_GROUPS`now includes`jarvis_write`+`CLASSIFIER_SYSTEM_PROMPT`teaches the group — previously the semantic classifier could never emit`jarvis_write`and only the regex fallback path caught KB-write intent. Rumi-M2`jarvis_write`regex in`scope.ts`extended with`KB`/`base de conocimiento` alternation covering both verb-first (`guarda esto en el KB`) and phrase-first (`en la KB guarda esto`) orderings plus up to 3 filler words between the verb and the target. Rumi-M3 **`pgBatchUpsert`now routes every entry through`validateKbEntry`before inclusion in the batch payload\** — the critical finding. Previously`pgvector-backfill.ts`would re-ingest historical tool-narrative lessons from the SQLite FTS5 source on every backfill run, fully bypassing the quality gate fixed in c15a06b. Return type now includes`rejected`count propagated through`backfillToPgvector`. Rumi-M4 NOISE*PATTERNS tightening + widening: `was executed with` now requires a tool-shaped suffix (`tool | herramienta | runner | skill | [a-z]+\*[a-z*]+`) so legitimate facts like "the contract was executed with the new terms" pass, while "executed with fast-runner" and "executed using jarvis_file_read" still reject. Added paraphrase verbs: `invoked/pulled/consumed/called`, `llamó a las herramientas`, `ejecutó herramientas`. claude-sdk.ts: coerced `success.result ?? ""`into a local`resolvedResult`so`resultText`never transiently holds`undefined`(the declared type is`string`). Total commit B: +5 source edits, +32 new tests across fast-runner, pgvector, scope, scope-classifier. **Full suite: 2168 → 2225 (+57). Zero type errors, zero lint failures.** Service restarted on PID 3974489. All v7.7.1 audit critical+major findings closed in a single session. **Lessons captured:** memory/feedback_audit_marathon.md — when a single session ships three audit rounds, batch findings by file not by severity to minimize file reads, and use the "fix all, no deferrals" option only when the audit scope is contained and the patterns are mechanical.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| 2026-04-14                  | `4195630`                                                                                                                                                                                                      | **Session 67 pt 7: fast-runner Layer 4b — directive cooldown hallucination guard**. User reported that around 10:00 AM CDMX Jarvis narrated SG4 (`DIRECTIVE*COOLDOWN_HOURS=48`) state with specific timing ("próxima disponible en ~15h", "33.2h ago", "14.8h restantes") across five consecutive tasks (452493ae → 453fb045 → 51395fe7 → f2976551) without ever calling `jarvis_propose_directive`to verify. When challenged he flipped from false-positive ("cooldown active, 15h remaining") to false-negative ("the cooldown doesn't exist, I hallucinated it") before finally calling the tool on the fourth try and landing on the truth. SG4 itself is working as designed (last proposal 33.4h ago, next slot ~00:51 CDMX 2026-04-15); the bug was purely in narration. **Root cause:** the LLM had SG4 in training/memory and was pattern-matching the system state from it, skipping the actual tool call — classic "right answer from memory, wrong process" hallucination. **Fix:** added Layer 4b to`detectsHallucinatedExecution()`in`src/runners/fast-runner.ts`, parallel to the existing Layer 4 git-claim check. If reply text narrates specific cooldown state (`cooldown active/activo`, `próxima propuesta en ~Xh`, `quedan Xh`near cooldown/propuesta/directiv,`Next allowed in Xh`, `Last proposal was Xh ago`, `propuesta en cola`) AND `jarvis_propose_directive`is not in`toolsCalled`, flag as hallucination → existing retry path forces the LLM to actually call the tool on retry. Abstract explanations without numeric state (e.g. "SG4 es un safeguard que limita propuestas") still pass; tool-response echoes when the tool WAS called still pass. Zero changes to SG4 itself, the 48h window, the cooldown store, or the `jarvis_propose_directive`tool — "everything else is there for a reason". 5 new tests in`fast-runner.test.ts`(Spanish timing / English tool-response echo / 'quedan Xh' phrasing / allowed-when-tool-called / abstract-explanation false-positive). 2163 → 2168 tests, zero type errors. Lesson captured in`feedback_sg4_cooldown_hallucination.md`. | 2026-04-14                  | `c2bedd6`+ audit follow-up                                                                                                                                                                                     | **Session 67 pt 6: v7.9 Prometheus Sonnet port — route heavy-runner LLM calls through Claude Agent SDK**. Audit found that since commit`70f8cc5`(2026-04-11, fast-runner Sonnet migration), Prometheus planner/reflector/executor still routed through`infer()`/`inferWithTools()` in the openai adapter → qwen3.5-plus. Heavy tasks — the ones where reasoning and tool-calling quality matter most — silently ran on the worse model while fast-runner chat enjoyed Sonnet. The fast-runner commit wired the SDK at exactly one call site (`fast-runner.ts`) and never updated the adapter to route through it. **Fix:** added two shape-compatible wrappers to `src/inference/claude-sdk.ts`: `queryClaudeSdkAsInfer(messages, options?)`returns an`InferenceResponse`-shaped result, `queryClaudeSdkAsInferWithTools(messages, tools, executor, options?)`returns the full`inferWithTools`shape including synthetic`messages[]`, `toolRepairs`, `exitReason`. Each of 6 Prometheus call sites (planner plan + replan, reflector reflect, executor selfAssess + main exec + self-assess retry) got a one-line `useSdkPath() ? queryClaudeSdkAsInfer(...) : infer(...)`branch. Prometheus test files mock`../config.js`to force openai path so existing 2143 tests stay green. 8 new wrapper tests in`claude-sdk.test.ts`cover both adapters and message flattening. **Live smoke test (task`6abdc41f`):** submitted a heavy synthesis task ("find 5 famous Rumi poems by Coleman Barks, synthesize"). Planner hit Sonnet cleanly ($0.048, 5.7s, 1 turn), executor ran 11 turns with 10 tool calls ($0.37, 53s), selfAssess + goal 2 + reflector added $0.16. Task completed success=true score=0.62 in 2:35. **Reflector caught a real bug in its own output:\*\* "all sources are tagged unverified despite active web\*read calls suggesting a provenance-tracking bug." That's Sonnet-quality reflection — qwen had not produced such pointed self-critique in prior runs. Total cost $0.58 vs estimated qwen ~$0.02. **qa-auditor run:** PASS WITH WARNINGS. Shipped follow-ups: **C1** `flattenMessagesForSdk`now handles array/null ChatContent via a new`normalizeContent`helper (vision blocks and normalized assistant arrays no longer silently erased). **C2**`exitReason`mapping widened to match`STATUS: BLOCKED`OR`STATUS: DONE_WITH_CONCERNS`OR`[timeout`prefix (was only catching BLOCKED). **M1 partial**`compressionContext`now prepended to the user prompt on the SDK path (openai adapter uses it mid-loop; we can't without SDK token counters but at least the signal reaches the model). **m5** synthetic`sdk_call*\*`IDs now include a base36 timestamp nonce to avoid cross-run collisions. 5 new tests cover C1/C2/M1/m5. **Deferred as follow-ups:\*\* M1 full (tokenBudget enforcement on SDK path — needs SDK counters), M2 (provenance extraction returns empty on SDK path because synthetic messages lack real tool_result pairings), M3 (retry path loses real tool arguments and results), W2 (no empirical JSON parse rate benchmark for Sonnet vs qwen), W3 (no integration coverage for SDK path in Prometheus layer — only wrapper unit tests), W4 (SDK path bypasses provider health/circuit breaker/degradation tracker), W7 (no cost dashboards for SDK path yet). Rollback is zero-LOC: set`INFERENCE_PRIMARY_PROVIDER=openai`in systemd drop-in and restart. 2151→2163 tests (+12). | 2026-04-14                  | `c15a06b`                                                                                                                                                                                                      | **Session 67 pt 5: Rumi-poem delivery chain — 4-bug cascade under one "hang" symptom**. After the session-wrap commit, user sent "Me regalas un poema de Rumi para dormir?" to Telegram and received silence. Task 2378 completed`status=completed`with`text=""`and`toolCalls=[jarvis_file_read, jarvis_file_update]`. Diagnosing this peeled back four distinct bugs. **Bug 1 (commit `d80e29c`) — claude-sdk text drop on tool-use-final turns.** `src/inference/claude-sdk.ts:289`set`resultText = success.result`. The Claude Agent SDK's `result.result`captures only the final assistant turn's text — when the final turn is tool-use-heavy, earlier-turn body text lived in local`streamingText`but was silently discarded because the line-354 fallback only fired when`resultText`was empty, not when merely shorter. Fix: prefer longer of streamingText vs success.result. Regression test reproduces the 3-turn flow (text → tool_use → tool_use → short closer). **Bug 2 (commit`c823092`) — jarvis_file writes always-on.** MISC_TOOLS included jarvis_file_write/update/delete/move with no scope gate. Any chat message could trigger KB writes. Memory enrichment recalled a self-written SOP (score 0.68) saying "Registro obligatorio en cada uso futuro" and Sonnet, being literal, faithfully called jarvis_file_read + jarvis_file_update to log the poem delivery. Fix: split into JARVIS_WRITE_TOOLS gated on jarvis_write pattern + northstar_write + northstar_journal + coding. Reads stay always-on (models can consult SOPs, just can't execute them). 8 new scope tests including the exact Rumi prompt as a regression assertion that jarvis_file_write is NOT in scope. **Bug 3 (commit `c15a06b`) — extractor self-reflection feedback loop.** background-extractor.ts was emitting "lessons" like "User has recurring workflow pattern using jarvis_file_read, jarvis_file_write" and upserting them to pgvector. Enrichment recalled these and Sonnet read them as user preferences — "Fede uses file tools for chat" — reinforcing bad tool selection. Self-tightening loop. CRYSTALLIZATION_PROMPT literally said "Focus on... tool usage patterns". Fix (three layers): (a) mechanical NOISE_PATTERNS in validateKbEntry() rejecting tool-narrative signatures at the storage boundary, per feedback_preformat_over_prompt wisdom that mechanical gates beat prompt fixes; (b) EXTRACTION_PROMPT rewritten to forbid tool-usage descriptions; (c) CRYSTALLIZATION_PROMPT rewritten so lessons describe the approach, not the tool inventory. 10 new tests covering exact failure strings plus positive cases (approach-level lessons and user facts mentioning tool names still pass). **Bug 4 (data fix, not code) — my own SOP rewrite was too defensive.** After dropping "registro obligatorio" I wrote a v2.0 with fallback "Si no recuerdas un poema exacto, dile No tengo uno". First live retest: Sonnet bounced the request, SOP interpreted too conservatively. Rewrote as v3.0 with explicit enumerated training knowledge ("Tú conoces The Guest House, Quietness, The Tent, A Great Wagon, Moses and the Shepherd, Like This..."). Delivered via SQL UPDATE + one-shot Node resync through `upsertFile()`. **Live verification:** task 2388 delivered "Where Everything Is Music" — Rumi, trad. Coleman Barks — clean, attributed, no fabrication, only `jarvis_file_read`in toolCalls. End-to-end chain validated. **Lessons captured:**`feedback_layered_bug_chains.md`(distrust "task completed" + silent-delivery symptom, trace both output pipeline AND context pipeline) +`feedback_extractor_self_reflection_loop.md`(LLM extractors summarizing agent's own tool usage need mechanical rejection at storage boundary, not just prompt rules). **Delta:** 3 code commits, 1 SOP v2→v3 data fix, 2131→2151 tests (+20: 2 claude-sdk + 8 scope + 10 pgvector). | 2026-04-14                  | TBD                                                                                                                                                                                                            | **Session 67 pt 4: v7.6.3 — re-audit follow-up, multi-trailing-dot bypass + plural URL keys**. Ran qa-auditor on commit`ee4566b`(v7.6.2). It caught a CRITICAL incomplete fix and three majors. **C1.5 (critical) —`\.$/`only stripped ONE trailing dot.** Verified live:`validateOutboundUrl("http://localhost../")`returned null because the v7.6.2 fix`.replace(/\.$/, "")`left`localhost.`after one strip, which still bypassed`BLOCKED_HOSTS.has("localhost")`. Same applied to `localhost...`, `metadata.google.internal..`, etc. Browsers and lightpanda's Zig parser collapse multi-dot FQDN suffixes — the blocklist must too. **Fix:** changed regex to `\.+$/`to strip ALL trailing dots in one pass. Verified live with the same Node`URL`parser. **M3 (major) — plural URL keys missing from whitelist.** v7.6.2 added`webhook*url`/`callback_url`/etc. but not `urls`/`endpoints`/`pages` plural forms. Combined with v7.6.2 W3 array walking, plural keys are the natural shape (`{urls: [...]}`). Without them, third-party MCP servers using batch-style schemas would silently bypass. **Fix:** added `urls`, `links`, `hrefs`, `targets`, `endpoints`, `pages`to`URL_PARAM_KEYS`. Whitelist now 49 entries (was 43). **M4 (major, already-fixed) — misleading test renamed.** A v7.6.2 test was titled `"blocks array of URL strings under 'urls' (wait — 'urls' not in whitelist)"`but asserted`toBeNull()`(the bypass). The auditor flagged this as a CI noise hazard — a future reader scanning test names sees "blocks" pass while the real semantic was "documents bypass". The test was implicitly fixed by the M3 plural-key addition (now`{urls: [...]}`is actually validated, and the test was rewritten as a positive`blocks array of URLs under 'urls' (plural — added v7.6.3)`assertion). **m2 (minor) —`URL_PARAM_KEYS`lowercase convention documented.** Added`// All entries MUST be lowercase`comment to the docstring. The lookup uses`.toLowerCase()`so an entry like`"Host"`would silently never match — convention is now explicit. **W6 follow-up (re-audit) — primitive args test added.** v7.6.2 covered`null`/`undefined`but not`42`/`"foo"` (numbers/strings at runtime when the TypeScript signature says object). Added one test. **Docstring count fix.** v7.6.2 docstring claimed "expanded from 15 → 30 keys" but actual count was 43. v7.6.3 now lists growth history accurately: 15 → 43 → 49 across v7.6.1 → v7.6.2 → v7.6.3. **NOT shipped (deferred per auditor):** structured audit log format (`[mcp] ssrf_block tool=X field=Y reason=Z`instead of free-form prose) — flagged as "not shipping-blocking" by the auditor; current format is parseable with a single regex; defer to v7.6.4 if`mc-ctl validation check`Pillar 6 item ever lands. **Tests:** 5 new in`url-safety.test.ts`(multi-dot regression × 3 + plural-key-array regression × 3 — wait, that's 6, plus rename of one existing test) + 3 new in`bridge.test.ts`(primitive args, multi-dot at bridge, plural`urls`at bridge). 8 new total. Full suite: 2123 → 2131 (+8). **Net v7.6.1 + v7.6.2 + v7.6.3 coverage:** 60 new security tests against the MCP URL attack surface across three commits in one calendar day. **The Pillar 6 Known Issue is NOW genuinely closed** — the second audit cleared it. Three deferred defense-in-depth items remain (iptables OUTPUT rule,`mc-ctl validation`Pillar 6 check, schema drift audit), all non-blocking. Phase α infrastructure track is complete except for the 2026-04-20 autoreason Phase 2 calendar gate. | 2026-04-14                  | `ee4566b`                                                                                                                                                                                                      | **Session 67 pt 3: v7.6.2 — QA audit follow-up on v7.6.1 SSRF defense**. Ran qa-auditor agent on commit`0c29f84`and caught three pre-existing SSRF bypasses in`validateOutboundUrl()`that v7.6.1 didn't fix — which would have made the "Pillar 6 closed" checkbox dishonest. All three plus four secondary audit findings shipped the same day as v7.6.2. **C1 (critical) — trailing-dot hostname bypass.**`http://localhost./` returned null because DNS strips the trailing FQDN dot but `BLOCKED_HOSTS.has()` did not. Fix: `.replace(/\.$/, "")`on hostname before the set lookup. Same bypass covered`metadata.google.internal.`and any other entry. **C2 (critical) — IPv6 unspecified address bypass.**`http://[::]/`routed to loopback on Linux but was not in`BLOCKED_IP_PATTERNS`. Fix: added `/^::0*$/`(covers`::`, `::0`, `::00`, etc.). **R4 (critical) — non-`//`URI schemes bypassed the scheme gate.** The v7.6.1 regex`^[a-z][a-z0-9+\-.]*:\/\/`required`://`, so `javascript:fetch("http://169.254.169.254")` under a `url` key in a playwright `browser_navigate` call was let through. Playwright's own `checkUrlAllowed` only blocks `file:`, so javascript:/data:/vbscript: would have been executed. Fix: replaced the regex gate with `URL.canParse()` — schemes without `//` now parse successfully, reach `validateOutboundUrl()`, and get rejected by the `ALLOWED_SCHEMES` check. All five test URI variants (`javascript:`, `data:`, `vbscript:`, `file:/etc/passwd` without `//`, `blob:`) now blocked. **W1 (warning) — URL_PARAM_KEYS expansion.** Extended whitelist from 15 → 46 entries: `destination`, `destination_url`, `webhook`, `webhook_url`, `callback`, `callback_url`, `redirect`, `redirect_uri`, `redirect_url`, `return_url`, `ping_url`, `api_url`, `base_url`, `endpoint_url`, `href_to`, `website`, plus case-insensitive variants. Zero false-positive risk because `URL.canParse()` filters downstream. **W3 (warning) — string arrays under URL keys now walked.** `{url: ["http://ok.com", "http://localhost/"]}` was previously unvalidated. Fix: when the parent key is in `URL_PARAM_KEYS` AND the value is an array, string elements now go through `validateUrlString()` too. **W5 (warning) — audit log contract asserted.** Added `console.warn` spy tests confirming `[mcp] blocked URL-bearing arg on <tool>: <path>: <reason>` fires on block and NOT on happy path. **W6 (warning) — null/undefined args handled.** `createMcpTool.execute()` now has explicit tests for `undefined` and `null` args. No throw, upstream call proceeds. **Refactor:** extracted `validateUrlString(value, path)` helper so both the direct string path and the array-element path share the canParse→validate logic. **Design principle post-audit:** generous URL-key whitelist + strict `URL.canParse()` gate + strict `validateOutboundUrl()` semantic check. Wide-key + strict-value beats narrow-key + loose-value because additive whitelist changes are zero-risk while regex-based value filters can be bypassed by encoding tricks. **Tests:** 21 new in `url-safety.test.ts` + 8 new in `bridge.test.ts`. Full suite: 2094 → 2123 (+29). `V7-READINESS-CRITERIA.md` Known Issue entry updated with honest audit history. Three deferred defense-in-depth follow-ups remain (iptables OUTPUT rule, mc-ctl validation Pillar 6 check, schema drift audit) but are non-blocking. **Pillar 6 Known Issue is now legitimately closed.**                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| 2026-04-14                  | `0c29f84`                                                                                                                                                                                                      | **Session 67 pt 2: v7.6.1 — MCP browser SSRF defense at bridge boundary**. Closed the last Pillar 6 Known Issue flagged in V7-READINESS-CRITERIA.md, unblocking v7.0 launch on the security gate. Single-point intercept at `createMcpTool.execute()` in `src/mcp/bridge.ts` — the one funnel every MCP tool from every MCP server passes through. New helper `validateArgsUrls()` in `src/lib/url-safety.ts` recursively scans args for URL-bearing string values under convention keys (`url`, `uri`, `href`, `link`, `target`, `target_url`, `location`, `src`, `source_url`, `endpoint`, `page`, `page_url`, `navigate_to`, `goto`, case-insensitive, max depth 3), and runs `validateOutboundUrl()` on any value matching `^[a-z][a-z0-9+\-.]*:\/\/`. On block: returns `{error: "Blocked outbound URL: <key.path>: <reason>"}`without forwarding to upstream MCP server. Walks arrays of objects but skips arrays of primitives. Short-circuits on first block. Scheme gating minimizes false positives — search queries under url-shaped keys let through, prose with URL substrings under non-URL keys let through. Emits`[mcp] blocked URL-bearing arg on <tool>: <path>: <reason>`to stderr for audit. Covers ALL current and future MCP servers (lightpanda, @playwright/mcp, anything added to mcp-servers.json) with one ~15 LOC delta. Strictly better than the v7.6 original plan to wrap @playwright/mcp as a ToolSource — single intercept catches lightpanda's browser\_\_goto + future tools + schema drift that add new URL params. Tests: 16 new in`src/lib/url-safety.test.ts`(allow paths for public URL / non-URL string / relative / empty args / primitives; block paths for file:// / localhost / 169.254.169.254 / 127.0.0.1 / 10.x under 'href' / nested config.target_url / URL inside array of objects; short-circuit behavior; maxDepth enforcement; handles non-object args). 7 new in`src/mcp/bridge.test.ts`(file:// blocked at bridge with`callFn`never called; http://localhost blocked; cloud metadata blocked; RFC1918 blocked; public URL passes through; non-URL-bearing args untouched; non-URL string under url key passthrough). 2071→2094 tests (+23). Verified in`dist/`: `grep -c validateArgsUrls dist/mcp/bridge.js`→ 3,`dist/lib/url-safety.js`→ 4. V7-READINESS-CRITERIA.md Pre-v7 checklist item 11 flipped to ✅. Known Issues section rewritten with the closure, design rationale, and 3 deferred follow-ups (iptables OUTPUT rule, validation script Pillar 6 check, non-URL-key attack surface audit — all defense-in-depth, non-blocking for v7.0). Service restarted, PID healthy, no behavior change on the allow path. Phase α continues: item 1 (v7.6 gws) done, item 2 (v7.7 Jarvis MCP) done, item 2.1 (v7.6.1 SSRF hardening) done, item 3 (autoreason Phase 2) scheduled for 2026-04-20. Second Tier C (Infrastructure) session from V7-ROADMAP. Exposes a read-only MCP surface at`POST /mcp`on the existing Hono server, gated on`JARVIS_MCP_ENABLED=true`(deployed via systemd drop-in at`/etc/systemd/system/mission-control.service.d/mcp.conf`). Lets Claude Code sessions query live Jarvis state — memory, tasks, schedules, feedback, gap telemetry — via typed MCP tool calls instead of SSH'ing in and running `./mc-ctl db "SELECT ..."`by hand. **Transport**: MCP SDK ships`WebStandardStreamableHTTPServerTransport`which takes a Web Standard`Request`and returns a`Response`— native Hono compatibility via`transport.handleRequest(c.req.raw)`. Stateless mode (no session persistence); Claude Code HTTP MCP client reconnects per call and that's fine. Discovery in the v7.7 planning pass dropped estimated effort from 4–6h → 3–4h — no custom transport glue needed. **Auth + rate limit**: bearer tokens generated via `openssl rand -hex 32`, SHA-256 hashed at rest in new `mcp_tokens`table (additive schema applied live), shown exactly once at`./mc-ctl mcp-token create --client <name>`time.`Authorization: Bearer <token>`header → SHA-256 → DB lookup → rejects missing/malformed/revoked (401).`last_used_at`touched on every call. In-memory sliding window rate limiter, 100 req/min per token id, emits`Retry-After`header on 429. **Audit**: every request writes rows to`events`table with`category='mcp_call'`and`type='request_received' No tool arguments, no tool output content — counts + shapes only. Raw tokens never touch audit logs. **8 read-only tools**: (1) `jarvis_status`— pid, uptime, memMB, task counts by status, memory backend health (live: 2366 tasks, 181 MB, hindsight backend, PID 2661873); (2)`jarvis_task_list`— filters status/since/limit (max 100); (3)`jarvis_task_detail`— full record + subtasks by task_id; (4)`jarvis_memory_query`— delegates to`MemoryService.recall()`with bank/tags/limit; (5)`jarvis_schedule_list`— active scheduled tasks + cron + delivery + last_run_at; (6)`jarvis_recent_events`— events in last N hours with category/type filters (max 168h, 500 rows); (7)`jarvis_reflector_gap_stats`— autoreason Phase 1 gap telemetry aggregation over a window, returns n/avgGap/maxGap/wideGapCount/wideGapPct/llmFallbackCount +`decisionHint`string ("close thread — no tournament" / "skip tournament" / "targeted tournament pilot" / "consider global tournament") that pre-computes the 2026-04-20 Phase 2 decision rules; (8)`jarvis_feedback_search`— substring grep over memory`feedback*\*.md`files with match count + snippet + case-insensitive. Each returns`{ok, data}`or`{ok, error}`envelope. **CLI**: new`./mc-ctl mcp-token <create       | revoke>`subcommand in mc-ctl. Token creation uses`openssl rand -hex 32`+ SHA-256, prints the bearer token exactly once with a ready-to-paste Claude Code MCP config JSON snippet. List shows id/client/scope/created/last_used/status. Revoke flips`revoked=1`per id. **Files**: new`src/api/mcp-server/`directory —`types.ts`, `auth.ts`, `rate-limit.ts`, `audit.ts`, `feedback-grep.ts`, `tools.ts`(8 tool handlers ~500 LOC),`index.ts`(router factory + transport wiring). Tokens never logged plaintext. Smoke-test token revoked post-verification. 2042→2071 tests (+29: auth 6 + rate-limit 4 + feedback-grep 7 + tools 12). Source files 263→270 (+7). Test files 147→151 (+4). **Total v7.7 surface ~1000 LOC net**. V7-ROADMAP Phase α item 2 ✅ DONE. First Tier C (Infrastructure) session shipped from the V7-ROADMAP master sequence. Unlocks ~25+ Google Workspace services (Chat, Tasks, People, Forms, Meet, Classroom, Admin Reports, Apps Script, Keep, Workspace Events, Model Armor) via one subprocess dispatch tool that reads Google Discovery at runtime — zero per-service handler code. **Install**: prebuilt`gws v0.22.5`linux x86\*64 binary via GitHub release, SHA256 verified, dropped into`/usr/local/bin/gws`. **New tool** `src/tools/builtin/google-workspace-cli.ts`(~250 LOC): zod schema`{service, resource, method, params?, json?, page_all?, timeout_ms?}`, execFile with 2MiB stdout cap + 30s default timeout, argv builder that dot-splits nested resources (`spaces.messages`→`["spaces", "messages"]`), NDJSON pagination parser, token redaction on stderr. **Auth model**: per-call injection of the cached access token from the existing `getAccessToken()`in`src/google/auth.ts`— no parallel credential store, never touches`gws auth login`. Token refresh happens BEFORE exec so auth failures surface fast. `isGoogleConfigured()`guard at call time + env gate in the tool-source registration. **Registration**:`BuiltinToolSource.registerTools()`gates`GWS_TOOLS`behind`GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID + GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET + GOOGLE_REFRESH_TOKEN`. Added to `GOOGLE_TOOLS`array in`src/messaging/scope.ts`so it gets scope-gated alongside the hardened builtins. Google scope regex broadened to catch "chat", "tasks", "forms", "meet", "classroom", "keep", "people", "apps script", "workspace events", "admin reports", Spanish variants. Added to fast-runner`WRITE_TOOLS`set (classified as write-capable because`method` can be create/update/patch/delete). **Tool description** (~600 tokens): teaches the Discovery command shape with 4 canonical examples (Chat spaces.messages.create, Tasks tasks.insert, People connections.list, Forms responses.list), documents the introspection pattern (`method: "--help"`), and explicitly routes the LLM to dedicated handlers (gmail_send, gdrive\*\*, etc.) when they exist. Deferred = true so the full schema only loads on first call. **Tests**: new `src/tools/builtin/google-workspace-cli.test.ts` with 12 mocked cases via `vi.hoisted()` — success/error/pagination/token-injection/timeout/unconfigured/refresh-failure/parse-failure/token-redaction/empty-required-field/empty-resource-help/argv-correctness. Per `feedback_vitest_mocking.md` the hoisted pattern prevents the "cannot access mockExecFile before initialization" trap that hit on the first run. **Also shipped (hardening)**: closed the `screenshot_element` URL validation gap flagged in the day-5 validation investigation (V7-READINESS-CRITERIA.md Known Issues). Added `validateOutboundUrl()` call before `page.goto()` so SSRF to `http://127.0.0.1:*`, RFC1918 ranges, and `file://`schemes is blocked at Jarvis's layer, not just Playwright's protocol allowlist. 2 new screenshot tests cover the file:// and localhost rejection paths. **NOT shipped** (deferred to v7.6.1 follow-up): full`@playwright/mcp`ToolSource wrapper. Requires a bigger architectural pass on how MCP-sourced tools get URL-validated — documented in V7-READINESS-CRITERIA.md as a remaining Pillar 6 item. **Smoke test**: invoked the registered tool via the compiled`BuiltinToolSource` against the live Google Tasks API (`tasks.tasklists.list`), returned `ok=true`with the real "My Tasks" tasklist — auth injection + argv building + JSON parsing all verified end-to-end. 2028→2042 tests (+14: 12 dispatch + 2 screenshot SSRF). Source files 262→263. Test files 146→147. Tools 179→180 (181 when gws is configured). 1 commit. Real messaging classifier uses `[router]`/`[classifier]`prefix; tightened validation regex in`mc-ctl cmd_validation`to require the explicit prefix AND filter`\$scope=`structured field. 10/10 pass, 0 warn, 0 fail — first fully clean validation day. Real signal surfaced during investigation: the 4 false-positive errors were Playwright correctly rejecting an LLM that tried to navigate to`file:///root/claude/mission-control/.env`and`file:///root/claude/mission-control/src/tools/builtin/`during a scheduled task on 2026-04-13 15:01 UTC. Layer 2 (Playwright UnsupportedProtocol) held. Investigation revealed the **MCP browser tool URL validation gap**:`validateOutboundUrl()`in`src/lib/url-safety.ts` only runs on builtin tools that explicitly call it (web*read, seo_page_audit, http). Playwright MCP tools (`browser\_\_markdown`, etc.) from `@playwright/mcp`bypass Layer 1 entirely.`file://`blocked by Playwright allowlist; SSRF to`http://localhost:*`/ RFC1918 ranges NOT blocked. Documented in`docs/V7-READINESS-CRITERIA.md`under new "Known Issues" section as Pillar 6 concern that must close before v7.0 launch. Fix recommendations: (1) MCP source wrapper calling`validateOutboundUrl()`before forwarding, (2) iptables OUTPUT block from Chromium process, (3) both belt+suspenders. | 2026-04-13                  | `ac42efc`                                                                                                                                                                                                      | **Session 65: Telegram vision fix — claude-sdk image bridge + CDN mime sniff**. Two-layer bug. Jarvis was answering blind to any Telegram photo despite clean "Image downloaded: NNKB base64" / "Injecting vision content" / "routing to primary provider" log chain. (1) **Structural gap in the Claude Agent SDK bridge** (`src/inference/claude-sdk.ts`): `queryClaudeSdk({prompt: string, systemPrompt: string, ...})` accepted only a string prompt, so the fast-runner's claude-sdk branch (`fast-runner.ts:795-867`) stripped `image_url`blocks out of the multimodal user content array and flattened to text-only before dispatch. Since`INFERENCE_PRIMARY_PROVIDER=claude-sdk`is live (Sonnet 4.6 is the vision-capable primary), \_every\* Telegram and WhatsApp photo was silently lost before reaching the model. The SDK itself supports images —`query({prompt: string | AsyncIterable<SDKUserMessage>})`per`sdk.d.ts:1891-1892`, and `SDKUserMessage.message`is an Anthropic`MessageParam`which accepts image content blocks — the bridge just never used that capability. **Fix**: added`ClaudeSdkImage`type + optional`images?: ClaudeSdkImage[]`opt,`buildVisionPromptStream()`async generator that yields one`SDKUserMessage`with`[{type:"text"}, {type:"image", source:{type:"base64",...}}, ...]`content. Non-vision calls keep the cheap string path (preserves prompt caching). Fast-runner now parses`data:([^;]+);base64,(.+)`out of each multimodal block, whitelists`image/{jpeg,png,gif,webp}`, and forwards via `images` when present. (2) **Telegram CDN lies about Content-Type** (`src/messaging/channels/telegram.ts`): `downloadImageAsBase64()`trusted the HTTP header, but`api.telegram.org/file/bot<TOKEN>/<path>`returns`Content-Type: application/octet-stream`for every photo regardless of actual format. Produced data URLs like`data:application/octet-stream;base64,/9j/4AA...`— the`/9j/`prefix is base64-encoded JPEG magic`FF D8 FF`, so the bytes were a real JPEG the whole time, but the mediaType whitelist in the fast-runner extractor rejected `application/octet-stream`and dropped the image after the structural fix. Diagnostic chain: first fix looked broken because`images=0`suffix stayed absent from the`[fast-runner] Claude SDK path: ...`log, giveaway was dumping the data URL prefix and seeing`application/octet-stream;base64,/9j`. **Fix**: `sniffImageMime()` inspects the first 12 bytes (`FF D8 FF`JPEG /`89 50 4E 47`PNG /`47 49 46 38`GIF /`RIFF ???? WEBP`), falls back to the HTTP header only if it starts with `image/`, last-resort defaults to `image/jpeg`since Telegram photos are always JPEG on the wire. Verified live: sent photo with caption "describe", Jarvis answered with actual visual content. WhatsApp photo flow shares the same fast-runner extractor and benefits automatically (its own`whatsapp.ts`already sets mime from baileys`imageMessage.mimetype`, so no CDN lying there). 2 new `claude-sdk.test.ts`cases: vision path yields streaming user message with text+image blocks, no-image path keeps plain string prompt. 2026→2028 tests (+2).`/diagnose`protocol used throughout — enumerated 8 failure points before editing, live log verification at each phase, 3-strike rule nearly tripped (structural fix looked "broken" after one retry, could have been mistaken for architectural dead end if mime prefix hadn't been logged). Learning captured as`feedback_telegram_mime_sniff`memory: never trust HTTP Content-Type for image delivery, always sniff magic bytes. 4 files modified, 1 commit.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| 2026-04-13                  |`9fa36db`                                                                                                                                                                                                     | **Session 64: autoreason Phase 1 +`error_max_turns` fix + V7 roadmap reorganization**. Session split across three threads: (1) **Paper mining — NousResearch/autoreason**. Read the full paper, distilled three adoptable lifts. **CoT rubric in LLM judges** (`src/prometheus/reflector.ts:20-36`, `executor.ts:66-79`): structured step-by-step reasoning before the JSON verdict in both the reflector and the per-goal self-assessor — matches Appendix A.5 judge prompt pattern. Table 3 from the paper: 3× faster convergence with zero architecture change. **`parseLLMJson` brace-scanning fallback** (`src/prometheus/types.ts:222-280`): tolerates CoT-prefixed JSON by walking the content for the last balanced `{...}` object with string-literal and escape awareness. Direct JSON path unchanged; fallback only engages on leading prose. **k=2 stability rule for Prometheus replan** (`src/prometheus/orchestrator.ts:414-555`): `checkReplan`now returns`ReplanVote{reason, severity}`. Soft votes (tool failure rate, tool-calls-per-goal) require 2 consecutive before firing a replan; hard votes (blocked-no-ready) fire immediately (k=1). Transfers autoreason Table 23's "incumbent survives unless beaten for 2 consecutive passes" to our replan decision point. `consecutiveSoftReplanVotes`counter,`replan_deferred` trace events. **Generation-evaluation gap telemetry** (`src/db/reflector-gap.ts`+`reflector_gap_log`table, additive IF NOT EXISTS applied live): logs`{taskId, llm_score, heuristic_score, abs_diff, llm_available, goals**}` per reflection. Write-only. Autoreason §7.10 central claim: tournament value = generation-evaluation gap. We can't decide Phase 2 without our own data — ships as observability, reads on 2026-04-20 via scheduled Telegram nudge (`eb3e4b14`). **Explicitly rejected full tournament** on paper's own scaling curve: Table 21 Sonnet 4.6 +4pp p=0.21 not significant; Haiku 4.5 0pp. 11 new tests (`types.test.ts`+5 for JSON extractor,`reflector.test.ts`+4 for CoT+telemetry,`executor.test.ts`+1 for CoT,`orchestrator.test.ts` +3 for k=2/hard-stop, existing tests updated for k=2 semantics). 2015→2026 (+11 net). (2) **`error_max_turns` streaming-text preservation** (`src/inference/claude-sdk.ts:236-276`): SDK `error_max_turns`/`error_max_budget_usd`/other terminal errors were overwriting `resultText`with a raw error string and discarding accumulated`streamingText`. Task `9a774ab5`surfaced this — 20-turn evolution log burned all turns on`jarvis_file_read`then delivered "Error: error\*max_turns — Reached maximum number of turns (20)" as if it were the answer (marked completed, no STATUS line → defaulted DONE). Fix preserves streamingText and emits`STATUS: DONE_WITH_CONCERNS`(or`BLOCKED`if zero stream), which the fast-runner safety net promotes through. New`src/inference/claude-sdk.test.ts`with 4 cases. Deployed live mid-session (PID 680538). Rest of session ran with live service healthy. (3) **V7 roadmap reorganization + naming collision fix**.`docs/V7-FINANCIAL-STACK.md`→`docs/V7-ROADMAP.md`. Rewritten header section in v6-roadmap format: Status Key + Execution Tiers table + Execution Phases + per-phase `## vX.Y — <Name> — **Status**`sections with Item/Source/Status tables for every F-series task, feature vertical, and infrastructure item. Consolidates Tier A Financial Stack (v7.0 F1–F10) + Tier B feature verticals (v7.1–v7.5) + Tier C infrastructure (v7.6 Workspace / v7.7 Jarvis MCP / v7.8 Autoreason). Full F-series technical reference preserved below the roadmap as`## Appendix: F-Series Technical Reference`. Dependency graph, Execution Invariants, Deferred/Skipped table, Metrics targets, Total Effort summary — all match V6-ROADMAP.md shape. Nothing dropped. File grew 905→1411 lines. **Collision fix**: the v7.4 and v7.5 planning memories drafted earlier in the session collided with canonical v7.4 video production / v7.5 skill evolution. Renamed `project_v74_workspace_expansion.md`→`project_v76_workspace_expansion.md`and`project_v75_jarvis_mcp_server.md`→`project_v77_jarvis_mcp_server.md`with full cross-ref updates. Process failure captured in the session learnings: check canonical roadmap before assigning version numbers. (4) **Also shipped** during the session: scheduled Telegram reminder`eb3e4b14`fires 2026-04-20 09:00 CDMX for autoreason Phase 2 decision (one-shot, self-deactivates via`delete_schedule`). Five planning/reference memory files saved: `project_autoreason_phase2_decision.md`, `project_v76_workspace_expansion.md`, `project_v77_jarvis_mcp_server.md`, `feedback_phantom_evolution_engine.md`(pattern study from reading Phantom`src/evolution/{engine,validation,constitution}.ts`+ triple-judge minority veto reference),`user_name_correction.md`(operator correction — not named "Daniel"). Repos evaluated and skipped as not worth integrating: NousResearch/autoreason (mined, above), Dochkina drop-the-hierarchy paper (validates existing Prometheus sequential-hybrid, no action), giancarloerra/socraticode (repo scale below pain threshold, AGPL risk on in-process integration), ghostwright/phantom (wrong shape for our workload, Docker socket mount is security blocker — but evolution engine code studied as reference). googleworkspace/cli (gws) drafted as v7.6 plan; safishamsi/graphify (re-checked, already planned as v7.2, confirmed production-readiness bugs fixed between v0.4.1 and v0.4.9). 2011→2026 tests (+15). Source files 261→262. Test files 145→146. Tools unchanged at 179. Disk grew 17 GB since session 62 (63 GB → 80 GB) without feature work. Audit found: (1) Docker build cache 13 GB (10 GB reclaimable) —`docker builder prune -f`recovered it. (2)`mission-control:latest`image 1.89 GB — built 2 days prior by`docker compose build`, never deployed (MC runs from systemd via `dist/index.js`). Removed. (3) MC backup directory had 8 daily backups instead of 3 — `backup-db.sh`had been **reverted** from`-mtime +3`to`-mtime +7`between sessions. Re-fixed and pruned. (4) Stale`mc.db.pre-unified-fs-1775321319` migration backup (86 MB) deleted. (5) **Orphan task container** (`mc-task-1775547319530`, nanoclaw-agent) running 5 days — inside it, a Claude CLI session hit "Not logged in · Please run /login" and the container never reaped because nothing exits on that error. Killed. (6) **Ollama daemon** orphan running 12 days, 9 MB RAM, 262 MB disk (`nomic-embed-text`model), **zero references in MC or CRM source** — leak from old manual install. Stopped, disabled, binary deleted,`~~/.ollama`removed, systemd unit removed. Total disk recovered: ~11 GB (80 GB → 69 GB). Operators can now drop them from`.env`. 2011 tests still pass, zero metric changes (operational session). 2 commits in MC repo. | 2026-04-12                  |`7b5b318`                                                                                                                                                                                                     | **Session 62 (cont): graph-aware recall reranker + reaction retry chain fix**. (1) **Coherence reranker for SQLite hybrid recall** — after FTS5+embedding fusion, reorders results to prefer entity-linked clusters via greedy subgraph selection. Connectivity acts as a strict tiebreaker capped at 15% so base relevance dominates outside the cluster bonus window. Pattern adapted (not copied) from soflutionltd/memorypilot source-available Rust implementation — clean-room reimplementation in TypeScript over Jarvis's conversations table with query-time entity extraction via v6.5 extractor. New`src/memory/graph-rerank.ts`(~180L), single integration point in`sqlite-backend.ts`, 13 new tests covering edges/clustering/invariants/options. Emits `coherence=`metric in hybrid-recall log line. Zero schema changes. (2) **Reaction retry chain fix** — diagnosed 87 consecutive autonomous-improvement task failures on 2026-04-11 (all`Container timed out after 300000ms`). Two compounding bugs: (a) `countReactionsForTask(source_task_id)`reset to 0 on every retry because each retry spawned a fresh task UUID, so`adjustedRetryRule`'s `previousAttempts > 0`guard never tripped. New`countReactionChainLength()`walks backward via`spawned_task_id → source_task_id`edges with 20-iteration cycle guard. (b)`TRANSIENT_ERROR_PATTERN`regex matched`timeout`but not`timed out`(space) — container errors fell through to non-transient path. Broadened to`/timed?\s?out | timeout | .../`. Together they bound retry storm blast radius to 3 tasks per cycle instead of 87. +8 reaction tests. 1990→2011 tests. 2 commits. (6) Structured gotchas KB: conditional file with operational gotchas (EurekaMD/CMLL confusion, user identity, tool behavior, timezone). | 2026-03-30                  | —                                                                                                                                                                                                              | fix: Gemini image hallucination — scope miss on short follow-ups + thread poisoning. (1) Short follow-up scope inheritance: messages <30 chars with no scope signals inherit scope from recent user messages, preventing tool dropout on "links?", "procede", "hazlo". (2) Poison filter for false capability claims: 3 new patterns catch "API no soporta generación de imágenes" / "requires Vertex AI" hallucinations. Root cause: user sent "links?" → no specialty scope matched → gemini_image unavailable → model web-searched Gemini docs → concluded API doesn't support images → poisoned thread. | 2026-03-30                  | —                                                                                                                                                                                                              | feat: v4.0.5 Observability Stack — Prometheus + Grafana monitoring via Docker Compose (docker-compose.monitoring.yml). Prometheus (prom/prometheus:latest) scrapes :8080/metrics every 30s, 30-day retention capped at 1GB, localhost-only binding. Grafana-OSS on :3001 (public, auth-protected) with auto-provisioned Prometheus datasource and 15-panel pre-built dashboard: 4 overview stats (spend gauge, budget remaining, success rate, conversations), 4 inference panels (provider latency avg/p95, success rate, token usage by model, cost by model), 3 tool/task panels (calls+errors, duration by runner, status breakdown), 4 system panels (heap, event loop lag, handles, GC). mc-ctl monitoring up/down/status commands added. Hostinger external firewall rule added for :3001 via API + sync. ~230MB RAM (Prometheus 30MB + Grafana 200MB). | 2026-03-30                  | —                                                                                                                                                                                                              | feat: v4.0.3 Hermes v0.5 Reliability Patterns — 3 patterns from NousResearch/hermes-agent v0.5.0 (17K stars). (1) Turn-scoped signal cleanup: stripStaleSignals() removes stale ALTO nudges and SYSTEM failure advisories from conversation history before each LLM round — prevents tool avoidance from leaked turn-scoped signals. (2) Think-block exhaustion: stripThinkBlocks() strips`<think>`/`<thinking>`/`<reasoning>`XML blocks from responses, detects all-thinking degenerate output (empty after stripping), forces wrap-up instead of useless retry. Returns clean content to callers. (3) SQLite WAL jitter retry: writeWithRetry() in db/index.ts catches SQLITE_BUSY with random jitter (20-150ms, 10 retries). busy_timeout reduced 5s→1s to surface contention faster. Periodic PASSIVE WAL checkpoint every 100 writes. Wrapped 3 high-contention callers: event bus persist(), outcome-tracker recordOutcome(), memory retain(). QA audit fix: selfAssess() token usage tracked (was invisible), JSON shape guard with safe defaults (was crash on malformed), insertBullet() wrapped in transaction (was TOCTOU race). 172 src, 69 test files, 756 tests.
